fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 BEIJING -- He never wore a swim suit, but nobody made a bigger splash. He never ran a lap, yet nobody travelled farther. The Beijing Olympics may initially be known for Michael Phelps' strength and Usain Bolt's speed, but, among American sports fans, no memory will prove as indelible as Kobe Bryant 's redemption. It's here. It's done. It will be officially completed on Sunday when the U.S. reclaims a gold medal against overmatched Spain, but, in terms of the one thing Bryant had lost, it's already happened. The Olympics will only give Phelps and Bolt fame. The Olympics have given Bryant the more enduring gift of respect. Seven games down, one remaining, and longtime Bryant watchers are hearing the most unusual things. Players speaking glowingly of him. Players speaking kindly of him. Players who once universally distrusted him, well, they like him. They really like him. The sort of all-star team that once froze him out now follows him, gaining energy from his defence, making it their mantra. Every game, Bryant has been the first player in a defensive stance, the first guy guarding the opponent in the backcourt, squatting and straining alone in front of four guys who have no choice but to imitate. If this team could have only one passport, Bryant playing defence would be the photo. "He gives it his all on every second of every play, you see that and you're like, you've got to do the same thing," said centre Chris Bosh. "You see a guy playing that hard, you'll do anything not to let him down." The sort of smart players that once shunned him now actually learn from him, drawing inspiration from his preparation. Every game, he's the player pointing to other players, directing them on both sides of the court, counseling them, cheering them. "You hear a lot of things about Kobe, but I had no idea he was such a basketball junkie," said guard Chris Paul. "He studies all the film, talks basketball all the time, knows everything." Finally, the sort of nice-guy players, who once ignored him, now treat him as an equal. "We're good friends, so none of what he does surprises me," said centre Carlos Boozer. Notice something interesting about that sentence? In my memory, it's the first time that anyone has ever been quoted saying that he and Bryant were "good friends." The loner has become an embraced leader, and you could see it again Friday in a 101-81 rout of defending Olympic champion Argentina at Wukesong Arena. Before the game, the handful of players who had competed on the embarrassing 2004 Olympics team in Athens pleaded with them for revenge. Bryant listened, and came out crazy. He scored the first points on a reverse follow-up layup. He made the first defensive stop while swarming Manu Ginobili into a three-point miss. He threw the first big elbow of the game, shoving Ginobili right in front of a whistle-chewing official, setting a tone for an hour-long scrum. And, then, he ran the first player off the court, chasing Ginobili to the bench with an apparent sprained ankle less than seven minutes into the game. Neither Ginobili, nor the Argentine chances, returned. "Kobe was the guy, he was like, 'I want to guard Manu,' " related Paul. "He always wants to guard the other team's best player." It's one thing for him to say that in a Laker locker room, on a team where he has to guard the opposing star. It's another thing to say it in a room filled with stars, where he knows that concentrating on defence will hurt his scoring, and that volunteering for anything can only bring embarrassment. "The things he does out there, they're not about putting the ball in the basket," said Carmelo Anthony. "They're about his presence." Notice something interesting about that sentence? How many times does a teammate compliment Bryant on something that doesn't involve numbers? Even when his Laker teammates talk about how Bryant won't let them lose, they are talking about his scoring. As perhaps the ultimate compliment, Bryant's teammates here are raving about him in spite of his numbers. He is only the third-leading scorer on the team at 14.3 points a game. He ranks fourth in steals. He ranks sixth in assists. He is eighth in rebounding. It's not about the numbers. It's about the perception. His teammates can't believe this is the Kobe Bryant who has, at various times in this career, cast himself as heartless and selfish. His teammates see him only as the leader of a defence that has scored 84 more points off turnovers than their opponents, and 91 more points off fast breaks. Notice something interesting about this column? As recently as a year ago, you couldn't write a story about Kobe Bryant 's impact on a team without talking to Kobe Bryant . His teammates never had much to say, and when they did, they seemed to say it with fear. This time, though, there are no Bryant quotes. There is not enough room. His teammates said plenty. A brand-new gold meda

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 BEIJING -- They mounted the medals platform as one. Their arms linked, their steps synchronized, America's Olympic basketball players picked up their prizes with a symbolic show of solidarity. It was a moment, in its own way, just as good as gold; just as good as the 118-107 victory over Spain that placed Team USA on the raised center of the medals stand. It showed that these guys get it; that privilege and pampering does not disqualify an athlete from patriotism; that the most accomplished and independent individuals can comprehend a cause larger than their own ego. "What you saw," Kobe Bryant said, "was a team." Imagine that. Imagine, after decades of slapdash barnstorming, the coddled cream of American basketball would embrace system and structure, discipline and demands, and knock the knucklehead image on its ear. Imagine a man who could make commitment cool. His name is Jerry Colangelo, and he did not show up on a single stat sheet while the Redeem Team reclaimed what most Americans regard as their birthright. But he was its architect, its visionary, its soul. Colangelo absorbed America's stunning shortfall in the Summer Games of Athens and set out to remedy the problem at its root. The managing director of USA basketball raised the requirements for prospective players, and simultaneously turned what many had viewed as a burdensome chore into an exclusive challenge. It was much the same business model that King Arthur conceived for Camelot. Turn a task into a quest and soon you'll have knights knocking at your door. Colangelo is already hearing from players and their agents about the 2012 team, and he expects several members of this year's squad to return for an encore performance. "Whatever skepticism there was, it was based on, `You're not going to be able to get the players to commit -- they just won't do that,' " Colangelo said in a corridor of Wukesong Indoor Stadium. "(But) players are human beings. If you have the ability to relate to them and talk with them about what's really important in life, which I tried to do, there was some mutual respect. "They bought into a vision, to a passion that I shared with them. And once they bought in, it started to snowball." Cynicism would say this was all about marketing; about famous athletes expanding their global brand on the sports world's largest stage. Yet to see their joy and relief made manifest after their toughest and final test of the tournament was like looking at a bunch of high school kids clinching a championship. It was genuine. It was real. If it was not a big enough blowout to satisfy America's sense of basketball entitlement, it was an eloquent argument for Colangelo's agenda. "I've seen a big improvement on their part," said Spain's star, Pau Gasol. "They took this tournament very seriously." They had no choice. Team USA can no longer expect to beat the world's best basketball teams on reputation and instinct alone, and it barely beat Spain with a level of cohesion that was conspicuously absent in Athens. It was a four-point game with less than eight minutes remaining when Spain's resourceful Rudy Fernandez (NBA property of the Portland Trail Blazers) missed a three-point shot that could have made it a one-point game. "Spain was unbelievable," LeBron James said later. "Every possession counted. You couldn't take one possession off, one second off. If it wasn't for our determination and willpower, we wouldn't have gotten through the game." Without Colangelo's blueprint, we'd likely be looking at a lot of red flags. "We couldn't put 'em away," Colangelo said. "They just kept coming back and coming back and making big plays and shots. "I do believe what we instituted three years ago and the continuity we've had with coaches and players helped us not crack. Because without that continuity, we may have." U.S. coach Mike Krzyzewski called it "one of the greatest international games in history," and was so moved by the experience that he kissed Colangelo on the cheek when they embraced after the final buzzer. The affection was unexpected, but not undeserved. "This is a testament to the system Mr. Colangelo put in place," Kobe Bryant said. "Everyone wants to talk about NBA players being selfish and arrogant and being individuals. What you saw today was a team binding together, facing adversity and coming out with a big win." What you saw was a gold medal bearing Jerry Colangelo's fingerprints. Graphic fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 3 PICS; CAPTIONS: 1. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 MADRID, 22 (EUROPA PRESS) The Los Angeles Lakers remain undefeated at home this season after the victory was scored on their visit to the Milwaukee Bucks (106-113), a result that is good for them to regain west conference leadership, while the New Orleans Pelicans have fallen to the Utah Jazz (129-118). Eight wins in so many other outings is the balance this season of the Lakers, who showed their credentials to another of the NBA's most powerful teams in a duel capitalized by the 'MVP' clash between LeBron James and Giannis Antetokounmpo. The 'laker', who last season did not hide his displeasure when the regular-season MVP went to the Greek, claimed 34 points and a big 6/10 in triples. Even more inspired from the long distance was his partner Kentavious Caldwell-Pope, who added 7 hits. For his part, Antetokounmpo added an insufficient 'double-double' (25 and 12 rebounds) supported by Jrue Holiday (22) and Khris Middleton (20), while Marc Gasol collaborated with 3 points, 3 rebounds, two assists and 1 stopper in 18 minutes to the Californian triumph, confirmed with a three-pointer over James' horn with one minute left that put 103-111 on the scoreboard. In addition, Willy Hernang\'f3mez again had a testimonial presence with the Pelicans, adding 2 points and 1 rebound in 2 minutes of play. Despite Zion Williamson (27), his team was unable to stand up to the end against Jazz directed Donovan Mitchell (36). --STATISTICS OF SPANISH PLAYERS. MARC GASOL: 3 points, 3 rebounds, 2 assists and 1 stopper. WILLY HERNANGOMEZ: 2 points and 1 rebound. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 BEVERLY HILLS, March 17, (THEWILL) LeBron James , the Los Angeles Lakers' superstar basketballer has reacted to the shooting deaths of eight people, including six Asian women, at Atlanta-area massage parlours on Tuesday. He called the act "senseless and tragic. " The self-styled "King" James made his reaction via a message of condolence he posted on the social media platform Twitter after the Lakers' game against the Minnesota Timberwolves on Tuesday night. In the message LeBron said: My condolences goes out to the families of all the victims and the entire Asian community tonight on what transpired in Atlanta at the Aromatherapy Spa. Coward a young man!! Just senseless and tragic!!" According to the reporting on the fatal shooting incident, a certain Robert Aaron Long, the 21-year-old primary suspect in the shootings, was arrested Tuesday night and charged with the execution-style killings across three spa locations. Agents from the Federal Bureau of Investigations (FBI) have arrived in Atlanta to assist in the wide scale investigations. As of Wednesday morning, local officials were still withholding the names of those killed as part of the investigation looking into the motive behind the shootings. Brian Kemp, the Governor of the state of Georgia,commiserated with victims via his official Twitter account: "Our entire family is praying for the victims of these horrific acts of violence. " There has been a growing increase in reported cases of racism across the United States. Stop AAPI Hate, an organization that tracks reported hateful instances of violence, abuse or discrimination against Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders in the United States, found there were almost 3,800 reports of anti-Asian racism in 2020. It is a near 1,000 increase from 2019. If retroactive reports are included, the number jumps to just over 3,200. Starkly, 68 percent of the reports came from women. The first part of Space Jam, a film that mixed animation and real action, grossed about $250 million worldwide. Its sequel is a project that was in development for years and was finally able to be realized. How the first Space Jam was made It's worth remembering details from that movie. Michael Jordan's relationship with Bugs Bunny already had a few chapters before the feature film was shot. In 1992 the basketball player, who was already a marketing success and the face of various products, starred in the Nike sneakers commercial "Hare Jordan". The idea also served Warner Bros. to bring back to popularity the Looney Tunes who sailed in oblivion to children's audiences. Success led to more advertisements with the duo, which always featured the direction of Joe Pytka. By the time it was agreed to make the film, the staff was almost ready. There was a little detail missing, the story. For this reason, the renowned director Ivan Reitman (The Ghostbusters, 1984) was chosen as producer. He brought the idea of the Monstars - the villains of the film who steal the talent and physical strength of five NBA cracks - and another of the cast's stars: Bill Murray?, just one of the protagonists of Ghostbusters. The cast was completed with another prominent comedian in those days, Wayne Knight, who played Michael's publicist Stan Podolak. And there was no missing other NBA stars: Charles Barkley, Patrick Ewing, Muggsy Bogues, Larry Johnson and Shawn Bradley, who in history lost their talent to the theft of the evil Monstars. One detail among the characters in the film was Mr. Swackhammer, the particular leader of Space Jam's villains whose English voice was voiced by Danny DeVito. The evil boss wanted to enslave Jordan was compared by many to Jerry Krause, the chicago Bulls manager, who had a lousy relationship with Jordan. In November 1996 the film that united Michael Jordan and the Looney Tunes finally made it to the billboards. Although she had no good responses from critics, the audience accompanied her massively at the box office with a gross of $230.4 million, making it the most popular basketball film in history and the 12th most watched sports film. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 A new trailer for the highly-anticipated sports comedy film Space Jam: A New Legacy was shared on HBO Max's official YouTube channel on Wednesday morning. The feature is centered on LeBron James , who forms a basketball team with several of the Looney Tunes characters in order to save his son from a malevolent computer algorithm. The flick serves as a sequel to the original 1996 movie and has been in development for several decades. The trailer begins with James hurtling through an alternate universe before he lands on a planet inhabited by the iconic cartoon characters and becomes animated as well. The 36-year-old basketball star initially reacts with incredulity after discovering that he has turned into a two-dimensional version of himself and remarks that he is 'shorter than Kevin Hart' after seeing that his legs are missing. The Lakers team member then meets up with Bugs Bunny and explains that his son has been kidnapped by Al-G Rhythm, played by Don Cheadle, who wants him to play a game of basketball in exchange for his child. After several of the other Looney Tunes characters are assembled, Daffy Duck steps up as the team's coach and instructs Yosemite Sam to shoot a basketball , although his friend misinterprets his command and blasts the ball with his pistols. The team is then transformed into three-dimensional versions of themselves and are transported to the basketball arena, where numerous characters from other films and television shows had shown up to watch the match. Al-G Rhythm then introduces his own version of the Goon Squad, which is comprised of animated versions of various professional athletes, including Anthony Davis and Nneka Ogwumike. The Tunes react with despair upon seeing the superpowered basketball players, leading Daffy to remark: 'I'm not a betting duck, but my money's on the other team.' Before the start of the game, Al-G Rhythm comments that if the Tunes lose, not only will James' son never be released, they will all be deleted from existence, which causes their coach's jaw to literally hit the floor. Lola Bunny, voiced by Zendaya, later gives a motivational speech before the start of the match, leading Bugs to proclaim, 'if we're going out, we're going out looney.' The former Cavaliers member is then told by his wife, played by Sonequa Martin-Green, that he needs to get their son back before he and the rest of the Tunes take to the court. Wile E. Coyote goes on to introduce a machine that accidentally sucks him in and creates multiple versions of himself, all of which were shot towards the basket, racking up several points for the team. The trailer ends with the Tunes convening in their locker room, where James points out that Granny is pouring herself a martini at halftime, which Daffy proceeds to throw at his face. A sequel to the original Space Jam film first entered development shortly after the release of the movie, which notably starred Michael Jordan and featured performers such as Bill Murray and Danny DeVito, as well as athletes like Larry Bird. After the former Chicago Bulls team member dropped out of the project, alternative versions of the feature were planned, with Jeff Gordon and Tony Hawk set to appear alongside the Tunes in their own flicks. Plans for a sequel were eventually scrapped after the release of the 2003 film Looney Tunes: Back In Action, which did not perform well financially upon its debut. In 2014, it was announced that work on a follow-up to Space Jam had commenced, and director Terence Nance and producer Ryan Coogler's involvement were revealed in the subsequent years. Malcolm D. Lee was eventually brought in to helm the feature just weeks into filming, and physical production came to a close in September of 2019. Space Jam: A New Legacy is currently set to be released on July 16th, and it will be made available for streaming on the HBO Max service the same day. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James sent Aussie Liz Cambage well wishes for her birthday this week Cambage, who controversially sat out of the Tokyo Olympics, turned 30 The NBA legend urged outspoken Cambage to 'keep being you' NBA legend LeBron James has told Liz Cambage to 'keep being you' after the Australian basketball star dropped out of the Tokyo Olympics in controversial circumstances. James sent birthday well wishes as Cambage turned 30 this week and urged her not to tone down her outspoken personality. 'Happy birthday @ecambage. Keep being you!' James wrote, followed by the praise, closed fist, heart and crown emojis underneath a photo of the pair. Cambage was flattered to hear from 'The King' and reposted the message on her Instagram. She has been caught up in a string of controversies in the last few months. Cambage first threatened to sit out of the Olympics in protest after taking offence to two promotional photos of athletes she said did not represent the diversity of the Australian team. One of the images shared by Cambage was an ad from Olympic partner and underwear supplier Jockey, which featured white athletes and which she claimed was 'whitewashed'. The other image was an Australian uniform reveal that led Cambage to say 'fake tan does not equal diversity'. She eventually backed down and agreed to join the Australian team for Tokyo but was then plagued by reports of an on-court fight and off-court partying while attending an Opals training camp in Las Vegas. The fiery confrontation while playing Nigeria is still under investigation by Basketball Australia's Integrity Division. When Nigeria's captain Adaora Elonu was quizzed about what happened in the well-publicised but non-televised clash with Cambage in the warm-up match, she remained tight-lipped. 'It's something that happened behind closed doors. There's nothing we can really talk about with that,' she said. There were also unconfirmed rumours other members of the Opals wanted to boot Cambage from the team for being too disruptive. She eventually released a statement saying she had decided to sit out the Olympics over mental health concerns and the fact she would struggle being confined to the Tokyo players bubble. 'Every athlete competing at the Olympic Games should be at their mental and physical peak, and I am a long way from where I want and need to be,' she said. 'It's no secret I have had my struggles with mental health in the past and living in a bubble without family and friends (in Tokyo) is honestly terrifying for me. 'Relying on daily medication to control my anxiety is not the place I want to be right now. 'It breaks my heart to withdraw from the Olympics, but I think it is the best decision for the Opals and myself right now.' Cambage wished her Opals teammates 'nothing but the best of luck in Tokyo' and said she hopes they 'go forth and win a gold medal'. Australian basketball legend Andrew Bogut weighed in on the Opals star's shock withdrawal. 'Having mental health issues does not mean you cannot be criticised,' he said. 'Poor behaviour and engaging in poor team culture can be a separate issue from mental health.' Describing her as 'one of the best players in the world', Bogut claimed that mental health issues weren't the sole reason for her sudden departure from the games. 'I believe her withdrawal from the Olympics, in my opinion, is the repercussions of her behaviour,' he said. Along with birthday wishes to Cambage, 36-year-old LeBron James took the oppourtunity to declare a recent poll on the best player in the NBA in which he got zero votes would only motivate him more. An ESPN survey of 10 top scouts and executives in the NBA asked them to name to league's best player - with their votes split between the Milwaukee Buck's Giannis Antetokounmpo and the Brooklyn Nets Kevin Durant. Referring to the poll, James took to social media with the hashtags #superwashed and #washedking joking he was anything but washed up. 'Thank you! As if I didn't need more to (motivate) me! #Washed,' he wrote. The 2021-22 NBA season is set to kick off on October 19 with the LA Lakers and the Brooklyn Nets early picks to reach the finals. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Although LeBron James is almost 37 years old, he still remains one of the best players in the NBA. But if that decision were up to United States basketball legend Magic Johnson, the Los Angeles Lakers forward is clearly at the top of the American basketball league. The two stars met at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles over the weekend for the Los Angeles Chargers-Dallas Cowboys NFL game. And with that they posted on their respective social networks a photo of the meeting that took place at the game. But in his post, the Lakers legend praised the strength that LeBron James has in the NBA, calling him "the best basketball player today". Although the former Lakers star's exit from the Lakers was troubled, his relationship with the current star seems intact. Magic Johnson was the franchise's president of basketball operations from 2017 to 2019 and had a major influence on LeBron James ' contract with the Los Angeles team in 2018. Even though he is already in the late stages of his career, LeBron James is still competing at a high level. Last season, he had averages of 25 points, 7.8 assists and 7.7 rebounds, even though he missed 27 games due to his ankle injury. While healthy, the star was among the favorites for the MVP award (best player), such as the Golden State Warriors' Stephen Curry and the Denver Nuggets' Serbian forward Nikola Jokic. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Duke, Kansas, Kentucky and UNC are all reportedly interested in young Bronny The 17-year-old guard from Cleveland is listed at 6-foot-3-inches, 190 pounds On Friday, his dad LeBron posted a video of Bronny showing off his dunking skills 'Scary hours coming soon!' the proud father bragged of his eldest son LeBron James ' eldest son Bronny is drawing the interest of top college basketball programs across the country, suggesting the young high school guard is poised to follow in his father's footsteps. Duke, Kansas, Kentucky and the University of North Carolina are all eyeing the Sierra Canyon High School junior, 17, who is listed at 6-foot-3 and 190 pounds, according to 247 Sports. On Friday, LeBron James posted an Instagram video showing Bronny bouncing a ball off the backboard and executing a windmill dunk in the gym of the Southern California high school. 'Scary hours coming soon!!' the proud father wrote in the caption. Bronny, whose full name is LeBron James Jr., is the eldest of the Los Angeles Lakers star's three children. While he is a year or more away from committing to a college, some of the top programs in the country are already taking a look. 'Athletically, James is impressive and he's continued to become more athletic over the last year,' 247 Sports Director of Basketball Recruiting Evan Daniels wrote. 'Where he impresses the most is with his feel for the game, vision and passing,' he added. 'James is also an impressive shooter with good shot mechanics. He's comfortable pulling up for one and two dribble pull-ups, and has a lot of potential as a long-range shooter. It's still early in the process, and ultimately his upside will be determined by his physical and skill growth.' Though young Bronny shows tremendous promise on the court, 247 analyst Jerry Meyer says a big question remains how he responds mentally to the pressure of being the son of an NBA superstar. 'What will be interesting to observe is how he deals with the intense pressure and scrutiny from being the son of Lebron James ,' Meyer said. 'How he deals with that circumstance will have much to do with how good he becomes. He will have to go through a tough process of defining and becoming who he is as his own individual,' the analyst added. Nevertheless, Meyer said he expects Bronny 'to develop into a quality high major player.' 'And with his lineage, there is a strong reason to believe that he will have a blossoming physically and in his game in the upcoming years,' he said. Last academic year, Sierra Canyon was 16-2 in a shortened season due to the coronavirus pandemic, but Bronny sat out the season to recover from surgery to repair a torn meniscus. As a freshman the year before, Bronny James averaged 4.1 points in 15 minutes per game and was Sierra Canyon's only player to appear in all 34 games of the season. The team's winter schedule this year kicks off on November 18 with a matchup against Montgomery High School. Sierra Canyon's high-powered roster will include Kijani Wright, a five-star power forward in the Class of 2022, and fellow five-star prospect Amari Bailey. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Tristan Thompson had a fan ejected for allegedly making jabs about the Kardashians during a game in Memphis on Sunday Thompson shares a three-year-old daughter named True with Khloe Kardashian, with the two having an on-off relationship since 2016 They most recently split in June after Thompson was reportedly caught cheating The Memphis incident came just four days after LeBron James kicked out two courtside fans for shouting insults during a game Tristan Thompson had a fan ejected from a basketball game in Memphis on Sunday after the fan allegedly heckled him about the Kardashians, just four days after LeBron James kicked two fans out of a game in Indiana for hurling insults at him. A noticeably perturbed Thompson is seen pointing out a courtside fan to stadium staff in video shared by TMZ of Sunday's game between the Sacramento Kings and Memphis Grizzlies. Game officials, Grizzlies personnel and NBA security then stop play to confront the fan. As they escort the fan out of the venue, he is seen raising a glass to the crowd. It is not clear what the fan said to Thompson, who shares 3-year-old daughter True with his ex-girlfriend Khloe Kardashian, but Grizzlies beat writer Drew Hill, of the Daily Memphian, tweeted that it had something to do with the Kardashians. 'Per multiple people around the fan that was thrown out of the Grizzlies game tonight, he was tossed because he was making comments about the Kardashian family in the direction of Tristan Thompson,' Hill tweeted. Interim Kings coach Alvin Gentry did not confirm the subject of the comments, but addressed the heckling in a press conference following his team's 128-101 loss to the Grizzlies. 'I think [when] you pay for a ticket you've got a right to boo guys, you've got a right to cheer for your team ... but inappropriate things should never be what anyone should say,' Gentry said. 'I don't think it needs to be talked about or anything, but obviously it's not anything that I'm going to tolerate people saying to my players, I know that, not without intervening and asking them to be removed,' he added. Thompson and Khloe Kardashian have been in an on-off relationship since 2016 and co-parent three-year-old True. They reportedly broke off the relationship in June after Thompson was allegedly caught cheating on Khloe Kardashian numerous times. Thompson has allegedly been 'begging Khloe to take him back,' sources told OK! Magazine, adding that Thompson has been on his 'best behavior' to show her that she has his support with True. 'Tristan is around way more than before - he's doing all the classes with True, doing the family dinners and is a very present dad. He's 100% co-parenting and there for everything,' the source told OK! Magazine. The Memphis incident came just four days after LeBron James kicked out two courtside fans for shouting insults during a game against the Indiana Pacers at Gainbridge Fieldhouse in Indianapolis. The fans were allegedly removed for ' violating the NBA Fan Code of Conduct,' NBA spokesperson Mike Bass said. The Action Network tweeted a clip of the incident captioned: 'LeBron unhappy with some fans courtside in Indiana.' 'There's a difference between cheering on your home faithful, booing opponents, not wanting your opponents to be successful, and then there's moments where it goes outside the line with obscene gestures or words. That shouldn't be tolerated in our game from nobody,' James told reporters after the game. Further detail on what exactly those fans said to James has not been shared. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 MADRID, 4 (EUROPA PRESS) Los Angeles Lakers player LeBron James showed his frustration and anger with the NBA after a false-positive coronavirus forced him to miss his team's 117-92 win over the Sacramento Kings. James returned in Friday's 119-115 loss to the Los Angeles Clippers having met the NBA's Covid-19 protocols after additional tests confirmed he was not a positive case. The four-time MVP and NBA champion, however, was frustrated by the process that cost him a game and caused him "great inconvenience" after he recorded a positive test before Tuesday's game in Sacramento. "Pretty confused, frustrated, angry that there was nothing I could do," James said at the postgame news conference. LeBron, 36, explained that his discomfort was due to immediate isolation rather than being able to take an additional test to confirm his status. "Usually when you have a positive test, they test him right away to make sure," James said. "There was no follow-up test after my positive test. It was straight into isolation and they put you on a protocol. That was the part that made me mad," he admitted. "I had to find a way to get back from Sacramento by myself, no security, no one. I had to put my kids in isolation for the time being. People in my house in isolation. It was a big inconvenience . That was the anger part," the Lakers player said. James added that he always knew he wasn't Covid-19 positive, that he had no symptoms, which further fueled his frustration in the process. "I knew I was going to be 'released,'" he said. "I never felt bad at all. I know you can be asymptomatic, but if what I had was a positive Covid test, what are we doing, what are we talking about?" he wondered "I thought this whole thing was handled very poorly. Being able to get the all clear was a breath of fresh air for me, my family and my friends," said LeBron James , who has missed four straight games this season because of a variety of problems including ankle and abdominal injuries along with a suspension and his false positive test. The Lakers forward, who returned with 23 points, 11 rebounds and six assists against the Clippers, has played in 11 games this season, averaging 25.8 points, 5.2 rebounds and 6.8 assists. The Lakers are 12-12 after Friday's loss. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 MADRID, 20 (EUROPA PRESS) Forward LeBron James became the NBA's second all-time leading scorer this morning, surpassing Karl Malone, after scoring 38 points in the Los Angeles Lakers' 127-119 loss to the Washington Wizards, trailing only Kareem Abdul-Jabbar on the all-time list. With his spectacular performance on Saturday - 16 of 29 shooting from the field, including 4 of 10 three-pointers, 10 rebounds and six assists - which still did not allow the Los Angeles Lakers to win, James reaches 36,947 career points, beating the Utah Jazz legend's mark (36,928). With that, he falls 1,440 points shy of Abdul-Jabbar's record (38,387). "I won't let myself think about that. I've always focused on playing and these things have happened organically. I hope to achieve it at some point in my career, but I won't think about it too much," he said in a press conference about the possibility to the historic player of the purple and gold. The Lakers superstar has scored 1,580 points this season. "Just being a part of this league for so many years, being linked with some of the best players, guys that I've seen play or read about, that have inspired me to be the way I am... I'm speechless. It's an honor for me, my friends and family to live these moments," he concluded. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 In recent days, Los Angeles has once again been dominated by a 34-centimeter gold figure. In the metropolis on the West Coast, the acting elite met for the presentation of the world's most important film award. LeBron James also received an award, but on the evening before the glamorous Oscar gala. The basketball star of the Los Angeles Lakers was presented with the Golden Raspberry. James received the mock award, which sees itself as the antithesis of the venerable Academy Award, for his performance as the lead actor in Space Jam 2. In the "115-minute commercial for everything from Warner-Media," as the jury smugly summed up the work, he helps a team of Looney Tunes characters led by Bugs Bunny win a basketball game. Just as Michael Jordan had already done in the first part of 1996. A classic basketball movie that has been remade but doesn't come close to the original. If you look at the games of the Los Angeles Lakers these days, the role of basketball player LeBron James resembles that of his movie character. At 37, the exceptional player sets personal records every week and is the undisputed leader of a team that looks up to him. The crucial difference: unlike on the big screen, James does not manage to lead his team off the pitch as winners. The image of the traditional NBA club, which is worth more than five billion dollars, is now clearly scratched. The glamour of its glory days is fading fast. Champions two years ago, the Lakers are now facing a sporting disaster: missing the playoffs. "It feels like another missed opportunity for me and for our team," James admitted after the 108-116 loss to the New Orleans Pelicans, visibly disillusioned. Despite leading by 20 points at halftime, Los Angeles had suffered its 43rd loss in the 74th game of the season. In the standings, the team from Hollywood slipped to tenth place behind New Orleans. If the direct comparison with the Pelicans decides the battle for the playoff places, the Lakers will also be inferior there. If they finish eleventh, they will have no chance of making the playoffs. In addition, rival San Antonio has just as many wins as the Lakers and the much easier remaining schedule. If you look at the mere balance sheet, Los Angeles is really just an ordinary NBA team this season. In most games, the team's performances do not justify participation in the playoffs. But in Los Angeles, the spotlights usually shine much more brightly on the pros. The demands, both inside and outside the club, are higher than in other locations. The squad, a collection of big-name players long past their prime, is not living up to expectations. The Lakers lead only one of the NBA's countless statistics this season: they make up the oldest team of all 30, with an average age of 30. Los Angeles had already failed in the first playoff round last season. This was a major blow to the team, which reacted by signing a number of veteran players. Russel Westbrook is a symbol of the problems in the squad. The set-up man, once the league's most valuable player, has been criticized since his first appearance in the purple and gold jersey. Westbrook is at his strongest when he has the ball in his own hands. If the 33-year-old doesn't have that, his weak throwing prevents him from making an effective impact on the game. In Los Angeles, however, Westbrook usually has to leave the ball to James, who calls for it more and more often the worse the sporting situation gets. A vicious circle. The result is that the 2.06-meter powerhouse sets another career best in almost every game: Most recently, he became the second player in league history to score at least 37,000 points. Even leader Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, a Lakers legend, is yet to overtake James. He's averaging 30.1 points this season, the most since 2005. The price: James is also forced to play the most minutes per game in six years. In the bone-milling NBA, with its 81 season games, that's an important factor. To be sure, James is famous for his outstanding athleticism and, by his own admission, spends up to $1.5 million per season on nutrition and workouts to maintain his performance level. But no one wins the battle against time in the long run. James often looks tired toward the end of games because coach Frank Vogel can't give him the rest he needs. After the exertions of the regular, the superstar would go into the playoffs anything but rested. In addition, the tension also increases off the field. James has a claim to be included in discussions about the direction of the club. Once again, media reports surfaced at the beginning of the year that James had demanded reinforcements for the team. When the desired changes did not happen, the star player had openly announced his dissatisfaction. James is playing for the Lakers in the first season of an $85 million, two-year contract. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 A granny from Geelong has gone viral for her slick basketball moves on-court Clips of her dominating 'walking basketball ' have been viewed 8.7million times The senior citizen is seen nailing a lay-up and displaying a flawless jump shot NBA legend LeBron James is no stranger to going viral with his insane skills, but he might have some competition after a video of an Aussie granny completely dominating a walking basketball game was seen by more than 6.5million people. There were shades of the late Kobe Bryant's signature rip-through move before the newly minted star - known only as Jules - completed a flawless lay-up in a video posted on Geelong United Basketball 's (GUB) TikTok account. Jules, who clearly has quite the basketball pedigree if her skills are anything to go by, wasn't content with only going viral once. The GUB account called it 'one of the nastiest rip-throughs we've seen at walking basketball '. In another game, her jump shot technique was on full display, with the senior even fading backwards a la LeBron or Steph Curry as the ball swished through the net. Curry's jump shot is the thing of legend, while James' monster athleticism is on full display when launches himself into a lay-up. Jules has racked up an incredible 8.7million views for her slick on-court moves, and looks to be an absolute nightmare for her hapless opponents. She even drew the attention of the 247basketball TikTok account, who jokingly welcomed her to the storied University of Maine women's basketball program. 'Welcome to the team, hell of a first step man. There might be some problems with your eligibility, but we'll work past that ... we're gonna call you Betty. Welcome to the team Betty,' laughed the viral performer. Basketball fans also doted on Jules/'Betty's' 'fire jump shot' and skills. 'She's still got it for real,' one user commented, which was liked by almost 108,000 people, while another laughed her rip-through was 'the move that made LeBron cry.' 'She used all her stamina bar in one go,' one wrote in reference to the popular game NBA 2K, and many were convinced Jules 'definitely played basketball back in her day.' Jules might even have a new nickname, with one user suggesting she is a 'midcourt maestro.' Walking Basketball is a more gentler form of the sport focused on inclusion for more senior players and has one simple rule - no running! One of Jules' opponents tried to call travel on her after the lay-up, but we've given the move a double thumbs up here at the Daily Mail Australia office; with other TikTok user also scoffing at the travel suggestion! Geelong United Basketball is 'Australia's largest regional community basketball organisation' according to its Facebook page, runs walking basketball and senior competitions, among others. By the looks of it, it's absolutely ON for young and old down in 'The Pivot' city. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Kevin Durant was announced as owner along with Rich Kleiman as MLP owners He now joins LeBron James , Draymond Green, Tom Brady and more as owners Pickleball became a small passion for Durant thanks to Kleiman having him play Click here for all your latest international Sports news from DailyMail.com Kevin Durant has become the latest sports superstar to own a professional pickleball franchise. The Brooklyn Nets forward, along with his manager Rich Kleiman and their joint venture Thirty Five Ventures, were announced as the owners of Major League Pickleball expansion team. '35V is committed to investing in the future of sports and emerging opportunities, and Major League Pickleball is a league we are incredibly excited about,' Kleiman said in a news release. 'We can't wait to build this team from the ground up as well as work to elevate the sport and the league to unprecedented heights. As an avid pickleball player and fan, the interest in the sport was a natural fit.' Durant spoke in a video recorded in one of 35V's board rooms and said: 'We really, really want to leave our mark from day one on how hard our team is going to play and how successful we're going to be as a group.' The 12-time all-star also said that he 'never thought he would get into pickleball, but it became a 'small passion' after Kleiman got him to play the game. 'Kevin Durant, Rich Kleiman, and 35V are going to be game-changing partners for Major League Pickleball,' Steve Kuhn, the MLP CEO and founder, said in the release. 'They not only bring passion for the game, but also unrivaled expertise to help us to continue to grow. We're especially excited about their plans to bring pickleball to underserved communities -- something they have done with basketball for years.' The Durant and Kleiman duo now joins the likes of Lebron James and Maverick Carter, Draymond Green and Kevin Love, as well as Tom Brady and Kim Clijsters as owners of a MLP expansion team. Brady and Clijsters' group is led by Knighthead Capital Management LLC and also includes financial advisor Kaitlyn Kerr, who will serve as the team's general manager. Matt Alvarez, a film producer who worked on 'Straight Outta Compton,' is also an investor. Played both indoors and out, pickleball is a paddle sport similar to tennis, only instead of a fuzzy Dunlop, it uses a perforated, hollow polymer ball. Court sizes are about a third the size of tennis dimensions, and the game relies on volleying much more than its sister sport. The MLP has six tournaments scheduled for 2023 with more than $2million in prize money up for grabs. The sport has suddenly gained in popularity, although pinpointing an exact reason for that is difficult. One survey from the Sports and Fitness Industry Association found that 4.8 million Americans played pickleball in 2021, which is up 11.5 percent over the previous five years. Pickleball has an advantage over other sports because it can be played by athletes of all ages, although it's most popular among the 18-to-34 demographic. The MLP said it aims to grow the sport to 40 million players by the end of the decade. Gloria Marie James , mother of the Los Angeles Lakers star, had a 'vision' and the famous toy basket from the American brand Little Tikes is one of the reasons why we have been enjoying the NBA legend for so many years. The most cost-effective gift, costing just $25 , started King James in the world of basketball , a sport he would eventually rule to become one of the best in history. "I chatted with LeBron James in the hallway after Sunday night's game and asked him what his most memorable Christmas gift had been. Many wonder what would have become of LeBron James if instead of receiving a toy basket... he had received a soccer, a sport he played in high school. He stood out for his size and speed as a wide receiver and received offers from some universities , such as Notre Dame . However, his soccer career was cut short after he broke his wrist the summer before his senior year in high school in an Amateur Athletic Union game. "Michael [Jordan]was a super athlete, so was Kobe [Bryant], so was Kawhi [Leonard] . But I don't know if there's ever been an athlete like LeBron James in our league. I truly believe that if LeBron James had played soccer, he could have been the greatest soccer player of all time, and at any position," Doc Rivers said in 2020. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 NBA great Lebron James teased the release of a clothing range with Liverpool The star arrived for LA Lakers latest NBA match wearing a special edition kit James has collaborated with Liverpool on a series of trainers in recent years LeBron James has all but confirmed an upcoming clothing range with Liverpool after been pictured arriving at a Los Angeles Lakers match sporting a special edition kit. The shirt matches matched images leaked from a Liverpool fan account on Twitter earlier this month. James arrived for the LA Lakers match against the San Antonio Spurs on Wednesday wearing a special edition kit, which is expected to be released in the LeBron and Liverpool collection. The shirt features his logo in the centre and is black with red pin stripes. Liverpool and Nike logos are also featured, while the letters YNWA (You'll Never Walk Alone) are included as part of of the design. James also wore Nike Air Max 1 trainers on Wednesday, which features his insignia on the heel, as well as the Liverpool logo. The 38-year-old star shared images on social media following the LA Lakers 113-104 win over the San Antonio Spurs, showing his support for Liverpool. An upcoming release of Nike LeBron 20 Liverpool trainers has also been teased, with a Liverpool's red colours central to the design of the latest collaboration. The release of a clothing range between James and Liverpool would build on the existing relationship, with footwear having been produced since the basketball legend took a minority stake in the Premier League club back in 2011. The four-time NBA champion, first bought a two per cent stake in Liverpool in 2011 for \'a34.7m with business partner Maverick Carter, the same year FSG bought Liverpool for \'a3300m. The Premier League team's value has soared to what Forbes estimates to be worth \'a33.6bn. This followed Liverpool's Champions League triumph in 2019 and Premier League title win in 2020, as well as renovation and expansion of the club's historic Anfield stadium and a money-spinning television deal worth. James converted his stake in Liverpool to a one per cent share in FSG's global empire in March 2021. As well as Liverpool and the Red Sox, FSG also owns the Pittsburgh Penguins hockey team, 80 per cent of New England Sports Network and half of Roush Fenway Racing. Forbes last year estimated James's FSG take to be worth \'a374m. Both James and Liverool have long-standing agreements with Nike. The basketball great first signed a deal with the sportwear giant in 2003 at age 18, before penning a lifetime deal in 2015, which is claimed to be worth 'tens of millions' a year. In June 2022, Forbes named James as the first active NBA player to become a billionaire. Liverpool chairman Tom Werner had told the Boston Globe in 2021 that a product line with James had been planned, with seven or eight products being produced by Nike. The collaboration was likened to Michael Jordan's historic deal with Nike, which began with the launch of Air Jordans in 1985 and has become a vastly successful brand. FSG announced last November that they were considering new stakeholders at Liverpool. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James sat in the visitors locker room at Madison Square Garden with ice on his 38-year-old knees and 28 more points to his name after his Los Angeles Lakers beat the Knicks in overtime. James s teammate Anthony Davis teased him about how close he was to breaking Kareem Abdul-Jabbar s N.B.A. career scoring record, then about 90 points away. Suddenly, James remembered something. His mother, Gloria James, was set to go on vacation soon. She might miss his record-breaking game. He called her on speakerphone, with a dozen attentive reporters close by. He asked when she was leaving, reminding her every once in a while, lest she disclose too much, that reporters could hear the conversation. Eventually, he looked around, sheepishly, and said he would call her later. I love you, he said. Then, just before he ended the call, he added: I love you more. It was typical James: He brings you along for the ride, but on his terms, revealing what he wants to reveal and no more. It is perhaps the only way someone who has been so famous for most of his life could survive the machine of modern celebrity. As he has closed in on Abdul-Jabbar s record of 38,387 points, the very idea of what it means to be a star has shifted since James scored his first two points on Oct. 29, 2003. And James has helped define that shift. He has risen above the din of social media celebrities and 24-hour news cycles, buoyed by the basketball fans who love him or love to hate him. He s now 36 points away from breaking Abdul-Jabbar s record after a game against the New Orleans Pelicans on Saturday. He has been a selfie-snapping tour guide for this journey, with a portfolio that now extends well beyond the court. He has a production company and a show on HBO. He s acted in a few movies and received some good reviews. His foundation has helped hundreds of students in his hometown Akron, Ohio, and a public school the foundation helps run there, the I Promise School, focuses on children who struggle academically. His opinions are covered as news, given far more weight than those of almost any other athlete. Hopefully I made an impact enough so people appreciate what I did, and still appreciate what I did off the floor as well, even when I m done, James said in an interview. But I don t live for that. I live for my family, for my friends and my community that needs that voice. Basketball Is the Main Thing In early 2002, James was a high school junior and on the cover of Sports Illustrated. News didn t travel as quickly as it does now. Not everyone had cellphones, and the ones they had couldn t livestream videos of whatever anyone did. Social media meant chat rooms on AOL or Yahoo. Facebook had yet to launch, and the deluge of social networking apps was years away. Thank God I didn t have social media; that s all I can say, James said in October when asked to reflect on his entry into the league. As a teenage star, he was spared the incessant gaze of social media and the bullying and harsh criticism that most likely would have come with it. But social media, in its many changing forms, has also helped people express their personalities and share their lives with others. It lets them define themselves something particularly useful for public figures whose stories get told one way or another. James began thinking about that early in his career. His media and production firm, now called the SpringHill Company, made a documentary about James and his high school teammates titled More Than a Game in 2008. It also developed The Shop, an HBO show James sometimes appears on with celebrity guests, including former President Barack Obama and the rapper Travis Scott, talking like friends in a barbershop. James likes to say that he always keeps the main thing the main thing meaning that no matter what else is happening in his life, he prioritizes basketball . He honors the thing that created his fame. He led his teams to the N.B.A. finals in eight consecutive years and won championships with three different franchises. He was chosen for the league s Most Valuable Player Award four times, and he has dished the fourth-most assists in N.B.A. history. James s talent meant it didn t take long for him to become the face of the N.B.A. He has mostly embraced that, capitalizing on an era when sports fandom was no longer about sitting down to watch a game so much as it was about catching small bites of the most compelling moments. People s interest in athletes moves very quickly, especially with the N.B.A. season, said Omar Raja, who in 2014 founded House of Highlights, an Instagram account for viral sports moments, because he wanted to share clips of the Miami Heat during James s time playing there with Dwyane Wade and Chris Bosh. LeBron s Instagram stories would do as well as his poster dunks, and you were like, This is crazy, Raja said. House of Highlights reposted two videos from James

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James has become the HYPERLINK /topic/nba 00 NBA s all-time leading points scorer after the HYPERLINK /news/people/who-is-dallas-cowboys-owner-jerry-jones-nba-star-lebron-james-comments-controversy-3940221 00 Lakers star hit 38 points in a 133-130 defeat to Oklahoma City Thunder. His 38 points saw him pass Kareem Abdul-Jabbar s record which had been held for 39 years. HYPERLINK /sport/nba-all-time-scoring-list-who-has-most-points-lebron-james-3885998 00 Abdul-Jabbar broke the scoring record in April 1984, eight months before James was born, and was present to watch the 38-year-old take the necessary 36 points. James hit the record-breaking points with a fadeaway jumper at the end of the third quarter, finishing the match with a career total of 38,390, and a brief break in play was held to mark the achievement. The 75-year-old Abdul-Jabbar, who stood and applauded when James celebrated the points, ceremoniously handed over the ball to the Lakers star in front of a crowd which included tennis icon John McEnroe, music stars Jay-Z, LL Cool J; HYPERLINK /news/people/who-is-the-highest-paid-youtube-star-top-10-earners-biggest-net-worth-2022-mrbeast-jake-paul-3855713 00 boxer Floyd Mayweather Jr and actor Denzel Washington. What has been said? Speaking after he hit the record-breaking points, James said: To be able to be in the presence of a legend and great as Kareem, it means so much to me. Everyboyd that has ever been a part of this run with me the last 20-plus years, I want to say thank you so much because I wouldn t be me without all you. You all helped. Your passion and sacrifices helped me to get to this point. And to the NBA, to Adam Silver, to the late great David Stern, thank you very much for allowing me to be a part of something I always dreamed about. I would never in a million years dreamt this to be even better than what it is tonight. Abdul-Jabbar, who retired in 1989, said of his successor: I thought it had every chance of being broken. It just had to have someone that the offense focused on continually. LeBron s career is one of someone who planned to dominate this game. You have to give him credit for just the way he played and for the way he s lasted and dominated. LeBron James s record-breaking career James was drafted first overall by his hometown team the Cleveland Cavaliers in 2003. The forward then joined the Miami Heat in 2010, where he won two titles, before returning to the Cavaliers and leading them to their only NBA title in 2016. Since 2018, James has been with the Lakers and helped them to win the 2020 title, which was also the fourth time he has been named NBA finals MVP. The two-time Olympic Champion also has four regular season MVP titles and has appeared in the NBA finals 10 times. The 38-year-old holds the record as the youngest player to reach every significant points tally fom 5,000 to 35,000, passing six-time NBA champion Michael Jordan to reach fourth overall in March 2019 before passing the late Lakers legend Kobe Bryant to move into third all-time in January 2020. 23,119 points were scored in 849 games for the Cavaliers during 11 seasons across his two spells; 7,919 points were scored for the Heat in 294 games and now James has 7,314 points for the Lakers in 266 games across five seasons. The 19-time All-Star is also in the top-10 all-time lists for assists, steals, defensive rebounds, field goals made and three-pointers made. What is LeBron James height and net worth? James stands at 6ft 9in tall (2.06m) and this makes him one of the tallest and most physically imposing players in the NBA. The tallest NBA star still playing is Boban Marjonivic who stands at 7ft 3in tall but the tallest NBA player in history is recorded to be the Romanian and US star Gheorghe Muresan who played for the Washington Bullets and New Jersey Jets. He was 7ft 7in tall and played in the NBA between 1993 and 2000. Not only is James now the all-time points record holder, but he is also listed as the wealthiest basketball star in the league. He is estimated to have a 2023 net worth of $1bn (\'a3826m) according to Forbes. Along with his career earnings, he is earned extra through contracts such as with Nike, his investment with Blaze Pizza and SpringHill Entertainment. James also owns a 2% stake in Liverpool FC which is estimated to be worth at least $40bn. Who are the highest all-time points scorers? Here are the top 10 highest points scorers in the NBA. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James passed Kareem Abdul Jabbar to become the top scorer in the NBA with 38,388 points over 20 seasons. Sport is obsessed with milestones to measure greatness, often to its own detriment. But some milestones truly mean something and are a culmination of an athlete's whole career. On Tuesday night (Wednesday morning in India), LeBron James became the all-time leading scorer in the National Basketball Association, surpassing a record held by the great Kareem Abdul Jabbar since 1984. 38,388 POINTS LeBron James hits the fadeaway jumper to become the NBA's all-time leading scorer! #ScoringKing - NBA (@NBA) February 8, 2023 Needing 36 points to pass Kareem in the match against the Oklahoma City Thunder, LeBron reached the mark in the third quarter with a fadeaway jumper over two defenders, scoring his 38,388th point in the process. Considered to be one of the greatest players to ever pick up a basketball , LeBron James has won four NBA titles, competing in a total of 10 NBA finals. He has a career average of 27.2 points, 7.5 rebounds and 7.3 assists, having played 1409 games over a 20-year-long career that started in 2003. This season, aged 38, LeBron has defied age and expectations, averaging 30/8.5/7.1 for the Los Angeles Lakers. LeBron James : One of the most hyped teenage athletes of all time The story of LeBron James begins in Akron, Ohio, being raised by a single mother. Despite many hardships faced growing up in the inner city of Akron, James's athletic talent was spotted early. In high school, he was not just a great basketball prospect, he also was the school's star football player, playing wide receiver with grace and athleticism. However, hoops were always his calling, combining the sheer force of his size with the unmatched nimbleness of his feet. Over his four years playing for St Vincent-St Mary High School, James carried the little-known varsity team to the national spotlight, racking up innumerable wins while showing off his own prowess. During his junior season, LeBron James appeared on the cover of Sports Illustrated, which christened him "The Chosen One". Not even in the NBA, James already had the weight of expectations on his back, with comparisons to Michael Jordan and "Magic" Johnson rife. And James delivered, more than anyone could ever imagine. While sports is constantly on the lookout for the "next big thing", few prodigies eventually pan out. As LeBron James became the NBA's highest-ever scorer, it is incredible to look back at where his journey started and how "the kid from Akron" surpassed the already sky-high expectations of him. A 'pass-first guy' who became NBA's leading scorer James's NBA career has been a showcase of raw athleticism combined with one of the sharpest basketball brains out there. While his tomahawk dunks over multiple helpless defenders make the highlights, LeBron's true brilliance lies in the fact that he always makes the right play, doing what is best for the team. "I'm not a natural scorer." - LeBron James , All-Time Leading Scorer - UNINTERRUPTED (@uninterrupted) February 8, 2023 This was noticeable from a very early age, as LeBron did not just put up amazing individual numbers but also raised the game of his teammates. On offence, his sheer presence commands the attention of the opposing defenders, providing his teammates with the space and time to make easier shots. For many, LeBron's greatness lies in the fact that he has seldom been selfish in such situations, always making the right pass, sometimes in routine ways and often, with a little pizazz. LeBron James has always been a "pass-first guy", as he himself admitted to ESPN's Dave McMenamin in a recent interview. Yet, he has managed to score more points than anyone else in the history of the NBA, more points than Karl Malone, Michael Jordan, Kobe Bryant, Wilt Chamberlain, Shaquille O'Neal or any other "pure scorer" that has graced the basketball court. Sustained excellence Yes, many people rightly say that he has climbed the top of the scoring charts simply because of the sheer length of time he has spent in the league. According to NBA.com, the average NBA player plays a little over 4.5 years in the league. While great players often play for a longer duration, "father time remains undefeated", as James himself said in 2022. Kareem just handed LeBron James the game ball... Chills... - Tavio Thrower (@TavioThrowerNBA) February 8, 2023 Yet James seems to consistently defy ageing. Not only has he managed to stay in the league for so long, but he has also been consistently great for twenty years now. While in his early days in Cleveland, he was the unstoppable wrecking ball, with bounce and athleticism to beat players, James' game has evolved today to be more cerebral. What has not changed is the output he produces and the impact he has on his team. While there has been a gradual decline, LeBron at 38 is a better playe

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Legendary Earvin "Magic" Johnson paid tribute Tuesday to LeBron James , who became the all-time leading scorer in NBA history by surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's mark. "I never thought anyone would break Kareem's points record but that says how special you are and what you've done for basketball around the world. You changed basketball ," said "Magic" in a video tribute to LeBron. (INFOGRAPHY) LeBron James , the one who rules NBA basketball . Although most of his points were scored by LeBron in his two stints with the Cleveland Cavaliers and his years with the Miami Heat, "Magic," a Los Angeles Lakers legend, was happy to see "King James" achieve this record with the team from the Californian city. "It means even more to me and to our fans because you're wearing the purple and gold jersey and you broke the record as a Laker," he said with a smile. Later, "Magic" stressed on his Twitter account that "this historic moment was so special because we will never see another LeBron James ." "I don't think anyone will ever surpass LeBron as the NBA's leading scorer!" he assured. With 38 points against the Oklahoma City Thunder (he needed 36), LeBron broke Abdul-Jabbar's legendary record, which had stood since 1989 at 38,387 points, and put it at 38,390 with the expectation that it will continue to rise in future games. LeBron, 38, added this enormous accomplishment to a spectacular career that includes four NBA titles and four MVP awards. In his 20th season in the league, LeBron continues to compete at an extraordinary level and is averaging 30.2 points, 8.5 rebounds and 7 assists per game this year. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 THERE are numerous factors that make it difficult to appreciate that which is special in real time. For starters, valuing the finite nature of anything brings into sharper focus the fleetingness of our own existence, and for obvious reasons, that's something we'd generally rather not dwell upon. Beyond that, life passes by at a frenetic pace, and most days are spent just trying to weather the storm. Most special, resonant, or important people or events, for that reason, are more easily appreciated in retrospect: The rose-coloured glass of the rearview mirror highlighting everything that was once muddled by the chaos of life. But it's a worthy exercise in mindfulness to take stock of that which is here now. Such an opportunity arises this week after LeBron James broke a record on Tuesday night that was long believed to be unbreakable: Passing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar to become the league's all-time leading scorer. James has referenced the recently "retired for good" Tom Brady as inspiration for what his decades-plus career may look like when all is said and done. Brady is a blueprint for the kind of athlete who was confronted by Father Time and, for the most part, laughed in his face - continuing to shatter records into his 40s. James isn't knocking on the door of retirement just yet - he has said that he wants to continue playing long enough to share the court with his eldest son Bronny, who won't be eligible for the draft until 2024 - which makes the celebration of this achievement feel unique. It's not often that athletes are still active, let alone still dominant, while receiving their flowers. "He's 38!" is a refrain that's become almost a clich\'e9 to NBA viewers in this, James's 20th season of dominance. It's mentioned pretty much every time he steps on the court, but with good reason: the sport has never seen anything like James's tenure. He's been one of the best (and for many seasons, the best) players in the league in every year of his storied career, singular in both production and durability. He was drafted to the NBA in 2003, directly out of high school, to unprecedented hype, gracing the cover of Sports Illustrated with the headline 'The Chosen One' at the ripe old age of 17. But somehow, even with all the fanfare, James managed to exceed expectations. No one has ever entered the league to more fever-pitched ballyhoo, but the Ohio native bested even the highest hopes for what he would become. It is, quite simply, extraordinary. Most thought Abdul-Jabbar's scoring record (set in 1984, the same year James was born), would never be broken. Even James himself seems surprised by the feat, telling ESPN's Dave McMenamin: "The scoring record was never, ever even thought of in my head because I've always been a pass-first guy." It's true. James' game, in many ways, more closely mirrors that of a creator like Magic Johnson than a scorer's scorer like his biggest GOAT title rival, Michael Jordan. James currently sits at fourth on the all-time assists list for that very reason. When asked about the impact James has had on the game, his team-mate Anthony Davis highlighted what a span of time like 20 years really means in the NBA. "Watching him since I was younger, he's been like a role model, idol type, for me," said Davis in the days leading up to Tuesday's game. "We had a good conversation when I first got here, about how I used to go to his camp, and looked up to him, like 'I want a picture!' and now, we're team-mates and ended up winning a championship that [first] year. It all comes full circle." And in a comical moment when the Houston Rockets came to town last month, rookie Jabari Smith Jr jokingly reminded James that Smith's own father had played in James's very first NBA game, jabbing at him: "You feel old, don't you?" The reality is, though, that breaking the scoring record is indelibly tied to the passing of time. As impressive (shockingly so, at times) as James has been in his 20th season, and as much as he impresses upon us that ageing doesn't (both by his play and in those pervasive commercials with Jason Momoa) matter, it does eventually come for us all. Because of this, the joy of passing Abdul-Jabbar comes as a package deal with the melancholy the feat is inextricably tied to: this "rollercoaster ride", as James called it after the record was broken, will end at some point. And that day grows ever nearer. The energy in Crypto.com Arena on Tuesday night was kinetic before warm-ups even began, everyone in the arena buzzing off the high that only the anticipation of proximity to something special can provide. James entered the building in an immaculately tailored black suit and black sunglasses, dressed like he meant business. It called to mind Johnny Cash when someone told him he was dressed for a funeral. Cash replied: "Maybe I am." The funeral this time would be for any doubts about his greatness. He accessoris

The NBA's all-time leading scorer took to social media Sunday morning to describe the dream he had overnight and it promised to be a good one for those who love basketball . It was James and legend Michael Jordan linking up but the 38-year-old woke up at a critical stage. James and fans were left in the dark over how 'shooting the s***' with Jordan panned out. The Tweet began: 'Just woke up from having a dream that MJ and I was shooting the s*** and back and forth at the Men's National Championship game between North Carolina/Duke! 'Great vibes and EPIC CLASSIC GAME! It was a tie game/94-94/5 secs left/NC with the ball.' With the game at a crucial moment, James revealed he woke up as he lamented 'never getting to the end.' fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Kevin Durant has agreed a lifetime contract with sportswear giant Nike He, Michael Jordan and LeBron James only basketball players to have such deals Dailymail.com provides all the latest international sports news Phoenix Suns star Kevin Durant has signed a lifetime contract with sportswear giant Nike. He has followed in the footsteps of NBA legends Michael Jordan and LeBron James as the only basketball players to have lifetime deals. The 34-year-old has been with Nike since he made his NBA debut in 2007 -- the season he won the Rookie of the Year award -- and has released 15 different pairs of basketball sneakers. 'When I first signed with Nike, I couldn't have dreamed of how far we'd go in this partnership. We've done amazing work creatively and philanthropically,' Durant, a twice NBA champion and 13-times All Star, said in a statement via Boardroom. 'We've traveled the world together and built a business that will now last forever. I'm excited for the future and honored to be in rare company with this deal.' Nike has supported Durant's involvement in grassroots basketball by helping refurbish courts in communities across the world through the Durant Family Foundation. 'As one of the best basketball players in the world, Kevin Durant has been an important part of the Nike family for the past 16 years,' Nike's executive vice president of global sports marketing John Slusher said. 'We look forward to continuing to serve the next generation of athletes together.' Durant's Suns are through to the Western Conference semifinals where they will play the Denver Nuggets. LeBron has previously said that before he retires, he'd like to play on the same team as his sons. Bryce is currently not rated and is part of the Class of 2025. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James is a famous U.S. player who always draws attention for his victories. This time, the spotlight is not only on the athlete, but also on his partner, Savannah James, who shines with her own light. That is why in this article we will tell you how much is the fortune that both public figures amass, among other data that you should know in relation to this topic. WHO IS LEBRON JAMES LeBron James is a renowned American professional basketball player. He was born on December 30, 1984 in Akron, Ohio. He is widely considered one of the greatest basketball players of all time and has left a significant mark on the sport. Since his high school days, LeBron James drew attention for his basketball skill and talent. He was chosen as the player of the year in high school and subsequently entered the NBA directly in the 2003 Draft, being selected in the first position by the Cleveland Cavaliers. Throughout his professional career, LeBron James has played for several NBA teams, including the Cleveland Cavaliers (in two different stints), the Miami Heat and the Los Angeles Lakers, his current team since 2018. He has achieved numerous successes and accolades, including four NBA championships, four NBA Most Valuable Player (MVP) awards and 17 All-Star Game selections. LeBron James is known for his versatility and exceptional athletic abilities. He has a unique combination of size, strength, speed and technical skills, which allows him to play a variety of positions and have a significant impact on the game both on offense and defense. In addition to his success in basketball , LeBron James has also been recognized for his activism and work off the court. He has used his platform to advocate for social justice, fight inequality and support various charitable causes. WHO IS SAVANNAH JAMES Savannah James, whose maiden name is Savannah Brinson, is the wife of renowned basketball player LeBron James . She was born on August 27, 1986 in Akron, Ohio, United States. Savannah met LeBron James in high school, where they began their relationship and have been together ever since. They married on September 14, 2013 and have three children together: LeBron James Jr, Bryce Maximus James and Zhuri James. Over the years, Savannah has been a constant support for LeBron in his professional career. She has been present at games and has been a pillar of support for him in both the moments of success and challenges he has faced in basketball . In addition to her role as a wife and mother, Savannah has also taken on personal projects. She has worked in the world of fashion and design, and has launched her own jewelry line called "The Jewel Box." She has also been active in charity work and has been involved in community projects in her hometown of Akron. Savannah James is known for her discretion and for maintaining a private life away from the media spotlight. Although her husband is a well-known public figure, she has chosen to keep a lower profile and focus on her family and personal interests. LEBRON AND SAVANNAH JAMES' IMMENSE FORTUNE According to information provided by Celebrity Net Worth, LeBron James ' net worth is estimated at $600 million. According to Forbes' list of the highest paid athletes in 2023, LeBron earned a total of $119.5 million, of which $44.5 million was generated on the playing field, while $75 million came from outside sources. In addition to his career in sports and sponsorship contracts, LeBron is involved in different businesses. He is co-owner of Ladder, a sports nutrition company, and also founded SpringHill Company, which is dedicated to the development of entertainment content. According to Forbes, he also owns a stake in Fenway Sports Group, which owns the Boston Red Sox, Liverpool FC and Pittsburgh Penguins teams. Recently, LeBron made investments in Major League Baseball's Pickleball team and companies such as Canyon Bicycles, Neutral Foods (a climate-conscious dairy company) and the Mitchell & Ness sportswear brand. On the other hand, Savannah James, wife of LeBron, has an estimated net worth of $50 million, according to Celebrity Net Worth. Savannah is a successful businesswoman, entrepreneur, philanthropist, and furniture and interior designer. In short, the couple of LeBron and Savannah James have a combined net worth of approximately $650 million, thanks to LeBron's successful basketball careers, as well as his investments and ventures in various businesses.
