fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James gave a cryptic response when asked about his NBA future It comes after the Lakers were swept 4-0 by the Nuggets in Conference Finals DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news LeBron James may have played his final NBA game after 20 years and is considering retirement, according to bombshell reports on Tuesday morning. The NBA superstar made headlines with his comments after the Lakers were swept by the Nuggets in the Western Conference Finals, with the legendary forward admitting 'I got a lot to think about'. And now Bleacher Report's Chris Haynes reports that James is indeed considering calling it a day, and if he does carry on he is uncertain it will be with the Lakers. James still has two years and $97million remaining on his current contract, but the final year is a player option and he could opt to forgo the cash and call it a day. Speaking after the Lakers' defeat, which saw them bomb out 4-0 to the Nuggets, James said: 'I got a lot to think about. Just personally, with me moving forward with the game of basketball , I got a lot to think about.' If he did call it quits, LeBron would leave the court with the all-time scoring record having overtaken Kareem Abdul-Jabbar earlier this season. He would also lead the playoff scoring records, having opened a 2,036-point gap between himself and nearest rival Michael Jordan. James has played 20 seasons in the NBA, starting with seven years in Cleveland after he was drafted first overall in 2003 by the Cavaliers. He then went on to the Miami Heat, where he won his first two NBA titles, before heading back to Cleveland to win his third. In 2018, he moved to the Lakers and won his fourth ring in a Covid-affected 2020. In addition to the titles, he also has four NBA Finals MVP awards, and is a four-time NBA MVP. At the age of 38, retirement is certainly looming, but it would come as something of a surprise for LeBron to call it a day now, with his son Bronny soon to be heading into the league. LeBron has previously made it very clear that he wishes to play alongside his eldest son, who recently committed to playing college basketball for USC. The absence of LeBron James from the Lakers' offices, the only player on the Los Angeles team to miss the end-of-season interview with coach Darvin Ham and manager Rob Pelinka , has not helped mitigate rumors of his retirement. "We'll wait for those conversations when the time is right. But I will say this: LeBron has given as much to the game of basketball as anyone. And when you do that, you earn the right to decide if you're going to give more," explained Rob Pelinka, general manager of the Lakers. I think sometimes we put athletes or entertainers on a pedestal, but they're human just like us. Rob Pelinka "I think sometimes we put athletes or entertainers on a pedestal, but they're human just like us. They have turning points in their career, and our job as Lakers is to support any player on our team if they reach a turning point in their career. Obviously, our hope would be that his career continues, but we want to give him the time again," he reflected. LeBron James and Anthony Davis as two pillars is, for us, an unmatched combination that we will continue to lean on and build upon. Rob Pelinka What Pelinka is very clear about is that " LeBron James and Anthony Davis as two pillars is, for us, an unmatched combination that we will continue to lean on and build upon. We're proud of that superstar combination and we want to continue to invest in that and invest in advancing the growth that we had this year into next season." fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Even before LeBron James brought a soon-to-be career end into play, Anthony Davis sat frustrated and shaking his head in his chair in the Los Angeles Lakers' locker room. Superstar James made the first hints about his future shortly afterwards, national team captain Dennis Schr\'f6der also spoke about the prospects on the other side of the room after the playoff exit against the Denver Nuggets. Four losses in four games to the Denver Nuggets ended the Los Angeles Lakers' season in the Western Conference Finals. The title in the best basketball league in the world will probably be determined by the Nuggets around their strong leader Nikola Jokic in a duel with the Miami Heat, who already lead 3-0 in their series against the Boston Celtics. And as if all that wasn't frustration and sadness enough for all fans of the league's glamour club, James just opened that one barrel his supporters fear. After much praise for the strong performance of the Nuggets around Jokic, who again hit the most impossible throws in the 113-111 in LA and thus helped his team to make up a 15-point deficit at the break already in the third quarter, James ended his talk with the media very thoughtfully - in the truest sense of the word. The question was "whether I want to keep playing," he said in a brief conversation with a reporter from the U.S. channel, according to ESPN. The journalist had asked James for more clarity on the latter's comments just minutes earlier. "At this point in my career, I'm just playing to win titles. I don't get a kick out of being in the Conference Finals. That's where I've been. A lot of times. I don't get a kick out of not being a part of getting to the Finals," the 38-year-old NBA point guard had said then. "We'll see what happens. There's a lot I have to think about. I don't. I have a lot to think about, if I'm honest. How basketball is going for me personally, there's a lot to think about. " James is under contract with the Lakers for at least one more season, and a second if he wants. Davis has a valid contract. Who will don the legendary jersey in yellow and purple alongside Jarred Vanderbildt and young Max Christie from the 2023/2024 squad that underwent a major overhaul in February, however, is unclear quite apart from James' musings. Austin Reaves, Rui Hachimura and D'Angelo Russell all played significant roles in the unexpectedly huge success of the record champions, who started the season with just two wins from 12 games and were now in the top four. But they all need a new contract and therefore probably cost more money - as does Schr\'f6der, who had many more fans behind him in his second stay in Los Angeles than he did the first time around in 2020/2021 and was an elementary part of the playoff successes with his tireless efforts on the defensive end. Because the Lakers, like all teams in the NBA, have a salary cap, it seems difficult to have all the relevant professionals back on board for the upcoming season. "That's up in the air. So I think both sides want, of course," Schr\'f6der said when asked if he had played his last game in the jersey of the record champion. Lakers coach Darvin Ham is like a family member to him, he said, and he knows, of course, "that I would always continue to play for him. No question about it," Schr\'f6der said. But the Lakers would first have to get a handle on the overall situation with the expiring contracts. "In the summer, when free agency happens, then my agent and adviser will talk to them. And then we'll see." So when and if Schr\'f6der, James and Davis will share a cubicle again is an open question. Disclaimer: Translation automatically generated. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Long before LeBron James donned the purple and gold en route to an NBA title and the league's all-time scoring record, he was introduced to the world in green and gold. The unprecedented hype that surrounded Mr. James in high school - and the bond with his teammates in Akron, Ohio, during his formative years - is chronicled in the movie "Shooting Stars." The film, streaming on Peacock starting June 2, is inspired by a coming-of-age memoir written by Mr. James and co-author Buzz Bissinger of "Friday Night Lights" fame. The look back at the trials and triumphs of the young player and his teammates is particularly compelling given recent news that Mr. James may be considering retirement. "I got a lot to think about," he said after his Los Angeles Lakers were recently eliminated in the Western Conference Finals by the Denver Nuggets. "Just personally, with me moving forward with the game of basketball , I got a lot to think about." Mr. James, perhaps not ironically, channeled Jay-Z on Instagram with another retirement hint earlier this week. "I'm supposed to be #1 on everybody list, we'll see what happens when I no longer exist," the basketball icon quoted on his Instagram story, a callback to Jay-Z's "The Black Album," which the rapper suggested at the time would be his last. He has since released five albums, and Mr. James has long suggested that he wants to continue long enough to play with his namesake son, "Bronny." The retirement hints are the musings of a man with a "championship or bust" mentality. It's a commentary that reflects the high stakes with which Mr. James continues to approach the game. This recent movie about his life shows us that those stakes were present long before his professional career. In what the memoir's summary describes as "challenges all too typical of inner city America," Mr. James had moved with his mother, Gloria, more than a dozen times by the time he turned 10. That sense of instability was also present in the life of his teammate, Willie McGee, who left his parents in Chicago to be raised by his older brother. The biopic does not handle those circumstances with kid gloves. Within the first few minutes of the movie, the actors depicting the young Mr. James, Mr. McGee, and the other members of the "Fab Five" fire off curse words. Those obscenities don't define the boys, nor their upbringing. They would eventually find a father figure in Dru Joyce, whose son, "Lil' Dru," plays a prominent role in the movie and on the team. The biopic does a good job of centering the players beyond their most famous standout. It is at its best when the risks the young people took are most apparent - from the younger Joyce's successful pitch to coach Keith Dambrot to have the group play together, to Mr. James' toeing the line between prep star and professional. It's hard to imagine now that his career as a basketball player might have ever been in question, but when Mr. James was ruled ineligible to play after he accepted two jerseys that cost less than $1,000, it cast a shadow over that surety. At the time, the jerseys and his mother's purchase of a $50,000 Hummer were filed under the umbrella of "scandal." While society still struggles with the earning potential and negotiating power of college athletes, much less top-flight high school players, it is fitting that "name, image, likeness" valuations of Mr. James' son, "Bronny," are in the millions of dollars. Yet the senior LeBron was "The Chosen One." Sports Illustrated gave him that name and the front cover, along with the burden of being Michael Jordan's "Air Apparent." Where that comparison devastated a host of young up-and-coming players, Mr. James has thrived under that pressure. "Shooting Stars" is a celebration of basketball savvy and the essence of sports: team. No modern-day player exemplifies the notion of team more than Mr. James. No one man could have beaten the odds when it came to poverty, or instability, or unrealistic expectations. Mr. James not only has risen to the top of his profession, but also has placed his children in the space to be basketball savants. Whether he chooses to retire this offseason or not, the past two decades of his very public upbringing and ascension have been remarkable. Mr. James and his colleagues took a deliberate approach to basketball that assured that their success was more than just a game of chance, but one of certainty. It is fitting that wherever Mr. James slipped on a jersey - whether in Akron, Cleveland, Miami, or Los Angeles - there has always been a hint of gold. Ken Makin is the host of the "Makin' a Difference" podcast. His younger brother, Bryce, is also a basketball player. His younger sister, Zhuri, is 8-years-old. At age 10, Bronny was already showing promise as a basketball player, earning media attention for his skill on the court. He played competitive basketball throughout middle and high school. After a short period of uncertainty, Hamlin rapidly recovered thanks to the efforts of the University of Cincinnati healthcare workers. "Prayers to Bronny & the James family as well," he wrote on Twitter. "Here for you guys just like you have been for me my entire process." fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Bronny James, 18, the eldest son of NBA superstar LeBron James , suffered cardiac arrest Monday during a practice with his University of Southern California (USC) team, his family said Tuesday. " The medical staff was able to treat Bronny and get him to the hospital. He is now stable and is no longer in the ICU," said a statement from the James family sent to U.S. media. "We ask for respect and privacy for the James family and will inform the media when there is more information," the statement highlighted. LeBron James of the Los Angeles Lakers and his wife Savannah "wish to publicly send their deepest thanks and appreciation to the USC athletic and medical staff for their incredible work and dedication to the safety of their athletes," the statement concluded. The pre-NBA jump Last May, Bronny James announced his commitment to UCLA for the upcoming season, which was expected to be his last before making the jump to the NBA . The talented and popular teenager, who has more than seven million followers on Instagram, was one of the biggest high school prospects in the United States and, when it was time for him to make the jump to college basketball , he had several offers from other top teams. Some analysts have included the forward as a possible top-10 pick in the 2024 NBA Draft . LeBron James ' intentions LeBron James , 38 , the NBA' s all-time leading scorer, has repeatedly expressed his intention to remain active until he can share a team with Bronny in the professional basketball league . Among the first figures to send his get-well wishes for Bronny was former Lakers legend Magic Johnson . "We are praying for LeBron and Savannah's son, Bronny , after hearing he suffered cardiac arrest. We are praying and hoping he makes a full and speedy recovery," Johnson wrote on Twitter . The following day, HYPERLINK https://www.tmz.com/2023/07/25/lebron-james-son-bronny-cardiac-arrest-usc-basketball-workout/ 00 TMZ reported on the incident, prompting a statement from James and his wife, Savannah, Bronny's mother. LeBron and Savannah wish to publicly send their deepest thanks and appreciation to the USC medical and athletic staff for their incredible work and dedication to the safety of their athletes, the James family said in a statement. The statement also said the family would provide updates on Bronny's condition as they learned more. Who is Bronny James? Bronny is James' eldest son. His younger brother, Bryce, is also a basketball player. His younger sister, Zhuri, is 8-years-old. At age 10, Bronny was already showing promise as a basketball player, earning media attention for his skill on the court. He played competitive basketball throughout middle and high school. He led the Sierra Canyon High School basketball team in his senior year, and played alongside his younger brother, Bryce. After high school, Bronny committed to play for the University of South Carolina Trojans during his freshman year of college. Basketball analysts believe he will likely be drafted into the NBA within a few years. James Sr has made it clear he desperately wants to compete either alongside or against his son once he reaches the NBA. I need to be on the floor with my boy, I got to be on the floor with Bronny, James Sr told ESPN in January. Either in the same uniform or a matchup against him. But I would love to do the whole Ken Griffey Sr and Jr thing. That would be ideal for sure. Support and conspiracies Social media flooded with support for Bronny in the wake of his health scare, especially among professional athletes. Damar Hamlin of the Buffalo Bills offered words of support and encouragement for the young basketball player on social media. Hamlin was especially sympathetic, as he also suffered a cardiac incident in January. After a short period of uncertainty, Hamlin rapidly recovered thanks to the efforts of the University of Cincinnati healthcare workers. "Prayers to Bronny & the James family as well," he wrote on Twitter. "Here for you guys just like you have been for me my entire process." Prayers to Bronny & The James family as well. here for you guys just like you have been for me my entire process. However, not all who commented on Bronny's situation were offering well-wishes. Many, including the owner of Twitter, now apparently called "X" Elon Musk, made conspiratorial comments linking Bronny's health scare with the Covid-19 vaccine. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 India, July 27 -- NBA fans are yet to hear from LeBron James after his eldest son Bronny suffered a cardiac arrest during a training session on Monday. The James family did release an official statement saying that Bronny is in "stable condition" but LeBron has not spoken following his son's health scare. DailyMail has now reported on LeBron's current mental condition after Bronny's cardiac arrest. The report quoted a source close to the James family saying that the legendary NBA player is "scared and devastated" by what happened with his 18-year-old son. "LeBron is keeping things as close to himself and his family as possible but is also keeping others like his agents and main people in the Lakers organisation privy to what is going on. He is scared and devastated by what has transpired with his son. The family is making sure he gets the best care possible as his well-being is the most important thing right now. LeBron's only focus is devoted to Bronny's recovery. It is going to be the only thing on his mind, he is in full dad mode and protecting his son in any way he can do that," the source close to the James family was quoted as saying by DailyMail. Bronny James, a University of Southern California (USC) freshman, collapsed on the court after suffering a cardiac arrest during a summer workout at Galen Center in Los Angeles. The teenager was then rushed to a hospital and admitted to the ICU. Cardiologists later reportedly conveyed that Bronny's career, which is still very much at a nascent stage at this point in time, could very well be over due to health issues. New York-based sports cardiologist Dr Christopher Tanayan shared the shocking piece of information with British daily. "If they find something that puts him at a high risk for sudden death then that ... may make or break his career. Whether or not Bronny's career can continue will depend on his family, the league and ultimately his own appetite for risk," the doctor revealed. Rated as a four-star recruit, Bronny James came up with a stunning display in a McDonald's All-American Game in March earlier this year. Playing 20 minutes for the West team, he finished with 15 points and four assists. The 6-foot-3 combo guard is dubbed by many as one of the most talented young basketball players currently hovering in the circuit. The USC had officially announced Bronny's signing in May this year. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James Jr. s release was one of several indications that things were returning to normal three days after he collapsed on a basketball court. Three days after LeBron James Jr. collapsed during a basketball practice, a cardiologist at Cedars-Sinai Medical Center in Los Angeles said on Thursday that he has been discharged home, where he is resting. His father, LeBron James , said on social media that his family was together, safe and healthy. And in a signal of the family s optimism and relief, LeBron was back in the gym on Thursday, working out with another young basketball star. The younger James, known as Bronny, who was recruited to join the U.S.C. basketball team in the fall, suffered cardiac arrest on Monday while working out at the university s Galen Center. He was treated in intensive care initially and had been at Cedars-Sinai since Monday. The Los Angeles Fire Department on Thursday released a redacted portion of a 911 call made Monday morning from the Galen Center. In the call, an unidentified individual urgently requested medical assistance. Listen, listen, listen, listen, the caller said. Get an ambulance here now. A dispatcher asked the caller if a doctor or registered nurse was on the scene, and the caller said there was not a doctor around. Help is already on the way, the dispatcher said. We re already on the way, sir. Fire department, paramedics are already on the way. Bronny was treated by U.S.C. medical staff and then taken to Cedars-Sinai. LeBron James s workout with a young prospect had been scheduled for Wednesday before being postponed after Bronny, 18, collapsed during a practice. The young star whom LeBron James worked out with on Thursday was AJ Dybantsa, the highly touted 16-year-old from Brockton, Mass., who was in Los Angeles with his father to meet with agents, including representatives from James s agency, Klutch Sports. I felt for the man, Ace Dybantsa, AJ s father and a Boston University police officer, said of LeBron James by phone following his son s hourlong workout Thursday. After what he s been through, he showed up, put on a good face and worked with the kid. I had chills, man. For AJ Dybantsa, a 6-foot-8 forward who has transferred to Prolific Prep in Napa Valley, Calif., from St. Sebastian s in Needham, Mass., the workout was the latest in a series with N.B.A. superstars. When asked what it meant that James showed up after what happened to Bronny, AJ Dybantsa said, he cares. He continued, Didn t know what was going to happen to his son, but even though it took him an extra day, he still showed up and worked out. Bronny James and his family also received well wishes from Ohio State Coach Chris Holtmann. In September 2022, during Bronny s senior year at Sierra Canyon School in Los Angeles, Bronny and his father visited Ohio State for a football game against Notre Dame. The fans chanted, We want Bronny. Ohio State was the school that LeBron, an Ohio native, had said he likely would have attended had he gone to college rather than jumping straight to the N.B.A. as the No. 1 overall pick in the 2003 draft. Holtmann said that he and his staff had been genuinely interested in landing Bronny. It was a situation where we just were really open about making sure it fit what they were looking for and vice versa, Holtmann said by phone on Wednesday. We knew it was going to be an experience where he may not be in college very long, so you want that experience to be a great one. Bronny James would be eligible for the N.B.A. draft after his freshman year; there has been speculation that he will jump to the N.B.A. in 2024, when LeBron will turn 40. As a senior at Sierra Canyon, Bronny averaged 14 points, 5.5 rebounds, 2.4 assists and 1.8 steals. He was named to the McDonald s All-American game in March, where he scored 15 points on 5-of-8 shooting from the perimeter while adding 4 assists and 2 steals. The U.S.C. men s basketball team is scheduled to leave for a 10-day exhibition tour to Greece and Croatia on Aug. 5, but it remains unclear if the team will still travel, or if Bronny will make the trip. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The life of the James family suffered a drastic turn last Tuesday when the news broke that Bronny James, 18 , one of the sons of LeBron James , top NBA star in the United States, suffered a cardiac arrest in the middle of training. The Los Angeles Lakers player used his Twitter account on Thursday to reassure his son's health and thanked fans for their support. "I want to thank the countless people who send love and prayers to my family. We feel them and I am very grateful. Everyone is doing great. We have our family together, safe and healthy, and we feel their love. I'll have more to say when we're ready, but I wanted to tell everyone how much your support has meant to all of us! #JamesGang," the Akron native wrote. Bronny James suffered cardiac arrest Monday while practicing with the University of Southern California (USC) basketball team and was immediately transported to a hospital. "Yesterday, while practicing, Bronny James suffered a cardiac arrest," a spokesperson for the James family said in a terse statement. He continued, "The medical staff was able to treat Bronny and get him to the hospital. He is now stable and no longer in ICU. We ask for respect and privacy for the James family and will update the media when there is more information." Bronny James' family, the hospital where he is being treated and the university he plays for refrained Wednesday from making any new public comments about his health , two days after the 18-year-old son of LeBron James suffered cardiac arrest during a basketball practice. A group of photographers spotted LeBron James as he was being admitted to Cedars-Sinai Medical Center, where Bronny is receiving care after what happened Monday morning during practice with his team at the University of Southern California. Bronny James was in stable condition Tuesday, and patients who experience an unlikely cardiac arrest typically undergo multiple tests over days to determine the cause. The young man spent only a brief period in intensive care - a positive sign for his long-term recovery from the chilling episode. Bryce James, 16, Bronny's brother , posted a photo of the two on his Instagram account, along with a heart. Bryce James is also a standout basketball prospect who has received Division I scholarship offers. It is too early to tell how Bronny's episode will affect his basketball career, as much of that depends on the underlying causes of the stoppage. LeBron James has actively supported the dreams of his two sons, both of whom want to be basketball players. He has even taken advantage of his days off during the Los Angeles Lakers' season to fly several hours to attend their games. The NBA's all-time leading scorer and four-time league champion has repeatedly expressed his intention to remain active until he can share a team with his son. Bronny will be eligible for the 2024 NBA Draft . In October of that year, when the season starts, he will turn 20 years old while his father will turn 40 that December. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Bronny was discharged on Thursday after suffering a cardiac arrest on Monday LeBron had to put the workout back a day but found time to help AJ Dybantsa DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news LeBron James is back in the gym following a scary few days for the basketball star. His world was turned upside down on Monday when his son, Bronny James, suffered a cardiac arrest at practice and was rushed into hospital. The 18-year-old was treated in intensive care in Cedars-Sinai before eventually returning home on Thursday as he and his family progress with a prognosis. Footage shared on Thursday showed LeBron working out with prodigy AJ Dybantsa outside LA on the same day Bronny was discharged as he looked to return to as much normality as possibly following the week's events. He also thanked supporters for sending their thoughts and positive messages following the events. He wrote on his Twitter account: 'I want to thank the countless people sending my family love and prayers. 'We feel you and I'm so grateful. Everyone doing great. We have our family together, safe and healthy, and we feel your love. Will have more to say when we're ready but I wanted to tell everyone how much your support has meant to all of us! #JamesGang.' The New York Times reported that Dybantsa is in Los Angeles with his father to meet agents as he looks to progress his career. The prodigy reportedly met with representatives from James' agency, Klutch Sports, before working out with the NBA legend. The workout was originally meant to take place on Wednesday, but was put back a day after Bronny's cardiac arrest. And Dybantsa thanked LeBron for agreeing to meet him despite the emergency off the court. 'Didn't know what was going to happen to his son, but even though it took him an extra day, he still showed up and worked out,' the Brockton, Mass. native said. 'I felt for the man [ Lebron James ],' Ace Dybantsa, AJ's father added. 'After what he's been through, he showed up, put on a good face and worked with the kid. I had chills.' HYPERLINK https://thesportsinstitute.com/sudden-cardiac-arrest-know-the-danger/ 00 According to the University of Washington s Sports Institute , about 1 or 2 in every 100,000 young athletes experience a sudden cardiac arrest every year.Men are at higher risk than women, Black men most of all, and the riskiest sports appear to be football and basketball . For male basketballers at the top level of US college sports, the risk is 20 per 100,000 each year.In fact, sudden cardiac arrest is the leading medical cause of death for college athletes in the US, according to research from long before the Covid era. HYPERLINK https://www.jacc.org/doi/10.1016/j.jacc.2016.03.527?articleid=2513587#bib15 00 According to an NCAA report in 2016 , men s basketball and football players account for 50 per cent of sudden cardiac deaths in the NCAA, despite making up only 23 per cent of all male NCAA athletes.So, although scientists still don t really know why these cardiac arrests are more common for young athletes, the fact that they are more common is well established.James family thanks USC staff for saving Bronny s lifeWednesday 26 July 2023 18:30 , Ariana Baio Bronny James parents, LeBron and Savannah, issued a statement Tuesday thanking the University of South Carolina s medical and athletic staff for saving him after the 18-year-old experienced cardiac arrest. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 A post by LeBron James on social media has generated great joy among his followers because it shows his son Bronny in very good condition after suffering a cardiac arrest earlier this week. The NBA star filmed the 18-year-old playing the piano on Saturday, in what is one of his first appearances since his health issue. LeBron, Los Angeles Lakers star and all-time leading scorer in NBA history, posted a video showing Bronny playing the piano with his brother Bryce behind him. "Great promotion! God it's awesome! Bronny you're awesome Just like that, keep going young man! We're here with you every step of the way," LeBron wrote in the caption of the post. In the images, Bronny James shows off his piano skills and makes his father laugh, notably happy about the progress made by the youngster who collapsed Monday morning at a practice with the University of Southern California (USC) basketball team and was released from Cedars-Sinai Medical Center Tuesday night, according to a family spokesperson. While it had already been clear that Bronny James was in stable condition, the images released by LeBron have come as a pleasant surprise to NBA fans around the world. The youngster who joined this year's USC Trojans, a college team preparing for an exhibition tour of Greece and Croatia, also went out to dinner with his family this Friday around Santa Monica, California. It's not the first time LeBron's oldest son has been seen playing said musical instrument, as he once performed a cover of John Legend's "All of Me" after a big win with the Sierra Canyon collegiate team in December 2021. After less than 48 hours in the hospital and after spending his first moments in the Intensive Care Unit (ICU), LeBron James ' oldest son was medically discharged and sent home. Bronny went into cardiac arrest during a workout at the Galen Center, from where the 911 call was made, and received cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) before being loaded into the ambulance. His last medical statement reported that he arrived "fully conscious, neurologically intact and stable" at the hospital. Bronny is expected to undergo further medical tests in the coming days to investigate the cause of the cardiac arrest. It is still unknown if he will ever play basketball again, something that has fans on edge because LeBron James had insisted he hopes they can play together in the NBA. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 UPDATE: A family spokesperson has denied Mail Sport's report on Bronny DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news A spokesperson for LeBron James ' family has denied DailyMail.com's report that 18-year-old Bronny could decide to postpone his first semester at USC following his recent cardiac arrest. 'There is no truth to any report suggesting Bronny is considering delaying the start of his first semester of college,' the James family spokesperson told DailyMail.com. 'That is completely inaccurate and should not be reported.' A source previously told DailyMail.com that Bronnyand his family were considering postponing his first semester of classes and budding basketball career at USC as he continues to recover. The source emphasized that the family is following doctors' recommendations, while not ruling out a return to the hardwood for Bronny. 'LeBron and the rest of the family have talked about putting off Bronny's school requirements during this fall semester to allow Bronny to fully recover,' the source told DailyMail.com. 'They are also looking to see if he can take classes from home, so he can start school at the time he intended. 'To continue his recovery is the first mission to complete. Decisions are 50/50 right now on what they will all do next, and they will also continue to look into possibly having Bronny play basketball again. As for school, they have to make the decision within the next week, basketball dreams and if they continue are conversations for the future. It is whatever the doctors recommend right now.' Bronny was seen on Friday for the first time since being taken ill when LeBron and the James family went out for dinner at the celeb hotspot. LeBron arrived only with wife Savannah and their daughter Zhuri but the Lakers star left a few hours later alongside Bronny and his other son, Bryce. People quoted a source as saying that diners approached the family to offer their best wishes. 'LeBron kept expressing how grateful he is,' the source was quoted as telling People. 'It was of course wonderful to see Bronny after the frightening week. LeBron and the family are regular guests. Everyone was praying for Bronny's recovery.' The following day, LeBron shared video of Bronny playing piano at home and wrote as a caption on his Instagram post: 'GRAND RISING!! God Is Great. @bronny YOU ARE AMAZING!!! Simple as that! Keep going!!! We're here right with you every step of the way.' Bronny was rushed to the hospital on Monday after collapsing at USC's Galen Center, where he was practicing with the rest of the Trojans basketball squad. The teenager was admitted to intensive care before later being stabilized. He was eventually released from the hospital altogether on Thursday. Upon announcing that Bronny had been discharged, Cedars-Sinai Medical Center said Thursday in statement: 'Thanks to the swift and effective response by the USC athletics' medical staff, Bronny James was successfully treated for a sudden cardiac arrest. 'He arrived at Cedars-Sinai Medical Center fully conscious, neurologically intact and stable. 'Mr. James was cared for promptly by highly-trained staff and has been discharged home, where he is resting. 'Although his workup will be ongoing, we are hopeful for his continued progress and are encouraged by his response, resilience, and his family and community support.' LeBron, meanwhile, tweeted out a statement on Thursday thanking fans for the messages of support that his family have been sent. 'I want to thank the countless people sending my family love and prayers,' he wrote on Twitter. 'We feel you and I'm so grateful. Everyone doing great. We have our family together, safe and healthy, and we feel your love. 'Will have more to say when we're ready but I wanted to tell everyone how much your support has meant to all of us! #JamesGang.' fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The United States' disappointing fourth place finish in the basketball World Cup (losses to Lithuania, Germany and Canada) went deep inside the country and Team USA is already aiming at the next goal: the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. Qualified for the event for having finished the World Cup as the second best team in America, the Americans are looking to retain the gold medal won in Tokyo and a team with NBA legends is envisioned to avoid a new humiliation. LeBron James , the NBA's all-time leading scorer and star of the Los Angeles Lakers, is already planning a super team. The King wants to have his "last dance" with the U.S. national team and for that he wants to play in Paris 2024 surrounded by stars. According to an article published in The Athletic, the 38-year-old picked up his phone and spoke with Stephen Curry, Kevin Durant, Anthony Davis, Jayson Tatum and Draymond Green. The response is encouraging, as all are willing to commit to the cause. The rest of the names of the new Dream Team that LeBron is looking for are scorers Devin Booker (Phoenix Suns), Damian Lillard (Portland Trail Blazers), De'Aaron Fox (Sacramento Kings) and Kyrie Irving (Dallas Mavericks), and they would also like to join the team, according to U.S. league sources quoted by journalist Shams Charania. Another name being analyzed by Steve Kerr's coaching staff is that of Miami Heat center Bam Adebayo, Olympic champion in Tokyo 2021. James, who won two gold medals at the Beijing 2008 and London 2012 Olympic Games with the USA, is said to have started "recruiting" players before the FIBA World Cup and his intention to put together an all-star team was not subject to the results of the competition that took place in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan and that had Germany as champion. Meanwhile, the team's sporting director, Grant Hill, said he was aware of LeBron's move, although he did not comment on it. One of the points to consider is the age of the stars of the new "Dream Team" for the Olympic Games to be held from July 26 to August 11, 2024 in Paris. LeBron James would arrive at 39, Curry at 36, Durant at 35, Green at 34, Irving at 32 and Davis at 31. In the case of the Golden State Warriors three-pointer, it would be his first Olympic participation, although the Chef was already world champion in 2010 and 2014. For their part, Tatum, Green and Lillard were all gold medalists in Tokyo, and Durant was the MVP of that team. The other plan is to convince NBA MVP Joel Embiid to wear the U.S. jersey instead of France. The Cameroon-born Philadelphia 76ers pivot is a triple national and may be eligible to represent both his native country and either the North American or European country. The 29-year-old will likely choose to be part of Le Bleu to form a stellar trident with Rudy Gobert and Victor Wembanyama. Although in the great country of the North they dream of repeating the case of Paolo Banchero, the star they managed to take away from the Italian national team for the World Cup. Team USA does not want any more surprises and will work to maintain its hegemony in men's basketball at the Olympic Games, an event in which the U.S. has won 16 gold medals, four of them in the last four editions (2008, 2012, 2016 and 2021). fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James has previously outlined his desire to bring basketball to Las Vegas The Los Angeles Lakers star believes 'it's only right' that the NBA expands there DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news LeBron James has explained why he wants to own an NBA franchise in Las Vegas, believing it is only a 'matter of time' before big-time basketball lights up the Strip. The Lakers star is nearing the end of an incredible playing career and the four-time NBA champion has previously outlined his desire to move into ownership. The 38-year-old was this week asked why he is particularly interested in running a team in Las Vegas, should the NBA ever launch a franchise in the city. The NFL, the NHL and the WNBA have all brought teams to Vegas in recent years and James believes the NBA would only accelerate the 'momentum' building in the desert. 'It just makes sense,' James said. 'Obviously, you have the Raiders here. You have the Knights here. You have the Aces here. You got F1 coming very soon. All-star weekend has been there a few times. 'We have NBA Summer League that's very popular. Sports, it's here. I think adding an NBA franchise here would just add to the momentum that's going on in this town.' James has no concerns over whether an NBA franchise would sell in Vegas and believes it is a matter of when - rather than if - a team pops up there. 'It's an attraction. You don't have to worry about getting fan support or nothing like that. I think it's only right. I think it's only a matter of time,' he added. James has been in Vegas on preseason duty with the Lakers. They faced the Nets on Monday night, when Ben Simmons appeared to stare down the Los Angeles superstar in the Lakers' 129-126 win. Early in the first quarter at T-Mobile Arena, Simmons produced a burst of pace to drive past James and score the Nets' first points of the game. The 27-year-old then appeared to stare down his counterpart as they made their way back down the court. James played 17 minutes and ended his night with 10 points and five assists. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Brown and Smith both recorded receiving touchdowns during the win vs. Dallas Philadelphia won the game against their division rival 28-23 after a late stop DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news Philadelphia Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown continued his dominant season by breaking out a new touchdown celebration during the 28-23 win against the Dallas Cowboys. After scoring a four-yard touchdown in the late third quarter to give the Eagles a 28-17 lead, Brown tossed the ball to fellow wide out DeVonta Smith before the duo ran towards the goal post. Smith then lobbed the ball up to Brown as he jumped into the air and caught it as if performing an alley-oop on the basketball court. After tossing up the ball up to Brown, Smith ran with his arms out to seemingly recreate the iconic celebration made famous by Dwayne Wade and LeBron James back when the two NBA stars teamed together on the Miami Heat in 2010. The one notable difference between the two alley-oops was that Brown did not actually dunk the ball through the goal post and instead faked a layup before releasing his grip on the ball in order to avoid punishment. Had Brown finished the alley-oop he would have risked drawing a penalty and potential fine for the celebration. The NFL determines that dunking the ball through the goalposts and using a prop in a celebration could be determined as unsportsmanlike conduct due to the risk of dislodging the goal post and delaying the game while it is reset. Brown finished the game with seven catches for 66 yards to go along with the touchdowns. So far this season Brown has 60 receptions, 939 yards, and five receiving touchdowns. He sits second in the NFL in receiving yards behind only Miami Dolphins wide out Tyreek Hill. Smith on the other hand had three catches for 51 yards and a touchdown. For the entire season he has 57 catches for 482 yards and three touchdowns. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 HYPERLINK https://www.deseret.com/2023/3/8//has-bronny-james-committed 00 Bronny James could be returning to USC basketball soon, his father, LeBron James , told reporters Monday. The elder James said his son, who went into cardiac arrest this summer, could be cleared to play for the Trojans later this month, according to HYPERLINK https://www.espn.com/mens-college-basketball/story/_/id//bronny-james-aims-play-usc-passes-medical-exam 00 ESPN . How is Bronny James doing? LeBron James said that "things are going in the right direction" in regards to his son's health and return to basketball . "He's doing rehab," he said. "Every week he gets to do more and more and more. We have a big moment at the end of this month to see if we can continue to go forward. If he's cleared, we'll be not too long away from him being back on the floor and back with his teammates and practicing, with the notion of being back on the floor and playing in game situations." Bronny James was in attendance when his USC teammates beat Kansas State 82-69 Monday, HYPERLINK https://apnews.com/article/usc-trojans-bronny-james-lebron- 00 The Associated Press reported. He was seen taking shots prior to the start of the game. Why is Bronny James not playing basketball ? In July, Bronny James went into cardiac arrest during a USC basketball workout and was rushed to the hospital, as the HYPERLINK https://www.deseret.com/2023/7/25//bronny-james-lebrons-eldest-son-in-stable-condition-following-cardiac-arrest 00 Deseret News previously reported. He was discharged less than 48 hours later, according to HYPERLINK https://theathletic.com/4727412/2023/07/27/bronny-james-lebron-james-discharged-cardiac-arrest/ 00 The Athletic . A month later, the James family announced in a HYPERLINK https://twitter.com/ShamsCharania/status/804?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw%7Ctwcamp%5Etweetembed%7Ctwterm%5E804%7Ctwgr%5E%7Ctwcon%5Es1_&ref_url=https%3A%2F%2Ftheathletic.com%2F4806841%2F2023%2F08%2F25%2Fbronny-james-cardiac-arrest-cause%2F 00 statement that an "anatomically and functionally significant congenital heart defect" caused the cardiac arrest. The family also said the condition could and would be treated. In October, LeBron James revealed that his son underwent surgery after the incident but didn't reveal when or what surgery was performed, according to HYPERLINK https://sports.yahoo.com/lebron-james-reveals-bronny-had-surgery-after-cardiac-arrest-is-dedicating-lakers-season-to-his-son-7.html 00 Yahoo! Sports . He then dedicated his 21st season to his eldest son. What was Bronny James' congenital heart defect? The James family has not specified what congenital heart defect Bronny James was diagnosed with. The family has only said that it was an "anatomically and functionally significant congenital heart defect" that would be treated, per a statement. What is a congenital heart defect? Congenital heart defects are defects in the structure of the heart that a child is born with, according to the HYPERLINK https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/congenital-heart-defects-children/symptoms-causes/syc- 00 Mayo Clinic . The defects are usually detected and diagnosed early in a child's life, but less severe defects could lead to a later diagnosis. There are several types of congenital heart defects, and they occur while the heart and blood vessels develop during the first six weeks of pregnancy, the clinic said. When can Bronny James go to the NBA? LeBron James has said numerous times that he wants to play on an NBA court with his son, "either in the same uniform or a matchup against him," as he told HYPERLINK https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id//lakers-lebron-james-talks-pursuit-scoring-record-staying-great-38 00 ESPN in January. Bronny James graduated from Sierra Canyon High School earlier this year but couldn't join the league right out of high school like his father did because of the NBA's collective bargaining agreement, according to HYPERLINK https://theathletic.com/3608468/2022/09/19/nba-cba-draft-age-limit-eligibility/ 00 The Athletic . To be eligible for the draft, players must turn 19 the calendar year of the draft they're entering and one NBA season has to have passed since the player graduated high school. The earliest Bronny James will be eligible for the NBA draft is in 2024, but when he returns to the basketball court could affect his decision to enter the draft next year or to play a full season of college basketball . fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 NEW DELHI: The stage is set for the monumental Oui Play Classic as the Notre Dame Fighting Irish and the South Carolina Gamecocks prepare to clash in the inaugural encounter on Monday at 1 p.m. ET (7 p.m. local time). Notably, this historic showdown between two of the nation's top-10 women's college basketball programs marks the very first NCAA basketball game to grace the soil of the fashion capital of the world, Paris.In a nod to the Parisian culture, both teams, both proudly sponsored by Under Armour, are gracing the court in luxurious attire ahead of this highly anticipated contest."It is going to epitomize luxury, and I think that's what female athletes need," said Rich Fresh, the celebrated fashion designer responsible for these opulent uniforms. "A lot of times, a lot of that energy is geared primarily to male athletes, but I think that this is a great opportunity for us to not just empower future generations of female athletes but to actually congratulate our current female athletes."Indeed, the two teams, perched at the pinnacle of their game, deserve nothing but the best in terms of attire.For Fresh, this undertaking holds personal significance as he identifies "a lot of parallels" between his journey to prominence in the fashion industry and the ascent of women's athletics. He experienced his fair share of struggle and instability before eventually rising to prominence. Similarly, women's athletes have faced years of determination and the endeavor to be taken seriously on their path to success.Fresh saw a shared spirit of independence and self-determination between his approach to fashion and women's athletes' approach to sports. He believes they're not conforming to someone else's standards but playing the game on their own terms.In order to ensure the attire he created for the players aligns with their personalities and game, Fresh engaged with Notre Dame's and South Carolina's coaches and players. This personalized approach resonated with the athletes, making them feel like they are an integral part of the design process.The fashion maestro meticulously studied each school's style, color palette, and personality, melding his own creative concepts with Under Armour's brand identity and the essence of what each team represents. The outcome, as he described, is an ensemble that makes the wearers feel like champions, radiating luxury."These young ladies are warriors," Fresh expressed. "That's what they're going out to do, is to fight. And so I wanted to make sure that I put together some garments that look like Warriors should be wearing 'em. They look like superheroes."The confidence boost from sporting such exceptional attire is palpable, as players from both teams attested to the impact of looking and feeling great on the court. "Being able to express our swag definitely translates onto the court," noted Notre Dame sophomore KK Bransford, emphasizing the connection between confidence and performance.Fresh flew to Paris in anticipation of the game to present his exquisite creations to the teams. The response was electric, with both players and coaches expressing their excitement and appreciation for the attire. The enthusiasm was infectious and left an indelible mark on all those present.This groundbreaking collaboration has the potential to elevate the profile of women's college basketball , capturing the attention of a broader audience. The game's increasing popularity, coupled with notable endorsements from the likes of Stephen Curry and LeBron James , signifies a bright future for women's basketball .The Oui Play Classic transcends women's basketball , leaving an indelible mark on women's sports as a whole. By putting women at the forefront and celebrating their achievements, this collaboration heralds a new era in sports. Notre Dame senior forward Nat Marshall summed it up aptly, stating, "This event is such a monumental moment, not only for women's basketball but for women's sports as a whole." "A lot of times, a lot of that energy is geared primarily to male athletes, but I think that this is a great opportunity for us to not just empower future generations of female athletes but to actually congratulate our current female athletes."Indeed, the two teams, perched at the pinnacle of their game, deserve nothing but the best in terms of attire.For Fresh, this undertaking holds personal significance as he identifies "a lot of parallels" between his journey to prominence in the fashion industry and the ascent of women's athletics. He experienced his fair share of struggle and instability before eventually rising to prominence. Similarly, women's athletes have faced years of determination and the endeavor to be taken seriously on their path to success.Fresh saw a shared spirit of independence and self-determination between his approach to fashion and women's athletes' approach to sports. He believes they're not conforming to someone else's standards but playing the game on their own terms.In order to ensure the attire he created for the players aligns with their personalities and game, Fresh engaged with Notre Dame's and South Carolina's coaches and players. This personalized approach resonated with the athletes, making them feel like they are an integral part of the design process.The fashion maestro meticulously studied each school's style, color palette, and personality, melding his own creative concepts with Under Armour's brand identity and the essence of what each team represents. The outcome, as he described, is an ensemble that makes the wearers feel like champions, radiating luxury."These young ladies are warriors," Fresh expressed. "That's what they're going out to do, is to fight. And so I wanted to make sure that I put together some garments that look like Warriors should be wearing 'em. They look like superheroes."The confidence boost from sporting such exceptional attire is palpable, as players from both teams attested to the impact of looking and feeling great on the court. "Being able to express our swag definitely translates onto the court," noted Notre Dame sophomore KK Bransford, emphasizing the connection between confidence and performance.Fresh flew to Paris in anticipation of the game to present his exquisite creations to the teams. The response was electric, with both players and coaches expressing their excitement and appreciation for the attire. The enthusiasm was infectious and left an indelible mark on all those present.This groundbreaking collaboration has the potential to elevate the profile of women's college basketball , capturing the attention of a broader audience. The game's increasing popularity, coupled with notable endorsements from the likes of Stephen Curry and LeBron James , signifies a bright future for women's basketball .The Oui Play Classic transcends women's basketball , leaving an indelible mark on women's sports as a whole. By putting women at the forefront and celebrating their achievements, this collaboration heralds a new era in sports. Notre Dame senior forward Nat Marshall summed it up aptly, stating, "This event is such a monumental moment, not only for women's basketball but for women's sports as a whole." fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 OVACIONES EDITORIAL STAFF Photo: Reuters From his roots in Akron, Ohio, to becoming the NBA's all-time leading scorer, LeBron James has forged a career filled with success on and off the court. Saturday, Nov. 25, marks a significant milestone in his career as 'The King' opens his own museum, ' LeBron James ' Home Court,' a space dedicated not only to his athletic achievements but also to his humble beginnings and community impact. With a ceremony scheduled to coincide with the Lakers ' visit to the Cavaliers, where LeBron will play his former team, the museum promises to be a unique tribute to his hometown. This project, in gestation for two years, is the culmination of LeBron' s entrepreneurial efforts , reflecting his commitment not only to basketball but also to his community. A tribute to Akron's origins and community. The museum, part of the LeBron James Family Foundation, is located at the base of House 330 in downtown Akron, an institution that has been in operation since 2004, providing support to the community . Tickets, at a cost of $23 and free access for children under five, go towards reinvesting in the local community. READ ALSO: Historic! Jaime J\'e1quez Jr. becomes the Mexican with the most points in an NBA game with 22 against the Cavs. From his small apartment in SpringHill to his international success, the exhibit offers a multimedia tour of LeBron's life. It includes a recreation of the apartment where he grew up with his mother Gloria, iconic artifacts such as his 2003 draft suit, and memorabilia from his time at St. Vincent-St. Mary's High School. A museum that goes beyond basketball Michele Campbell, executive director of the Foundation, emphasizes that the museum is not only focused on basketball , but also displays the television where he watched games, the computer where he did his homework and his first trophies. In addition, achievements outside the NBA are displayed, such as Olympic golds and a replica of his office in Los Angeles, where he carries out business projects. YOU MAY BE INTERESTED: King of the clutch! LeBron James gave the win to the Lakers in the last second. The show recognizes the entrepreneurial success of LeBron, who recently became the first active basketball player to reach billionaire status . His savvy investments in 'Fenway Sports' Group , 'Blaze Pizza', 'Ladder', 'Wolves', among others, show that his talent for success is not limited to the court. LeBron James , a sports and business icon, has carried his hometown with him every step of his career. His museum, ' LeBron James ' Home Court', is more than a tribute to his accomplishments; it's a testament to how he turns everything he touches into gold, enriching both his life and the community that watched him grow up. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Maverick Carter, the longtime business partner and friend of Los Angeles Lakers superstar LeBron James , bet on NBA games with an illegal bookie, HYPERLINK https://www.washingtonpost.com/sports/2023/11/30/maverick-carter-bet-nba-games/ 00 The Washington Post reported Thursday after reviewing law enforcement records. Carter met with federal agents in 2021 as they investigated bookie Wayne Nix, a former minor league pitcher who pled guilty in 2022 to one count of conspiracy to operate an illegal gambling business and one count of subscribing to a false tax return, according to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. Nix's sentencing hearing is scheduled on March 6. The case also involves former MLB player Yasiel Puig, who is accused of lying to federal law enforcement investigating Nix s gambling operation. Puig has pled not guilty, and his trial is scheduled on Jan. 16. In 2021 and before 38 states and the District of Columbia legalized sports betting, Maverick Carter was interviewed a single time by federal law enforcement regarding their investigation into Wayne Nix, Carter s spokesperson, Adam Mendelsohn, said in a statement to the Post. HYPERLINK https://www.gannett-cdn.com/authoring/authoring-images/2023/11/30/USAT/007-usatsi-.jpg 00 Link to Image Carter told investigators he could not remember placing any bets on the Lakers, according to law enforcement records. Carter told investigators he made approximately 20 bets, each about $5,000-$10,000, on football and basketball games in a year. Basketball Hall of Famer Scottie Pippen also admitted to placing one bet with Nix in a separate investigative report, according to the Post. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Last July, Bronny James , LeBron's oldest son, made headlines after suffering a cardiac arrest while training with the University of South Carolina basketball team. Just five months after that scare, the 19-year-old returned to the court, making his college basketball debut against Long Beach State on Sunday. Under the watchful eye of his father -who enjoyed the game in the front row- the NBA legend's firstborn played 16 minutes, contributing 4 points, 3 rebounds, 2 assists, 2 steals and 1 block . However, that was not enough to prevent his team from losing 84-79 in overtime. The young James had a debut that lived up to expectations, in which he could be seen more than recovered physically and showed off with a three-pointer on the move. "I just want to say I'm thankful for everything ," Bronny said at a press conference after the game. The youngster thanked Mayo Clinic for helping him in his recovery, as well as his family, coaches and teammates for the company during such a difficult time. The point guard, who is considered one of the best players of his generation, suffered a cardiac arrest in July and the tests he underwent detected a congenital heart defect. Doctors considered the problem to be "anatomically and functionally significant" but treatable. For that reason, and after a long recovery process, Bronny was cleared to return to the courts. LeBron's emotional message after his son's debut After witnessing his son's debut in the front row, LeBron -who one day ago was crowned NBA Cup champion with the Lakers- made an emotional post on his Instagram account, where he showed all his pride and admiration for Bronny . "I can't even tell you how emotional today was for me! I'm literally exhausted and all I can say is: Bronny, you're just amazing! Damn the wins and losses that will happen. you've already won the ultimate championship and that's life! Proud of you little guy and you gave me more life today! Thank you and I love you ," wrote the star of the Los Angeles franchise on his social networks. The 38-year-old has already commented in the past on his interest in sharing court with his son during the end of his career. " My last year I will play with my son. Where Bronny is, I'll be there. I would do anything to play with my son for a year. It's not about the money ...," the four-time NBA champion said a few months ago. A day before Bronny's debut, LeBron was crowned champion of the inaugural NBA In-Season Tournament after beating the Indiana Pacers 123-109 in the tournament final. The King was chosen as the tournament's MVP and added a new title to his list of honors, which includes the record as the NBA's all-time leading scorer, four championship rings (2012, 2013, 2016 and 2020) and two Olympic golds (2008 and 2012). fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Jordan is widely regarded as the greatest player in basketball history by many But others believe LeBron has since surpassed the Bulls legend as the NBA GOAT DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news Legendary basketball coach Doc Rivers has weighed in on the Michael Jordan vs LeBron James debate - despite stressing that he doesn't like comparing the two. Jordan is considered the greatest player to ever grace the sport by many, having inspired the Chicago Bulls to six NBA championships during his iconic stint with the franchise. Yet just as the Bulls legend brought the curtain down on his career, LeBron then emerged as the apparent heir to his throne in 2003, with the Ohio-born star winning four NBA titles over the past two decades with the Cleveland Cavaliers, Miami Heat and LA Lakers. James is also the all-time leading scorer in NBA history with 39,440 points, while Jordan holds the record for both career regular season scoring average (30.1 points per game) and career playoff scoring average (33.4 points per game). Given the pair's dominance in their respective eras, basketball fans have not tired of comparing LeBron with Jordan over the years. And now Rivers has had his say. 'I hate the GOAT debate, [but] I'm a Michael guy,' the former Philadelphia 76ers coach said during an interview on Showtime Basketball with Kevin Garnett. 'But that doesn't mean anything bad about LeBron. Bron's gonna have the greatest career ever. 'There's no doubt he's going to have a GOAT career, but for me the single player is Mike. But that doesn't make LeBron bad. I feel like, if you say one, that means you're putting down the other one. They're both amazing. 'It's a fun debate, but I just hate that when you do it, people think you're putting down one guy or the other.' Rivers was most recently in charge of the 76ers before he was fired in the aftermath of their defeat by the Boston Celtics in game seven of the 2023 Eastern Conference semifinals. He previously guided the Celtics to the NBA Championship in 2008, while also enjoying spells with the Orlando Magic and LA Clippers. 'I don't know what I'm gonna do,' Rivers told TMZ after his Philadelphia departure back in July. 'I'm just gonna enjoy life.' LeBron is still going strong in the NBA at the age of 39, and he showed the passion for the game is still there last week. The Lakers icon exploded in fury after a last-gasp three pointer to tie their game vs. Minnesota was ruled out after officials said his foot was on the three-point line. LA trailed the Timberwolves by three points with just 2.3 seconds remaining when James' shot found its home. But the 39-year-old's birthday celebrations soon turned into rage after the officials ruled it only as a two-pointer, leaving the Wolves up 107-106. The play was reviewed, only for the call on the floor to stand. LeBron then spotted a replay of his shot on a courtside screen, which appeared to show his foot being on the three-point line by a razor-thin margin, although differing angles suggest otherwise. 'It's obviously a three - my foot is behind the line,' said James, who was almost ruled out of Saturday's game with COVID-19 illness. 'You can see the space between the front of my foot and the three point line. You can clearly see the wood on the floor - there's a space between the front of my foot and the three point line. Stevie Wonder can see that, champ!' fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 USA Basketball have revealed their 41-man player pool for the Paris Olympics LeBron James and Kevin Durant are both included b ut Draymond Green is not DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news LeBron James , Joel Embiid, Steph Curry and Kevin Durant were all included on the USA Basketball men's player pool for the Paris Olympics that was announced Tuesday. However Draymond Green, who helped the U.S. win gold at the 2016 Rio de Janeiro Games and then another title at the rescheduled Tokyo Olympics in 2021, was not among the 41 names released. Whoever makes the 12-member team that will compete in Paris this summer will be hunting a fifth consecutive gold medal for the USA. The naming of the pool is the first official phase in the process of assembling a 12-player Olympic roster that will be coached by Golden State's Steve Kerr and assisted by Miami's Erik Spoelstra, the Los Angeles Clippers' Tyronn Lue and Gonzaga's Mark Few. Team selection will be finalized this spring, with many of those decisions likely hinging upon player health and how deep their respective teams go in the NBA playoffs. 'The United States boasts unbelievable basketball talent and I am thrilled that many of the game's superstars have expressed interest in representing our country at the 2024 Olympic Summer Games,' men's national team managing director Grant Hill said. 'It is a privilege to select the team that will help us toward the goal of once again standing atop the Olympic podium. This challenging process will unfold over the next several months as we eagerly anticipate the start of national team activity.' The pool, which is subject to change, includes 13 players who already have Olympic gold medals - Durant has three, James and Chris Paul each have two, while Anthony Davis, Bam Adebayo, Damian Lillard, Devin Booker, James Harden, Jayson Tatum, Jimmy Butler, Jrue Holiday, Kyrie Irving and Paul George each have one. If he plays and the U.S. wins, Durant would be the first men's player with four basketball golds. 'I will play in the Olympics,' Durant adamantly said last fall at Phoenix's media day. The other 28 players on the list are headlined by a pair who may make their Olympic debuts - Embiid and Curry among them. Both said last year that they would like to play on the Paris-bound U.S. team, though again, nothing will be finalized until their NBA seasons end. 'Definitely want to be there,' Curry said last fall. 'Definitely want to be on the team.' Embiid - the reigning NBA MVP, two-time scoring champion and Philadelphia star who scored 70 points Monday night - could have decided to play for France (he has dual citizenship) or even Cameroon, his birthplace, if it qualifies. He chose to play for the U.S. last fall instead. Also picked for the pool are Jarrett Allen, Paolo Banchero, Desmond Bane, Scottie Barnes, Mikal Bridges, Jaylen Brown, Jalen Brunson, Alex Caruso, Anthony Edwards, De'Aaron Fox, Aaron Gordon, Tyrese Haliburton, Josh Hart, Tyler Herro, Chet Holmgren, Brandon Ingram, Jaren Jackson Jr., Cam Johnson, Walker Kessler, Kawhi Leonard, Donovan Mitchell, Bobby Portis, Austin Reaves, Duncan Robinson, Derrick White and Trae Young. 'We get to go to Paris. We get to go do something special,' said Adebayo, who got his first gold in 2021 and is expected to be part of the 2024 team. 'I reflect on it and I get excited because I get an opportunity to do something special - twice.' Green had expressed some interest last year in being part of the Paris-bound team. But the Golden State forward missed 16 games spanning mid-December to mid-January while serving what was originally called an indefinite suspension for striking Phoenix center Jusuf Nurkic in the face. Green said he considered retiring and sought counseling before he was reinstated. Players not in the pool aren't exactly ineligible to make the Olympic team; while it is a long shot, it has happened before. San Antonio's Keldon Johnson was not one of the 57 players USA Basketball announced in March 2021 as a pool member, but he was eventually selected for the Tokyo Games team and won a gold medal under Spurs coach Gregg Popovich. That said, it took an extraordinary set of circumstances for Johnson to be in that position. He was on the U.S. Select Team that practiced against the Olympic team to help it prepare for the Tokyo Games, got promoted to the national team in part because some players were still taking part in the NBA Finals, then got added to the Olympic roster (along with JaVale McGee) when Bradley Beal and Kevin Love - both of whom had been picked for the team - had to drop out late in the process. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James has found himself going viral for an unpredictable reason An old image of the Lakers star's foot has resurfaced across social media DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news LeBron James has gone viral on social media - but not for any heroics on the court with Los Angeles Lakers. The all-time NBA leading points scorer, who stands at 6ft 9', has instead been gaining attention because of the condition of his feet after one of the most fabled basketball careers ever. A picture of LeBron on vacation has resurfaced showing his toes bunched uncomfortably together, with his little toe appearing to point across his foot rather than upwards. Incredibly, the picture appears to have been taken over 10 years ago, with one report dated from 2013 remarking on the condition of LeBron's feet. Now age 39 and likely not retiring this season, the demands of the NBA has likely taken a far greater toll on his body. Lebron James currently has a picture of his feet going viral from all his years of playing professional basketball .pic.twitter.com/hVieTfKjzS- VIBES (@fashiotainment) February 6, 2024 NBA player Lebron James feet from years of playing professional basketball pic.twitter.com/DbJl9yBUjB- Creepy.org (@CreepyOrg) February 6, 2024 James manages his minutes carefully for the Lakers, who this season are 27-25 after Monday night's six-point win over the Charlotte Hornets. James played 40 minutes in that game, scoring 26 points just two nights after starring at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night in New York against the Knicks. He actually scored one point less that night, in the same amount of time, in an impressive victory for his team. The Lakers return to action Thursday on the road to NBA champions Denver Nuggets. A podiatrist told The Sun last year that James could be spending up to $7,000-a-month to keep his feet in good enough condition. 'For his feet generally, I would say that every month LeBron will be likely spending around $7,000 on podiatric care, orthotics, and custom sneakers,' they said. 'LeBron also has a complicated left foot and would need scalpel treatment to get the calluses removed and prevent ulcerations. 'Now there's rehabilitation for his right foot on top of that. That's a huge bill - adding up to $84,000 a year.' LeBron James is 39 years old, and 20 of them have been dedicated to playing professional basketball . But behind his successes, there are also costs to bear, such as the sneakers that fit his feet and that apparently have caused him problems. An old photograph of him on one of his vacations went viral on social networks. In it, you can see his right foot with a particular shape that would be a product of the continuous use of sneakers and the effort on the court. Photo: AP/Ashley Landis The photo of LeBron James ' foot that went viral on social networks. It has been more than ten years since the basketball star's feet began to attract attention. And it is that after two decades playing non-stop, it is common for athletes who are subjected to high physical demands, begin to have podiatric problems. After the photograph of his foot went viral, many people commented that this was "the price of greatness" and that it is "a side effect of a successful athlete". According to the British newspaper The Sun, a podiatrist claimed that the athlete spends US$7,000 ($6,600,000 Chilean pesos approximately) a month on "podiatric care, orthotics and customized shoes." HYPERLINK https://www.lacuarta.com/resizer/vGAzkqBqZ7pm_kX4lefdSbtlwbc=/1023x767/smart/cloudfront-us-east-1.images.arcpublishing.com/copesa/EIK55YFMBBCGVEKNTVZIWNC6YU.jpg 00 Link to Image "The price of greatness": the shocking photo of LeBron James ' foot after 20 years of playing in the NBA. "LeBron also has a complicated left foot and would need a scalpel treatment to remove calluses and prevent ulcerations," Marion Yau told the media outlet. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Stephen A. Smith criticized LeBron James for moaning about Bronny speculation The Lakers star criticized mock drafts talking about his son's arrival in the NBA READ MORE: LeBron James hits out at talk of son Bronny joining the NBA Stephen A. Smith has criticized LeBron James for complaining about speculation over when his son Bronny could join him in the NBA. The Los Angeles Lakers star took to social media on Monday night in a bit to ease the pressure on his 19-year-old son after mock drafts predicted Bronny would enter the NBA lottery in 2025. In now-deleted posts, James wrote on social media 'let the kid be a kid' but Smith has insisted the four-time NBA champion only has himself to blame for the spotlight. James has previously talked about wanting to play with his son in the NBA and it's also been reported in the past that the Lakers would 'explore the notion of adding Bronny' to keep LeBron in Los Angeles. Speaking on First Take on Tuesday, Smith said James is 'completely culpable' for anybody discussing his USC star son's future. 'When we get to Bronny James, that kid has done nothing. The kid has done nothing but work hard, try to get on his game and all this other stuff,' Smith said. 'Any attention that he's warranted has been because of his daddy - "I want to play with my son. I want to stay here until my son gets to the NBA". 'There's no crime in that whatsoever. You can want that. 'But when you're LeBron James and you put the word out that you want to be with him. And you put the word out that you want to play with him. And you put the word out that you'd be willing to go to any NBA team that picks him up, knowing the power of LeBron James , how influential he can be, now you've got to wonder what team would draft Bronny James just so they can get LeBron James . 'You're gonna tweet about folks needing to leave your son alone? You did that. 'LeBron is entirely and completely culpable for any kind of critique coming in Bronny's direction at this juncture.' On Monday, the Lakers star took to X to comment on the report from basketball news aggregator NBA Central. The post reported that Bronny, a freshman at USC, has been removed from ESPN's 2024 NBA mock draft and is now expected to enter the league next year. ESPN currently has Bronny projected to get picked at No 39 in the 2025 NBA Draft. 'Can yall please just let the kid be a kid and enjoy college basketball ,' James wrote in a post that has since been deleted. 'The work and results will ultimately do the talking no matter what he decides to do. If y'all don't know he doesn't care what a mock draft says, he just WORKS! Earned Not Given!' After reiterating his son's dedication to the sport, James commented on the little importance held by mock drafts. 'And to all the other kids out there striving to be great just keep your head down, blinders on and keep grinding,' he continued. 'These Mock Drafts doesn't matter one bit! I promise you! Only the WORK MATTERS!! Let's talk REAL BASKETBALL PEOPLE!'
