fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 NEW DELHI: LeBron James , a basketball legend in his own right, has reached another extraordinary milestone by surpassing 40,000 points in the NBA, further solidifying his place as one of the greatest players in the history of the sport. Achieving this feat on Saturday night, James extended the all-time scoring record he claimed from Kareem Abdul-Jabbar last season.James has now added approximately 1,700 points to his total, creating a significant gap between himself and Abdul-Jabbar's previous record of 38,387 points. This accomplishment raises discussions about whether James' NBA scoring record could join the ranks of seemingly unbreakable records, such as Cal Ripken's consecutive games played in baseball or Wayne Gretzky's point record in the NHL.Wilt Chamberlain's incredible season average of 50.4 points remains a record that no one has come close to matching. Even the highest full-season average by another player, Elgin Baylor's 38.3 points per game, would require almost 13 seasons of playing all 82 games to reach 40,000 points.Miami forward Kevin Love, a former teammate of James, echoed the sentiment last year, stating, "Nobody will ever, ever touch it." The sentiment is shared across the basketball community, recognizing the unparalleled combination of excellence and longevity that James has demonstrated throughout his career.Looking at the current NBA landscape, only seven active players have surpassed 20,000 points, with Kevin Durant being the closest to James, approximately 12,000 points behind. However, Durant, with a career average of 27.3 points per game, would need to maintain an exceptional level of play, essentially playing every game until the end of the 2028-29 season, to reach the 40,000-point mark.James' journey to 40,000 points is a testament to his commitment, skill, and longevity in the sport. As the NBA community celebrates this historic achievement, the discussion around records that seemed untouchable now includes LeBron James ' scoring record, underscoring his impact on the game and his lasting legacy in basketball history.(Inputs from agencies) For Reprint Rights: timescontent.com Classification_186 Classification_186 Classification fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Language: ENGLISH Publication-Type: Newspaper Subject: BASKETBALL (91%); ATHLETES (78%); ICE HOCKEY (77%); SPORTS & RECREATION (77%); BASEBALL (72%) Person: LEBRON JAMES (92%); KEVIN DURANT (79%) Geographic: NEW DELHI, INDIA (59%) Load-Date: March 4, 2024 fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor 0 lineWidth 12700 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 End of Document Page PAGE of NUMPAGES Magic Johnson calls LeBron James 'best in the NBA ' after meeting at NFL game HYPERLINK https://advance.lexis.com/api/document?collection=news&id=urn:contentItem:63NM-6MR1-JBJN-M3H3-00000-00&context=1519360 00 Magic Johnson calls HYPERLINK https://advance.lexis.com/api/document?collection=news&id=urn:contentItem:63NM-6MR1-JBJN-M3H3-00000-00&context=1519360 00 LeBron James HYPERLINK https://advance.lexis.com/api/document?collection=news&id=urn:contentItem:63NM-6MR1-JBJN-M3H3-00000-00&context=1519360 00 'best in the NBA' after meeting at NFL game CE Noticias Financieras English September 21, 2021 Tuesday Bookmark_189 Bookmark_189

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Chicago (USA), Apr 7 (EFE) - LeBron James , star of the Los Angeles Lakers, lashed out Sunday against the detractors of Caitlin Clark, the great figure of American women's college basketball , during the final lost by the Iowa player against South Carolina in the March Madness. "If you don't get your hopes up for Caitlin Clark's game you're just a hater!!!!!. Stay away from those people!!! Please," LeBron James wrote on X. Clark, an offensive prodigy called to mark a before and after in women's basketball , was playing her last NCAA game before making the jump to the WNBA, but crashed against a South Carolina Gamecocks that became the tenth undefeated college champions after finishing the course with a perfect 38-0. The Iowa player, who will likely be the Indiana Fever's first overall pick in the WNBA draft, was also celebrated by her opponents in Sunday's final. "I have to congratulate Iowa on their season and I want to personally thank Caitlin Clark for elevating our sport. She's carrying a great weight for our sport and it's not going to end in college basketball ," South Carolina coach Dawn Staley said after the final was over. "When she's the No. 1 pick in the WNBA draft, she's going to elevate that league as well. Caitlin Clark, if you're out there, you're one of the greatest in our sport and we thank you," Staley concluded. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Chicago (USA), Apr 7 (EFE) - LeBron James , star of the Los Angeles Lakers, lashed out Sunday against the detractors of Caitlin Clark, the great figure of American women's college basketball , during the final lost by the Iowa player against South Carolina in the March Madness. "If you don't get your hopes up for Caitlin Clark's game you're just a hater!!!!!. Stay away from those people!!! Please," LeBron James wrote on X. Clark, an offensive prodigy called to mark a before and after in women's basketball , was playing her last NCAA game before making the jump to the WNBA, but crashed against a South Carolina Gamecocks that became the tenth undefeated college champions after finishing the course with a perfect 38-0. The Iowa player, who will likely be the Indiana Fever's first overall pick in the WNBA draft, was also celebrated by her opponents in Sunday's final. "I have to congratulate Iowa on their season and I want to personally thank Caitlin Clark for elevating our sport. She's carrying a great weight for our sport and it's not going to end in college basketball ," South Carolina coach Dawn Staley said after the final was over. "When she's the No. 1 pick in the WNBA draft, she's going to elevate that league as well. Caitlin Clark, if you're out there, you're one of the greatest in our sport and we thank you," Staley concluded. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 "The Cleveland Cavaliers secured their biggest win in history Thursday night by winning the draft lottery and earning the right to select James, the 18-year-old high school phenom from Akron," said an article in ESPN in May 2003. Months later, the young James, LeBron James , stepped onto the court to forever mark not only American basketball , but world basketball . After 21 years, he continues to write new chapters, contradicting predictions and exuding longevity. In the final stretch of another NBA season, the 39-year-old star even admits that his career is coming to an end. But his achievements on the court continue to place him in an increasingly noble position in basketball eternity. In February last year, the star, now with the Los Angeles Lakers , had already become the NBA's all-time leading scorer, surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar. A few weeks later, he broke Oscar Schmidt's record and became the highest scorer in the history of the sport, surpassing Santa Claus' 49,737. - I'm not going to (keep playing) for long. I'm already on the other side of the mountain, the downhill side. I won't be playing for another 21 years, that's for sure. And it won't take me that long (to stop). I won't know how or when this door will close until I retire. But I don't have long to go," he said cryptically after scoring 40 points against the Brooklyn Nets on the 1st. Among the 21 seasons he has played, LeBron is close to closing his 20th with an average of at least 25 points. Not even the likes of Michael Jordan, Kobe Bryant and Kevin Durant (still active, with 15) have managed that. The number on his back has only changed, at times, from 23 to 6. His CV includes three franchises: in addition to his two stints with the Cavaliers and the current one with the Lakers, he represented the Miami Heat. In these two decades in the league, LeBron has transformed himself physically and changed his game - from the first half of his career, when he was a monster in terms of physical explosion and attacking the basket, to the second, when he refined his fundamentals even more and became a technical reference in every team he played for. He was a point guard, a wing, a shooting guard and even a power forward (briefly, now with the Lakers). At the start of his career, he returned to the perimeter as a point guard. Today, he doesn't exactly have a fixed position, although he plays closer to the basket. LeBron is the record holder for points in the regular season and playoffs and the fourth highest for assists in league history. He is also the only player with a "triple-double" in the thousands in points, assists and rebounds. Since he began his professional career in the 2003/04 season, the star has scored 0.78% of the NBA's points. The figure may not seem impressive, but in a league that now has more than 500 players per season, for every 128 points scored (less than a game's total, on average), LeBron has scored one. While he continued to maintain his high standards, No. 23 saw several stars and great players who had been selected in his draft or later begin and end their careers. Names like former Heat teammates Chris Bosh and Dwyane Wade, friend Carmelo Anthony and important players like Andre Iguodala, LaMarcus Aldridge, Rajon Rondo, Joakim Noah and Marc Gasol. There have been several extraordinary feats on the court: from Game 5 of the Eastern Finals, which he "single-handedly won" for the Cavaliers against the Detroit Pistons in 2007, at just 22 years old, to the three finals and two titles with an overwhelming Miami, culminating in his triumphant return to Cleveland. There, he gave the franchise its first title in 2016, turning a 3-1 series lead and upsetting the Golden State Warriors' hegemony in an incredible rivalry with his friend and fellow Akron (Ohio) native Stephen Curry - a title marked by his historic touch on Iguodala. As if that wasn't enough, LeBron also led the Lakers to the 2019 title, which brought the Los Angeles franchise level on wins with the Boston Celtics, the all-time champions with 17. In December, he also won the newly created In-Season Tournament. In 2024, the star has a few missions. The first is to reach the playoffs for the 17th time. He'll have to get through the play-in game against the New Orleans Pelicans tomorrow. The second is to compete in the Paris 2024 Olympic Games, where he must lead a group of league stars. And with his son Bronny declaring for the next draft, scheduled for June, he's also close to becoming the first athlete to play alongside or against his own son on NBA courts, a personal goal. - It's been a fantastic journey. Every year comes with a new roller coaster, and we spend a lot of time on those roller coasters. Lots of high points, lots of loops, high speed, and you come out exhilarated, wanting to go again. That's what my career has been about. A few punches in the stomach, excitement, scre

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Kawhi Leonard and LA Clippers will face the Dallas Mavericks in the playoffs Leonard has been sidelined since March with a right knee inflammation DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news Kawhi Leonard completed Team USA's 12-man roster for the upcoming Summer Olympics in Paris. On Monday, ESPN reported that the United States finalized 11 of the 12 roster spots. Less than 24 hours later, The Athletic reported that the LA Clippers star will complete the team, per sources. According to ESPN's Adrian Wojnarowski: 'USA Basketball Managing Director Grant Hill met with most of the 12 invitees on the 2024 Olympic team over the past few days, and it wasn't until today that Team USA officials were able to firm up Kawhi Leonard's acceptance to join the roster for Paris.' Leonard will join fellow superstars LeBron James , Kevin Durant, Stephen Curry, and Joel Embiid in spearheading the Stars and Stripes. Guards Jayson Tatum, Devin Booker, Jrue Holiday, Tyrese Haliburton, and Anthony Edwards have also committed to international duty. Meanwhile, bigs Bam Adebayo and James' Lakers running partner, Anthony Davis, will help bolster the front court for Team USA. The news of Leonard's selection came as fans campaigned and expected New York Knicks star Jalen Brunson to get picked for the final roster spot. Brunson made an immediate impact after joining the Orange and Blue last season. With a new cast of teammates, Brunson elevated his game to new heights this year and gave New York one of their best seasons in recent memory, securing the second seed in the Eastern Conference. The former Villanova star averaged 28.7 points and 6.7 assists per game, putting him at 4th and 13th in those categories, respectively. Leonard was selected despite not playing a game for the Clippers since March 31. The forward's status remains a game-time decision with a right knee inflammation as the Clippers gear up to face the Dallas Mavericks in the first round of the playoffs. With the acquisition of James Harden, the Clippers - with Leonard at the forefront - finished the season with a 51-31 record and clinched the fourth seed out West. The Claw averaged 23.7 points, 6.1 rebounds, and 3.6 assists per game. Other notable names in the player pool released in January include Leonard's Clippers teammates, Harden and Paul George, Damian Lillard, Donovan Mitchell, and Jimmy Butler. Among them are Stephen Curry, LeBron James , Kevin Durant and Jayson Tatum. The United States wants to continue dominating basketball worldwide. After the team's fourth-place finish at the World Cup, for the Olympic Games they will seek to regain the throne and win their fifth consecutive gold medal (Beijing 2008, London 2012, Rio de Janeiro 2014 and Tokyo 2020). That is why Golden State Warriors coach Steve Kerr announced 11 of the 12 players who will be at the next Olympic Games in Paris. Of the 41 pre-selected, only 12 players will be eligible and are already on the list: Stephen Curry, LeBron James , Kevin Durant, Jayson Tatum, Joel Embiid, Devin Booker, Tyrese Haliburton, Anthony Edwards, Jrue Holiday, Bam Adebayo and Anthony Davis. Perhaps the name that shines the brightest on this roster is LeBron James . The Los Angeles Lakers player, who will have to play in the NBA play-inn this season against the New Orleans Pelicans, would be playing his last Olympic Games. He already won gold at the Beijing and London Olympics and has a bronze medal after winning it in Athens 2004. Another name that dazzles is Joel Embiid. The Philadelphia 76ers player had among his plans to play for the French national team, but everything seems to indicate that he will finally represent the Stars and Stripes. The United States basketball team is the team that has won Olympic gold the most times in history (16 gold, one silver and two bronze) and with this team they will want to maintain that winning streak. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The United States made official the list of 12 players that will make up the basketball Dream Team that will seek the gold medal at the next Olympic Games in Paris 2024, which is headed by veteran LeBron James . Led by the NBA's all-time leading scorer, the Americans have assembled a roster full of stars, as is customary in each edition of the great event. Kevin Durant , Stephen Curry , Anthony Davis and Joel Embiid are also on the list of basketball players who will represent the United States in Paris 2024 . View this post on Instagram A post shared by USA Basketball (@usabasketball). Steve Kerr , who has won multiple titles as coach of the Golden State Warriors, is pleased with the makeup of the roster he will lead in Paris 2024 . "We plan to represent USA Basketball with the highest class and hope to make our country proud," the also former Chicago Bulls player in the 1990s said in this regard. All 12 members of the U.S. Dream Team have played in the NBA All-Star Game, and six of them have been champions and four have been MVP of the most important basketball league in the world. The 12 players of the U.S. Dream Team LeBron James (Los Angeles Lakers) Anthony Davis (Los Angeles Lakers) Joel Embiid (Philadelphia 76ers) Stephen Curry (Golden State Warriors) Kevin Durant (Phoenix Suns) Anthony Edwards (Minnesota Timberwolves) Jayson Tatum (Boston Celtics) Jrue Holiday (Boston Celtics) Bam Adebayo (Miami Heat) Devin Booker (Phoenix Suns) Kawhi Leonard (Los Angeles Clippers) Tyrese Haliburton (Indiana Pacers)? View this post on Instagram A post shared by USA Basketball (@usabasketball). Who will the Dream Team play against in Paris 2024? The United States is in Group C of the men's basketball tournament at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. The basketball tournament begins July 27 and concludes Aug. 10. The Dream Team makes its debut on July 28 against Serbia. Its second game will be against South Sudan on July 31 and on August 3 it will close the first phase against the winner of an Olympic qualifying tournament to be held in Puerto Rico. The quarterfinals will take place on August 6, the semifinals two days later and the grand final on August 10. Since the Dream Team led by Michael Jordan in Barcelona 1992, the United States has won Olympic gold in all editions of the event except for Athens 2004, when Argentina took first place. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Stephen Curry will make his Olympic debut for the USA after he was named on a 12-man roster for this summer's Olympics in Paris. LeBron James , the NBA all-time leading point scorer, will play at the Games for the first time since London 2012. Kevin Durant is seeking to make history by becoming the first male athlete to win four gold medals in basketball . Joel Embiid, the NBA's reigning Most Valuable Player, has also been included. The USA are aiming to improve on a disappointing fourth place at last year's Basketball World Cup. They have won 16 Olympic gold medals in the sport and have been the champions at each Games since 2004. Steve Kerr, of the Golden State Warriors , will be their head coach, assisted by Mark Few (Gonzaga University), Tyronn Lue (LA Clippers) and Erik Spoelstra (Miami Heat). USA B'BALL TEAM Bam Adebayo (Miami Heat), Devin Booker (Phoenix Suns), Stephen Curry (Golden State Warriors), Anthony Davis (Los Angeles Lakers), Kevin Durant (Phoenix Suns), Anthony Edwards (Minnesota Timberwolves), Joel Embiid (Philadelphia 76ers), Tyrese Haliburton (Indiana Pacers), Jrue Holiday (Boston Celtics), LeBron James (Los Angeles Lakers), Kawhi Leonard (LA Clippers), Jayson Tatum (Boston Celtics).

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James left systematic criticism behind him a long time ago. 20 seasons performing at his best in the elite of world basketball are enough to consider him one of the greatest ever. The Akron native has won four rings with three different teams and has also put his name at the top in terms of points by becoming the NBA's all-time leading scorer , surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar . A record that seemed unattainable until he arrived. LeBron James is still getting older but his physique continues to accompany him and he continues to be decisive, so it is a real unknown how far he will be able to go. With his Lakers far from being favorites in these playoffs, the triumph in the last In Season Tournament, the first in NBA history, and his more than likely (seeing the U.S. national team) third Olympic gold in Paris 2024 continue to fatten a record that is difficult to improve. Although LeBron has been accustomed to winning since he was in high school, with the fifth ring between his eyebrows as his main goal before his retirement, there is one award that has eluded him throughout his career. And that one was taken away from him by a Spaniard more than a decade ago. LeBron James 's disdain for Marc Gasol: "It doesn't make sense." In an interview with The Shop, LeBron James was asked if there was any award he hasn't won that still pained him, and the Lakers' James was very clear: "Yeah, Defensive Player of the Year . That's the only award I don't have in my house that kind of hurts . I've talked about this before. It doesn't make sense. The year I finished second for the award, the guy who won it wasn't even on the All-Defensive First Team." LeBron is referring to the 2012/2013 season, in which Spaniard Marc Gasol was considered the best defender and The King's Miami Heat won the ring, with the Akron native also taking home the season MVP. The defensive fifth chosen consisted of Mike Conley, LeBron James , Serge Ibaka, Joakim Noah and Tyson Chandler, but it did not include Marc Gasol , then in Memphis, who years later would share a locker room with the forward in the Los Angeles Lakers in 2020/2021. Unlike LeBron James , Michael Jordan did win the Defensive Player of the Year award in 1988, winning his first MVP that same season, when he had yet to win any of the six rings he ended up winning with the Chicago Bulls. LeBron James put the systematic criticism behind him a long time ago. 20 seasons performing at his best in the elite of world basketball are already enough argument to consider him one of the greatest there has ever been. The Akron native has won four rings with three different teams and has also put his name at the top in terms of points by becoming the NBA's all-time leading scorer , surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar . A record that seemed unattainable until he arrived. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 In the Spanish-speaking world of streaming and especially in the Spanish one, one of the 'giants' is Xokas . The Galician over the years has been making a niche for himself among the Twitch 'elite' thanks to his work and effort and above all to a unique and overwhelming personality that day after day leaves us incredible clips . Over the years, those of us who are aware of the current affairs of streamers and social networks have laughed many times thanks to the Galician 's videos, but the first great clip with which Xokas was on everyone's lips has been the 'this is not a game' . LeBron James ' statements that 'portray' Xokas In this viral clip Xokas left us phrases to remember as: "If you lose a game to a video game and do not get angry because you think you're a game you're a shit, you're a trash , you will not get anything in life, you're a loser" . But he also makes a reference to LeBron James : "Ask LeBron James if it's a game , he might slap you " . Precisely the Lakers player has made some statements that 'destroy' this argument of Xokas : "It's just basketball . At the end of the day, it's just basketball ." Streamers 'laugh' at Xokas These words of the NBA player of course have reached 'the hands' of other content creators who have taken the opportunity to make jokes as Spursito who put: "Pullita very ugly and direct to @elxokas". Ibai also reacted to LeBron' s words : "The Xokas right now" (next to the video in which a man drinks in the rain)". fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Today Xokas is one of the best known streamers in the Spanish-speaking world. The always controversial content creator is one of the most watched on Twitch and has behind him a huge social mass that follows him daily either live, Youtube, TikTok... However, Xokas did not always have this fame , he had to get it little by little, although there was certainly a moment that was an important 'boom' in his career. This was none other than the now mythical clip in which he says: "This is not a game, nothing is a game. You ask Lebron James if for him it is a game''. Lebron James ' statements Despite the fact that what happened with the Galician was already years ago, the matter has come to the fore again due to the words said by the basketball player in an interview. The American left some words that completely dismantled the arguments of Xokas. These were: "It's just basketball . At the end of the day it's just that, just basketball '', making it clear that it is a game and is not the most important thing in his life. These statements did not take long to start flooding the networks and of course the mentions in 'X' of the Galician streamer. There were many users who took the opportunity to rub his face in the clip, always in a humorous tone and without offensive purpose of course . Xokas responds to Lebron James Yesterday, the long-awaited reaction of Xokas to this viral video took place. He did it through his official account of 'X' and threw several sticks to the Los Angeles Lakers athlete: "Rome does not pay traitors. You're a bastard, at this point you come to dismantle me the chiringuito ...''. A response clearly in jest and that has aroused the laughter of a lot of people within the social network. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 HYPERLINK https://i0.wp.com/eltiempolatino.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/Diseno-sin-titulo-55-1.png?fit=800%2C550&ssl=1 00 HYPERLINK https://i0.wp.com/eltiempolatino.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/Diseno-sin-titulo-55-1.png?fit=800%2C550&ssl=1 00 LeBron James HYPERLINK https://i0.wp.com/eltiempolatino.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/Diseno-sin-titulo-55-1.png?fit=800%2C550&ssl=1 00 Lakers LeBron James announced that he is undecided about his NBA future following the Los Angeles Lakers' 108-106 loss in Game 5 of their first-round playoff series against the Denver Nuggets on Monday night. Fresh off this tough playoff loss, James quickly turned his attention to his personal life and his upcoming USA Basketball training camp commitments, preparing for the Paris Olympics. Doubts about LeBron James ' future During interactions with the press after the game, James showed doubts about his future plans. "To be honest, I don't have an answer yet. I don't really think about it too much," he revealed, adding that he would soon discuss his options with his agent, Rich Paul, and his family. When asked if this game could be his last in Lakers colors, LeBron James chose not to answer directly, adding to the uncertainty over whether he will pick up the $51.4 million player option with the Lakers for next season or test the free agency waters before June 29. The Lakers also face coaching uncertainties, with Darvin Ham's future as head coach hanging in the balance after this playoff exit. The future of the Lakers ESPN sources indicate that a resolution on Coach Ham's tenure is expected shortly. Reflecting on his tenure, Coach Ham expressed mixed feelings. "It was an incredible two-year journey, a lot of accomplishments, but ultimately, we chased the ultimate prize," he stated. Teammate Anthony Davis, still bound by a contract through the 2027-28 season and battling a shoulder injury to post 18 points and 15 rebounds during the game, showed his support for James' pending decision. Davis stressed the importance of giving James the space he needs to think about his path forward, while assuring that he would be among the first to know whatever decision James makes. As the Lakers and their fans navigate these trials, the NBA community watches eagerly. Decisions made in the coming days will not only influence the team's trajectory, but will also have a significant impact on the legacy of LeBron James , one of basketball 's titans. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 CALIFORNIA, U.S.A. - After the Los Angeles Lakers' painful loss to the Denver Nuggets in the season finale, all eyes are on the future of LeBron James in the NBA. According to The Athletic's sources, the Lakers are eager to retain the King and are willing to roll out a three-year, $164 million contract in the upcoming free agency. LeBron James is expected to sign a new 2 - 3 year contract with the Lakers this summer. - @wojespn pic.twitter.com/I5xIsEhYFe - The Laker Files (@LakerFiles) April 30, 2024 LeBron James ' Crucial Decision. After the Lakers' stumble against the Nuggets, LeBron James finds himself at a crossroads. Reports indicate that the Lakers are willing to offer him a contract that could keep him in Los Angeles for three more years. However, the basketball star has yet to make a final decision on his future in the league. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James and the Lakers lost to the Nuggets in the playoffs twice in a row James, 39, is entering his 22nd season in the NBA with four titles to his name DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news Kendrick Perkins insisted that LeBron James should retire to keep from further hurting his legacy and GOAT argument. James and the Lakers recently fell to the Denver Nuggets in the postseason for the second straight year. After getting swept by the defending champions in the Conference Finals last season, Los Angeles fell to Nikola Jokic and co in five games in the opening round this year. On Monday's edition of First Take, Perkins went on a tangent about how James is hurting his legacy by prolonging his career despite playing well for his age. 'At the end of the day, I hate to say it, but I believe this in my soul right now - I wish LeBron James would retire,' Perkins said. 'Not because I don't want to see more of LeBron James , not because I don't appreciate LeBron James ,' he continued. 'But I honestly believe deep down inside, the more LeBron James continues to play the game of basketball , the more it's going to hurt his legacy and the more it's going to continue to hurt his GOAT argument.' Since winning the NBA title in 2020, the Lakers have been eliminated in the first round twice and missed the postseason in 2022. With four titles under his belt, Perkins emphasized how expectations will hover over James as long as he plays. 'Long as he steps foot on that floor, every single season, there's gonna expectations of him winning a championship,' Perkins said. 'And when he don't do that, we're gonna be looking at him saying: 'Well what happened now?'' Additionally, the Lakers fired coach Darvin Ham after getting eliminated, as he took most of the blame for their losses. With names such as Ty Lue and JJ Reddick being thrown around to potentially fill the vacancy, Perkins alluded to how they can't cast blame on another coach if they fail to make the finals next season. 'So now you're looking at the Lakers, they're most likely going to re-sign LeBron James this upcoming season. If they don't make it to the Finals or the Western Conference Finals, who's going to be to blame then? I got a problem, man. And I'm telling you, I'm torn between the two.' fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 NBA teams were notified Monday that Bronny James, the son of star LeBron James , was medically cleared to play in the league and will be able to participate in the pre-draft combine, a person with knowledge of the matter told The Associated Press. In a memo sent early Monday, teams were made aware of the clearance, according to the person who spoke to the AP on condition he not be identified because the league did not comment publicly. All players who will attend the combine in Chicago must undergo a medical examination. The combine is an event in which college basketball 's top talent shows up to take various shooting and other skills tests prior to the draft. The medical clearance for Bronny James was somewhat expected, given that he was able to play the last 25 games of the season for the University of Southern California as a freshman. He suffered a cardiac arrest during a workout in July 2023 and had to undergo surgery to repair a congenital anomaly and missed several games while recovering. The NBA convened a panel of three doctors to review the medical history to determine if James was fit to play. They concluded that he is "medically fit to play basketball in the NBA," making him eligible to go to the combine. James is on the roster for Tuesday's games, although rosters and playing assignments usually change at the combine. All players are scheduled to appear before the media on Tuesday. The guard averaged 4.8 points, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 assists for USC last season. He has until month to decide whether to return to college basketball for at least another season. LeBron James - the leading scorer in NBA history and who with the Los Angeles Lakers just completed his 21st season in the league - has said several times over the years that he wishes he had his son as a teammate in the league. But at the end of the campaign, the star, who will be eligible to become a free agent this northern summer, indicated that "I haven't thought much recently" about the possibility of playing alongside Bronny James. "The kid has to decide what he wants to do. And I have to stop saying he's a kid - he's a young man who has to decide what I want to do. I think just having this conversation is great," LeBron James said after the team's elimination to Denver in the first round of the playoffs. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Everything seems to indicate that LeBron James will be able to achieve one of the few goals he has yet to achieve in the NBA, as Bronny, his eldest son, was selected by the Los Angeles Lakers with the 55th pick of the Draft. Although Bronny James was not one of the most talented prospects to follow during this edition of the NBA Draft, he grabbed the attention of the media because he is the son of one of the best players in the history of the league and it was rumored that the team that chose him could also get a deal to incorporate LeBron. Although the Los Angeles Lakers star has a player option in his contract and could negotiate with other franchises, he is expected to remain with the California team after Rob Pelinka selected his son in the draft. SportsNBA Finals: Pep Guardiola not only knows about soccer, he was key in Boston Celtics' championshipSportsNBA Finals: Celtics defeat Mavericks to win their 18th championshipSportsMexico City Game 2024: How to buy tickets for the official NBA game between Miami Heat and Washington Wizards? Here's Bronny James' journey before joining LeBron on the NBA's Lakers LeBron Raymone James Jr, better known as Bronny, was born on October 6, 2004 in Cleveland Ohio and is the eldest son of the marriage that LeBron James has with Savannah Brinson, whom he knew since he was in high school. Although Bronny James played several sports since he was a child, LeBron's eldest son decided to go deeper into basketball , the same sport in which his father is considered one of the best in history. SportsEl Gato Ortiz finally breaks his silence after the final; he will make Cruz Azul fans explodeSportsAlberto Garc\'eda Aspe blasts Chaco for defending Santiago Gim\'e9nez in the middle of the program; Shut the fuck up, he tells himSportsThe proof that nobody pays attention to Jaime Lozano in the Mexican National Team Although Bronny James is known mainly for being the son of one of the NBA legends, since he started playing for the Sierra Canyon High School team, videos of his best plays began to go viral and he quickly caught the attention of basketball fans. As it usually happens with athletes in the United States, Bronny's good performances at Sierra Canyon High School did not go unnoticed, and the University of Southern California (USC) offered him a scholarship. In addition to having played basketball at the high school level, LeBron's oldest son also played with several Amateur Athletic Union (AAU) teams. Cardiac arrest put Bronny James' career in doubt. While Bronny James was already with the USC Trojans, the eldest son of LeBron and Savannah suffered a cardiac arrest while working out in the gym. The college basketball player had to receive emergency treatment and was taken to a hospital to be stabilized. After several days in a California hospital, LeBron James ' family reported that Bronny had been diagnosed with a "congenital heart defect". However, they also mentioned that their eldest son was already resting at home and would follow a treatment to recover from the event and return to the court as soon as possible. Bronny was away from the courts for almost five months, so his debut in college basketball had to wait. With the USC Trojans, LeBron's oldest son played 25 games, in which he averaged 4.8 points, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 assists. LeBron James and Bronny would become the first father and son to play together in the NBA. LeBron James has achieved virtually everything a player in the NBA can aspire to achieve, however, he has expressed on several occasions that one thing he wants to do before he retires is to play with one of his sons. He even stated that the team that drafted Bronny would probably be the team on which he would retire. Possibly this was the reason why the Los Angeles Lakers did not hesitate to select the son of "The King" with their draft pick, since now it is very likely that LeBron will decide to extend his bond with the Californian franchise to share the court with his firstborn. Although LeBron is 39 years old, he is still one of the most decisive players in the league, so the Lakers would have used their draft pick to keep LBJ in their ranks. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Bronny James , the 19-year-old son of Los Angeles Lakers star LeBron James , was selected Thursday by his father's Los Angeles team with the 55th overall pick in the NBA Draft for next season. LeBron , a four-time NBA Most Valuable Player, has made it clear that he would like to play alongside his son next season , marking the first time in league history that a father-son combination has taken place. A guard at the University of Southern California , Bronny averaged 4.8 points, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 assists per game last season after shining in high school basketball . During a workout last July, Bronny, a freshman at USC, collapsed after suffering cardiac arrest brought on by congenital heart disease. The teenager was released from the health center a few days later and his medical team gave the green light for him to continue his basketball career . Bronny is considered a promising prospect because of his defensive play and athleticism , but he may need time to develop his game to compete in the world's biggest league. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The Los Angeles Lakers selected Bronny James with the 55th pick of the NBA draft yesterday, a major step in Lakers star LeBron James 's long-held dream of teaming up with his eldest son in the NBA. For the 19-year-old James, who spent one season at the University of Southern California, the selection in the second round of the draft completes a remarkable comeback from a sudden cardiac arrest he suffered during a July 2023 practice with the Trojans. Shortly after the incident, James's family announced he would pursue a return to basketball after consulting with several doctors, and he made his USC debut in December. "Not a feeling in the [f-ing] world better than being around raising my boys to MEN," LeBron James wrote on Thursday alongside an Instagram photo of him playing basketball with Bronny and Bryce, his 17-year-old son. "Pops over here getting emotional as hell just thinking about y'all journey so far! Thank you both for allowing me to be your DAD and be a part of your compass through life's trials and tribulations!" ESPN commentator Bob Myers said yesterday on the draft broadcast that James's agent, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul, had "a plan and a place" to land his client with the Lakers. "Rich Paul is calling teams [and saying] don't take Bronny James," Myers said early in the second round. "[Paul] is saying, 'If you take him, he's going to Australia.'" LeBron James , 39, has long hoped he and Bronny would become basketball 's version of Ken Griffey Sr and Ken Griffey Jr, who were teammates with MLB's Seattle Mariners in 1990 and 1991. LeBron James has been a regular presence at his son's USC, high school and AAU tournament games in recent years. Should LeBron and Bronny James take the court together, they will become the first father and son duo to accomplish that feat in NBA history. Bronny James, who first garnered national attention as a freshman at Sierra Canyon School north of Los Angeles, blossomed into a four-star recruit and one of the highest-earning name, image and likeness (NIL) athletes given his celebrity and massive social media followings. On the court, the 6-foot-2 guard averaged 4.8 points, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 assists in 25 games with USC, and he spent most of the season coming off the bench. James displayed his ability as a perimeter defender, but he wasn't utilized as a primary playmaker and shot just 26.7% on three-pointers. LeBron James 's contract with the Lakers includes a $54.1 million player option for the 2024-25 season, and the 21-year veteran has until tomorrow to decide whether he will exercise it. The 21-year veteran is reportedly seeking a multiyear extension. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The Los Angeles Lakers selected Bronny James with the 55th pick of the NBA draft yesterday, a major step in Lakers star LeBron James 's long-held dream of teaming up with his eldest son in the NBA. For the 19-year-old James, who spent one season at the University of Southern California, the selection in the second round of the draft completes a remarkable comeback from a sudden cardiac arrest he suffered during a July 2023 practice with the Trojans. Shortly after the incident, James's family announced he would pursue a return to basketball after consulting with several doctors, and he made his USC debut in December. "Not a feeling in the [f-ing] world better than being around raising my boys to MEN," LeBron James wrote on Thursday alongside an Instagram photo of him playing basketball with Bronny and Bryce, his 17-year-old son. "Pops over here getting emotional as hell just thinking about y'all journey so far! Thank you both for allowing me to be your DAD and be a part of your compass through life's trials and tribulations!" ESPN commentator Bob Myers said yesterday on the draft broadcast that James's agent, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul, had "a plan and a place" to land his client with the Lakers. "Rich Paul is calling teams [and saying] don't take Bronny James," Myers said early in the second round. "[Paul] is saying, 'If you take him, he's going to Australia.'" LeBron James , 39, has long hoped he and Bronny would become basketball 's version of Ken Griffey Sr and Ken Griffey Jr, who were teammates with MLB's Seattle Mariners in 1990 and 1991. LeBron James has been a regular presence at his son's USC, high school and AAU tournament games in recent years. Should LeBron and Bronny James take the court together, they will become the first father and son duo to accomplish that feat in NBA history. Bronny James, who first garnered national attention as a freshman at Sierra Canyon School north of Los Angeles, blossomed into a four-star recruit and one of the highest-earning name, image and likeness (NIL) athletes given his celebrity and massive social media followings. On the court, the 6-foot-2 guard averaged 4.8 points, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 assists in 25 games with USC, and he spent most of the season coming off the bench. James displayed his ability as a perimeter defender, but he wasn't utilized as a primary playmaker and shot just 26.7% on three-pointers. LeBron James 's contract with the Lakers includes a $54.1 million player option for the 2024-25 season, and the 21-year veteran has until tomorrow to decide whether he will exercise it. The 21-year veteran is reportedly seeking a multiyear extension. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Considered one of the best players in NBA history, basketball player Lebron James has broken several records throughout his career. One of his latest milestones was entering the list of millionaires as the first active player in the league. The Los Angeles Lakers player became the highest paid athlete in the world in 2022, so this would have earned him the figure of being a billionaire . Michael Jordan, for example, achieved this some time after leaving the league, thanks to the investment he had with the Charlotte Hornets . LeBron James is also astute in the business world, as he has invested in recent years part of his fortune in various fields, including soccer, and has been successful. According to Forbes magazine's latest list of the NBA's richest players, LeBron is in second place with u$s1.2 billion , second only to Jordan, with u$s1.7 billion . Lebron James ' business dealings The high-performance athlete has managed to stand out as an entrepreneur, becoming one of the main investors in Spring Hill . This business, valued at u$s3000 million, merges the Robot Company, the player's marketing agency, Spring Hill Entertainment and the Uninterrupted platform. LeBron also has a stake in sports through his association with Fenway Sports Group, leader of the consortium that forms Strategic Sports Group and owners of the Boston Red Sox, Liverpool FC and Pittsburgh Penguins of the NHL. In addition, this year, the basketball player ventured into golf with shares in PGA , the main U.S. men's professional golf circuit and the most important in the world. This investment took place after SSG injected around u$s 3,000 million in the PGA Tour Enterprises, a new entity in which almost 200 PGA Tour players will have shares. in order to improve the level of the major leagues of this sport. In 2022 he became the new investor in Milan , after one of his companies called RedBird acquired the Italian club. James has also shown interest in owning an NBA team if the league expands to Las Vegas. This additional investment in the PGA Tour allows him to deepen his ownership ranks. He also lurks in the food and beverage sector , with his shares in Pizza Blaze, and his Lobos 1707 tequila brand , a drink that has repeatedly won awards worldwide and is preparing its arrival in Dubai. Lebron James ' expensive tastes It is public knowledge that one of the favorite whims of the athlete are high-end cars . In his garage it has been possible to find a Rolls-Royce Phantom of u$s450,000, a Mercedes-Benz S63 AMG of almost u$s300,000 or a Ferrari 458 of just over u$s230,000. He also invested in impressive properties such as the 1,200 square meter property located on a hectare in Beverly Hills that belonged to Katharine Hepburn, to which he allocated u$s36.8 million. The sportsman also spends a significant amount of money on his physical well-being. It is estimated that every year he invests around u$s1.5 million in chefs, personal trainers and recovery therapies. As reported by ESPN on Wednesday, the contract includes a player option and a non-transfer clause, ensuring that James will remain a central figure on the Lakers' roster. LeBron James ' contract extension is not only significant for the veteran; it also opens up the possibility of playing alongside his son, Bronny James. The Lakers selected Bronny in the second round of the draft last week, igniting hopes for the first father-son duo in NBA history to play simultaneously. Did he make the best choice to stay with the Lakers? James made this decision after turning down the final year of his previous contract, valued at $51.4 million for the 2024-25 season. His agent, Rich Paul of Klutch Sports, revealed that James prioritized improving the Lakers' roster while negotiating the new deal. Despite being eligible for a three-year, $162 million maximum contract, discussions hinted that James could accept up to $1 million below the maximum to allow greater flexibility in building a competitive team around him. LeBron James ' NBA accomplishments. LeBron James will turn 40 in December and his accomplishments remain unparalleled. Universally recognized among the greatest players in basketball history, he is the only player to surpass 40,000 points in his NBA career. He ranks among the best in several categories, including eighth in assists, fourth in steals, sixth in three-pointers and third in free throws. Last season, James made history by becoming the first player with 20 All-Star selections, surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar. In addition, James holds the career postseason points record and ranks in the top ten in rebounds, assists and steals during playoff games. Prior to his time with the Lakers, James reached the NBA Finals for eight consecutive years with the Miami Heat and Cleveland Cavaliers, winning playoff series in 13 straight seasons. LeBron James now heads into his 22nd NBA season in 2024-25, tying Vince Carter for the most seasons played in league history. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The U. S. men's basketball team will field a star-studded NBA squad for the Olympics . A new 'Dream Team' will seek Olympic gold number 17 in basketball at Paris 2024. The U.S. squad features iconic figures such as LeBron James , Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant . Meanwhile, the coaching staff will be led by Steve Kerr and will be assisted by Erik Spoelstra and Tyronn Lue, all NBA champions. The focus will be on the stars on the court, led by LeBron James , who is seeking his third Olympic gold at the age of 40. In contrast, Kevin Durant, now with the Phoenix Suns, could become the first player to reach four consecutive gold medals. Stephen Curry, the greatest three-point shooter in history, will make his Olympic debut in Paris . The U.S. Dream Team The union of LeBron James , Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant evokes memories of the 1992 Dream Team and the 2008 Redeem Team. This team wants to redeem itself from the failure at the 2023 World Cup, where the U.S. could only finish fourth. The team will also feature Jayson Tatum, Jrue Holiday, Joel Embiid, Anthony Edwards and Tyrese Haliburton. The roster is rounded out by Anthony Davis, Bam Adebayo, Devin Booker and Kawhi Leonard. The United States has scheduled a series of friendlies beginning July 10 in Las Vegas against Canada. They will also face Australia, Serbia, South Sudan and Germany in their preparation for the Olympic Games . At the Olympic tournament, the U.S. could face host France and its young star Victor Wembanyama , a basketball prodigy. When does the U.S. make its debut? The U.S. men's basketball team is in Group C of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games basketball tournament. The new 'Dream Team' will debut in the Olympic tournament next Monday, July 29, against Japan. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 READ MORE: NBA stars Ivica Zubac and Dario Saric involved in nightclub brawl READ MORE: Anthony Edwards makes shocking claim about US basketball team In a room full of NBA superstars, it was a kid coming out of high school that stole all the attention at a practice for the US Olympic basketball team on Monday. 17-year-old Cooper Flagg showed off his skills against some of the best defenders in the NBA - including Anthony Davis, Jrue Holiday, and Bam Adebayo to name a few - in a stunning display that stole headlines. The inbound Duke freshman was part of the select team invited to work out against the Olympic squad during their training camp and Flagg already looked like he belonged. 'The opportunity, it was great,' Flagg said. 'It was a blessing to be here. 'I think just the physicality, and just the level of where I want to get to, there's a lot to get better at, a lot where I need to keep improving. This showed just how big the details are.' He will surely get better. That might be a scary concept. 'He kicked butt here,' U.S. select team coach Jamahl Mosley of the Orlando Magic said. 'There's a respect factor for what he's done. People who have not seen him play, as he gets himself going within the game, they quickly see what he can do.' The select team is typically made up of young NBA players, brought in to scrimmage the national team and help them prepare for either World Cup or Olympic competition. Flagg was the first college player asked to be part of the select team since Doug McDermott and Marcus Smart were asked to join a mini-camp in 2013. But those situations weren't anywhere near the same as Flagg being part of this camp. McDermott had played 110 college games for Creighton at that point and was a two-time consensus All-American. Smart was coming off a freshman season when he won Big 12 rookie of the year and player of the year honors at Oklahoma State. They were already proven. Flagg has zero college points. He still proved plenty in this camp. 'He wants it,' U.S. guard Devin Booker said. 'I know this experience, he's going to take with him and move forward.' In Monday's scrimmage - the Olympic team beat the select team 74-73 - Flagg was in the middle of everything down the stretch and looked perfectly comfortable. And when it was over, there were plenty of pictures with everyone; the select team's final day in camp was Monday, though some may be invited to stick around to continue assisting the Olympic squad. 'To be able to do what he did, not even playing a college game, let alone an NBA game, there's no fear,' select team forward Jaime Jaquez Jr. of the Miami Heat said. 'It's relentless. And the thing that you can tell about him is that he just has a knack and the will to win. 'He doesn't need the ball. He just finds a way to it. And the ball finds its way to him. That's something that you can't teach. He's just got a great feel for the game.' Flagg is a 6-foot-9 forward from Newport, Maine, but played his last three years of high school basketball at Montverde Academy in Florida. He chose Duke over Connecticut, was USA Basketball 's male athlete of the year in 2022 after leading the Americans to gold at the U17 World Cup, and was the Gatorade National Player of the Year, Naismith Player of the Year and a McDonald\'b4s All-American in his final high school season. 'I was shocked, I was surprised, and I was really excited for this opportunity,' Flagg said. 'And I'm just really blessed that I was able to come out and capitalize on it and show what I have. I was really grateful to come out and learn. 'That was the biggest thing for me, just being able to learn and grow, to share a gym with all of these great, great names. Legends. So, I'm just truly blessed.' fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Lebron James and Stephen Curry were at the top of the NBA at the same time, when the Cleveland Cavaliers and Golden State Warriors played in the finals four years in a row (2015-2018). The constant clashes created a rivalry between the two, who are now teammates on the US national team for the Olympic Games in Paris. In an interview with The Athletic, Curry spoke about his career, which often crossed paths with Lebron's, and the rivalry they developed. "It was like a healthy resentment towards someone who was getting in our way. But despite everything, there's obviously the utmost respect for who LeBron is as a person and as a player, for his quality and for the challenge of trying to beat him, of trying to solve that problem every year," said the 36-year-old Golden State Warriors star. Lebron James , in the same interview, returned the praise to the point guard. " Basketball doesn't last forever. You don't want to waste the opportunity to have a relationship with someone. Obviously we have to respect Steph as a person and as a player as much as possible", said the Los Angeles Lakers forward. Both understand that they have never hated each other and Lebron even spoke of older rivalries in the NBA. "Many of you may have grown up in the Bird-Magic era, but I also know that Isiah Thomas and Magic also hugged and kissed on the floor, because it was just mutual respect." The United States make their debut in the Olympic basketball tournament on July 28 against Serbia. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James saved the U.S. from what would have been a stunning loss. James' layup with 8 seconds left was the go-ahead basket Saturday, and the U.S. Olympic team that'll soon head to the Paris Games escaped with a 101-100 win over South Sudan in London, rallying from a 16-point deficit to avoid perhaps the biggest upset in the program's history. South Sudan, the African nation that gained its independence just 13 years ago and is about to play in the Olympics for the first time, led for more than half the game and had a chance to win at the end. But Carlik Jones' runner off the glass missed with about 4 seconds left, and the Americans survived. "I'm going to be honest: I like those better than the blowouts," James said as he walked off the court. "At least we get tested." Oh, they got tested by a team that came into the game as 43.5-point underdogs , according to BetMGM Sportsbook. "A good reminder that when we play against teams, it's the biggest game of their lives," U.S. coach Steve Kerr said. "We have to expect everyone to play like that." Down by 16 at one point, the Americans dug out of that hole with an 18-0 run in the second half then needed heroics at the end anyway. JT Thor's 3-pointer with 20 seconds left gave South Sudan a 100-99 lead, then the Americans called timeout and put the ball in James' hands. He made it look easy: He waited, waited, waited, then drove and laid it in with ease to put the U.S. back on top, and the Americans got the stop they needed at the end. James finished with a game-high 25 points, six rebounds and seven assists for the U.S., which improved to 4-0 with one game left Monday vs. World Cup champion Germany on its pre-Olympic exhibition tour. Anthony Davis added 15 points and 11 rebounds for the Americans. Marial Shayok had 24 points for South Sudan and Jones had a triple-double 15 points, 11 rebounds and 11 assists. The teams will meet in group play in the Paris Olympics on July 31. "It's basketball . It happens," U.S. guard Stephen Curry said. "You want to play perfect every game; it doesn't happen. But can you dig deep and do the little things that help you win? Offense can come and go, but defense is the thing that helps you win championships, medals, all that stuff." It should have been a mismatch, and for the first half, it was just not in the way anyone would have expected. The U.S. roster has 12 players, all of them All-Stars or NBA champions or both, with a total of 189,038 points in their regular-season careers, with 7,832 combined starts. South Sudan has four players who have appeared in an NBA game. They've scored a combined 1,228 points and started 19 games. Didn't matter. It was 8-0 U.S. after 2 1/2 minutes. The rest of the half: South Sudan 58, U.S. 34. The Americans allowed South Sudan to shoot 61% in the first half and got outscored 21-3 from 3-point range in the first 20 minutes. "I did not do a great job preparing our team," Kerr said. "We did not focus enough on what they're capable of, and that's on me. I think that really allowed South Sudan to gain confidence early. ... They were great. They played a wonderful game and the ending was good for us, just to feel that, to feel what it's going to be like in Paris and Lille." An 18-0 run in the second half James involved in most of it was what really saved the U.S., turning a 76-65 deficit into an 83-76 lead. South Sudan led by 16 late in the first half 58-42 before the Americans got the last basket to cut the deficit to 14 at the break. But the 18-0 run was key. James had four assists during the spurt and Curry, from about 35 feet, connected on a 3-pointer late in the third that gave the U.S. its first lead since the first quarter at 79-76. Wenyen Gabriel banked in a 3-pointer to get South Sudan within 85-84, but James his former teammate with the Los Angeles Lakers connected on a 3 on the ensuing U.S. possession, and the Americans would eventually pull through by the slimmest of margins. "Today we were able to represent our country with pride and we put up a good fight and showed the potential of our country," Gabriel said. "I think it was a proud moment for a lot of people." Davis said the team got to the arena late, thrown off by standstill London traffic, and that routines going into the game were disrupted. Maybe so, but South Sudan showed the U.S. that nobody at the Olympics will concede anything to the four-time defending gold medalists. "Doesn't matter when it comes to how close the game is," James said. "We went out there to get better." fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 On its way to the Paris 2024 Olympic Games, where it will defend its gold medal, the United States basketball team, with all its NBA stars, almost lost in the last second against South Sudan in a friendly played on Saturday in London. Steve Kerr's team won 101-100 in an incredible finish. The Americans were trailing 99-100 and, with 10 seconds left, LeBron James took over the offense. The Los Angeles Lakers forward filtered through the defenders and deposited the ball with a layup to give his team the lead. There were only 8 seconds left in the game. South Sudan called timeout and rebounded the ball from half court to make the last attack and have a chance to win the game against the powerful Dream Team. Carlick Jones got off Jrue Holiday's mark and tried a double near the rim, but missed. The rebound was given to Wenyen Gabriel, who missed the dunk in an unusual way. The 2.06-meter-tall power forward failed to put the ball inside the hoop twice and deprived the African team of the victory. "The end was good for us, to feel what it's going to be like in Paris, in Lille (the host city of basketball ). It was a good experience for us, a good reminder that when we play other teams, it's the greatest experience of their lives and we have to expect everyone to play like that," coach Kerr said after the game. The United States, 16-time Olympic gold medalists and champions of the last four editions, is the favorite to win first place again. In this penultimate friendly against South Sudan prior to Paris 2024 (they will play Germany on July 23), LeBron James (25 points, 6 rebounds and 7 assists), Anthony Davis (15) and Joel Embiid (14) starred. On the other hand, South Sudan, which had Marial Shayok as top scorer with 24 points, will have a rematch in Paris against the United States, as both share Group C (with Puerto Rico and Serbia) and will meet on July 31, but already for the points. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The Olympic Games are about to begin, and in basketball the great favorite is once again the United States , in a team that has many NBA stars such as LeBron James , Stephen Curry, Anthony Davis, Joel Embiid and Anthony Edwards. However, in their most recent friendly match they have left many doubts. On Saturday night, the United States faced South Sudan in London, in what was the fourth preparation game for the Americans before the Olympic Games in Paris. Although it was only a friendly, it served as a warning to the U.S. team: they came very close to losing against the African team, which they will also have to face in the group stage of the Olympic Games. South Sudan, the youngest sovereign state in the world (it became an independent country in 2011), managed to reach halftime 14 points ahead on the scoreboard, and with 13 minutes left in the game still had an 11-point lead , thanks in large part to Marial Shayok's three-pointers (currently playing in the Chinese league) and the lack of success of the American players beyond the three-point line. However, in the final stretch of the game, the U.S. team managed to come from behind to take a hard-fought victory. Winning basket by LeBron James Late in the third quarter, the U.S. went on a 16-0 run, including a pair of three-pointers by Stephen Curry, and looked like they were finally in control of the game. But the South Sudanese did not give up , and the last quarter was a thrilling one. JT Thor, the only South Sudanese player currently playing in the NBA, hit a three-pointer with 20 seconds left to put the South Sudanese up by one point. But that's when LeBron James , who at 39 had arguably been the best of the game, scored the final basket to make the final score 100-101 for a narrow U.S. victory. This agonizing basket is being widely shared on networks by users like @ElCesar_Guerra. In this way, LeBron James saved his national team from a defeat that would have been painful despite being a friendly, and the veteran star finished with 23 points, 6 rebounds and 6 assists. Of course, in the final seconds there was still time for a controversy: the referee did not signal a foul by Anthony Edwards on Wenyen Gabriel, which could have changed the final outcome. The U.S. has one last friendly before the start of the Olympic Games: this Monday against Germany, also in London. After that, in Paris, Serbia, Puerto Rico and South Sudan , who have made it so difficult for them in this preparation match, await them in the group stage. For its part, Spain shares a group in the Paris Games with Australia, Greece and Canada . The first match of the Spanish team in the Olympic Games will take place next Saturday, July 27, against the Australians. The Olympic Games are about to begin, and in basketball the great favorite is once again the United States , in a team that has many NBA stars such as LeBron James , Stephen Curry, Anthony Davis, Joel Embiid or Anthony Edwards. However, in their most recent friendly match they have left many doubts. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 PARIS, France - Los Angeles Lakers NBA player LeBron James has been assigned to carry the flag for the U.S. Delegation during the opening ceremony of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. This event will mark James as the first male basketball player in history to assume this honor at an Olympic opening. LeBron James , a four-time NBA champion, will lead the U.S. Delegation at the opening event, which will take place on the Seine River in the French capital this Friday, July 26. It is an incredible honor to represent the United States on this global stage, especially at a time that can unite the entire world. Sports have the power to bring us all together and I am proud to be a part of this important moment," said James after the news broke. Fit to lead ???? The moment LeBron James found out he will be the first ???????? #USABMNT athlete to serve as a @TeamUSA Flag Bearer. pic.twitter.com/kEWK45BBQH - USA Basketball (@usabasketball) July 22, 2024 USA Flag Bearers. James' recognition as a flag bearer follows the precedents of basketball players Dawn Staley at Athens 2004 and Sue Bird at Tokyo 2020, who also carried the U.S. flag at their respective opening ceremonies. Fourth Olympic Games for LeBron James These will be the fourth Olympic Games in which LeBron James , who previously won a bronze medal in Athens 2004 and two golds in Beijing 2008 and London 2012, will compete. According to information from El Universal, the name of the female athlete who will accompany LeBron James at the opening of Paris 2024 will be revealed this Tuesday, July 23. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/lebron-james 00 LeBron James has officially been chosen as flag bearer for the HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/united-states 00 United States delegation during the HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/olympics/olympics-2024-live-news-paris-team-gb-opening-latest-.html 00 Paris 2024 Olympics opening ceremony . The four-time HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/nba 00 NBA champion and two-time Olympic gold medalist was voted by a group of nearly 600 members of HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/team-usa 00 Team USA to be the men's flag bearer during the Paris Games' opening ceremony, which takes place on Friday, July 26. James, 39, was nominated by his USA men's basketball teammate HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/stephen-curry 00 Stephen Curry for the honor. "On behalf of myself and the entire men's Olympic basketball team, we would like to nominate LeBron James to be the flag bearer for all of Team USA. We understand how much of an honor it is to be in that position, and I think LeBron's entire career on and off the court should speak for itself as him being worthy of that honor," Curry said in a nominating HYPERLINK https://www.usab.com/news/2024/07/lebron-james-to-serve-as-flag-bearer-for-team-usa-at-paris-2024-opening-ceremony 00 video . On Monday, Curry found the perfect way to inform the Ohio native that he will be serving as flag bearer during the televised event later this week. "You get to wave the flag in Paris, my man," the Golden State Warriors star told James in a viral HYPERLINK https://www.instagram.com/reel/C9uWD_AJ8Rm/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=-3754-4d9f-9396- 00 clip . As the entire men's USA basketball team clapped for James, he held his hand to his chest and shook hands with his fellow athletes. In a HYPERLINK https://www.usab.com/news/2024/07/lebron-james-to-serve-as-flag-bearer-for-team-usa-at-paris-2024-opening-ceremony 00 statement shared by the US Olympic and Paralympic Committee, James expressed how honored he felt to wave the American flag during the opening ceremony on the Seine River. HYPERLINK https://www.instagram.com/reel/C9uWD_AJ8Rm/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading 00 View this post on Instagram HYPERLINK https://www.instagram.com/reel/C9uWD_AJ8Rm/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading 00 A post shared by TODAY (@todayshow) "It's an incredible honor to represent the United States on this global stage, especially in a moment that can bring the whole world together," he said. "For a kid from Akron, this responsibility means everything to not only myself, but to my family, all the kids in my hometown, my teammates, fellow Olympians and so many people across the country with big aspirations. Sports have the power to bring us all together, and I'm proud to be a part of this important moment." The basketball star is set to compete in his fourth Olympics this summer. James helped lead the men's USA basketball team to gold in both 2008 and 2012. He competed in his first Olympic Games in 2004 at just 19 years old, where he won a bronze medal. James currently plays forward for the Los Angeles Lakers. In 2011, he launched the "I Promise Program" as part of his LeBron James Family Foundation, which has provided more than 1,400 Akron-area students with educational resources. With approval from Akron Public Schools, James officially opened the I Promise School in July 2018. "We are thrilled to announce LeBron James as one of two flag bearers who will lead Team USA in the Opening Ceremony, and officially open the HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/paris-2024 00 Paris 2024 Games," said USOPC CEO Sarah Hirshland. "Being selected by your teammates to carry the flag is a tremendous honor - and a testament to LeBron's passion for Team USA and his dedication to his sport. We will watch with great pride as the flag bearers lead our athletes who are about to embark on a remarkable journey together." James becomes the first man and third member of USA Basketball to earn the honor of Team USA flag bearer, joining five-time Olympic champion Sue Bird (2020) and five-time Olympic gold medalist player and coach Dawn Staley (2004) as the only basketball players to receive the designation. The female flag bearer who will lead the US delegation alongside James will be announced Tuesday, according to the USOPC. The 2024 Olympic Games HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/olympics/olympics-2024-watch-tv-online-live-stream-.html 00 kick off in Paris, France, on July 26 with an opening ceremony along the Seine River. The event will air live from Paris at 1:30pm ET on Friday. The USA men's basketball team will play its first Olympic game against Serbia on July 27, one day after the opening ceremony. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The historic basketball player LeBron James , player of the U.S. men's basketball team and the NBA's Los Angeles Lakers, will be the male athlete in charge of carrying the flag of his country at the opening of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games . James, a four-time NBA winner and internationally recognized figure in his discipline, will lead the U.S. delegation at the event to be held this Friday, July 26, on the Seine River in the French capital. LeBron James and the Olympic Games In what will be his fourth Games , after previously winning a bronze in Athens 2004 and two golds in Beijing 2008 and London 2012 , James has been chosen as flag bearer with the votes of the 592 athletes that make up the U.S. team. The name of the female athlete who will join him in this task will be unveiled on Tuesday. "It is an incredible honor to represent the United States on this global stage , especially at a time that can unite the entire world. For a kid from Akron, this responsibility means everything, not only to me, but also to my family, all the kids in my hometown, my teammates, my fellow Olympians and so many people across the country with high aspirations. Sports have the power to bring us all together and I am proud to be a part of this important moment," James said after hearing the news, according to a statement released by Team USA. The same statement included remarks from Sarah Hirshland, CEO of the U.S. Olympic and Paralympic Committee (USOPC): "To be selected by your teammates to carry the flag is a tremendous honor and a testament to LeBron's passion for Team USA and his dedication to his sport. We will watch with great pride as the flag bearers guide our athletes who are about to embark on an extraordinary journey together." LeBron will be the third NBA basketball name to receive the honor , following previous honors for players Dawn Staley (2004) and Sue Bird (2020), the latter most recently along with baseball's Eddy Alvarez. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Michael Jordan and Magic revolutionized sports history, but at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics, the U.S. flag bearer was marathon runner Francie Larrieu Smith. In fact, until Tokyo 2020, no basketball player had ever received that honor, but this Monday morning Team USA announced that LeBron James will be the bearer of the Stars and Stripes flag at the Opening Ceremony of Paris 2024, which will take place on the Seine River this Friday, July 26. The decision to name LeBron is highly symbolic, as the 39-year-old is living his final years as a professional athlete and these will be his last Olympic Games. ADVERTISEMENT In addition, James has great leadership among his basketball peers, in the sports world in general and carries out a lot of community work. In fact, LeBron was chosen by the nearly 600 athletes on the U.S. Olympic team through a vote conducted by the U.S. Olympic and Paralympic Committee (USOPC). As the Opening Ceremony will be on the Seine, James will lead a boat of athletes and will be accompanied by a female flag bearer to be named in the coming hours. What did LeBron say upon receiving the news that he will be the flag bearer? "It is an incredible honor to represent the United States on this world stage, especially at a time that can unite the entire world," LeBron James said via a statement. And, he humbly added that, "For a kid from Akron, this responsibility means everything, not just to me, but to my family, all the kids in my hometown, my teammates, my fellow Olympians and so many people across the country with great aspirations. Sports have the power to bring us all together, and I'm proud to be a part of this important moment." How many medals does LeBron James have? Paris 2024 will mark the 39-year-old veteran's return to the Olympics, who captured the bronze medal in Athens 2004, then was part of the redemption of the U.S. basketball team, which under coach Mike Krzyzewski, won gold in Beijing 2008 and London 2012. ADVERTISEMENT LeBron will lead a star-studded team of Stephen Curry, Joel Embiid, Jayson Tatum, Devin Booker and Anthony Edwards at the Olympics, but last Saturday they had a big scare in a warm-up game in London when they beat South Sudan by just one point. In the first round of men's Olympic basketball , the United States will be in Group C with Serbia, Puerto Rico and South Sudan.
