(AP Photo/Steve Marcus) Body_116 Body_116 Body fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Female honoree to be revealed Tuesday LeBron James wasn't totally sure what the opening ceremony was all about when he was picked for his first Olympics in 2004. This time, he'll be one of the stars of the show. James has been picked by his fellow U.S. Olympians to serve as the male flagbearer for the Americans in Friday night's opening ceremony for the Paris Games. He becomes the third basketball player - and the first men's player - to carry the U.S. flag at the start of an Olympics, joining Dawn Staley for the Athens Games in 2004 and Sue Bird for the Tokyo Games that happened in 2021. "It's an incredible honor to represent the United States on this global stage, especially in a moment that can bring the whole world together," James said. "For a kid from Akron, this responsibility means everything to not only myself, but to my family, all the kids in my hometown, my teammates, fellow Olympians and so many people across the country with big aspirations. Sports have the power to bring us all together, and I'm proud to be a part of this important moment." The 39-year-old James got word of the honor Monday in London, a few hours before the U.S. men's team was scheduled to play its final pre-Olympics exhibition game against World Cup champion Germany. Fellow U.S. star and first-time Olympian Stephen Curry, on behalf of the U.S. men's team, nominated James for the flagbearer role. "We understand how much of an honor it is to be in that position and I think Bron's entire career, on and off the court, speaks for itself as him being worthy of that honor," Curry said in the nomination video. "He has represented what it means to be excellent both on and off the court in his commitment to service and to uplifting the community in all ways that he knows how has been a lifelong passion," Curry added. "And the work speaks for itself." The female U.S. flagbearer is expected to be revealed Tuesday. The International Olympic Committee decided in 2020 that national delegations would have two flagbearers - one male, one female - at the opening ceremony at an Olympics, a move to promote gender parity. The U.S. is expected to have nearly 600 athletes in the Paris Games, about 53% of them female. "Being selected by your teammates to carry the flag is a tremendous honor - and a testament to LeBron's passion for Team USA and his dedication to his sport," U.S. Olympic and Paralympic Committee CEO Sarah Hirshland said. James - a global icon, a four-time NBA champion and the league's all-time leading scorer set to go into his record-tying 22nd NBA season - is set to play in the Olympics for the fourth time, after he was part of U.S. teams that won bronze in 2004, gold at Beijing in 2008 and gold again in London in 2012. He walked in the opening ceremony at each of his three previous Olympics. This time, he'll float. This will be an opening ceremony like none other in Olympic history: Thousands of athletes will be part of a flotilla sailing along the River Seine at sunset toward the Eiffel Tower. It's a 6-kilometer (3.7-mile) route, with about 320,000 guests set to watch from the river bank and about 1 billion more, Olympic officials estimate, watching on televisions around the world. Not all Olympic athletes take part in the opening ceremony; many skip it for logistical reasons, such as having to compete the following day. James and the four-time defending gold medalist U.S. men don't open Olympic play until Sunday, when they face Serbia at Lille, France. James and the U.S. Olympians will be waiting longer than almost any other nation for their trip on the Seine. How many NBA championships has LeBron James won Since first being drafted by the Cavaliers in the 2023 NBA Draft, he has become one of the most decorated basketball players of all time having been named a 20x NBA All-Star, NBA Rookie of the Year, 4x NBA Finals Most Valuable Player, 3x NBA All-Star Game MVP, 6x NBA All-Defensive selection and a NBA Cup winner in 2024. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 As Paris celebrates the kickoff to the Olympics, a sports statesman starts his goodbyes. Basketball star LeBron James , who is carrying the U.S. flag with tennis player Coco Gauff at Friday's opening ceremony, first participated in the Olympics 20 years ago. This will be his fourth - he sat out in 2016 and 2020 - and last. For this reporter, and basketball fan, it made me want to be here to witness these Games more. Mr. James has flourished, winning gold with the men's team twice, and bronze in his 2004 debut in Greece. Two decades later, this Olympics is shaping up to be one of encouragement and poise - and may even include another visit to the podium. This time, he is literally the standard-bearer - chosen by his fellow athletes to represent them. Another basketball great, Stephen Curry, nominated him for the honor. "He has represented what it means to be excellent both on and off the court in his commitment to service and to uplifting the community in all ways that he knows how has been a lifelong passion," Mr. Curry said in a video. "And the work speaks for itself." Mr. James sets the tone this year as the oldest (nearing age 40) and most decorated U.S. basketball player. After an exhibition game against Canada earlier this month, he gathered teammates on the court, put his arms across as many shoulders as he could reach, and offered words of encouragement. "I think LeBron - I don't want to speak for him - but I think he understands, like, 'I've been through it. We have guys who haven't,'" said managing director Grant Hill, who chose the members of this year's team, at an event for reporters in New York this spring. Mr. Hill, himself an NBA Hall of Famer and Olympic gold medalist, added that Mr. James is a leader at the Olympics in the same way he has been to NBA players on the championship teams he's been on. The combination of Mr. Curry and Mr. James, Mr. Hill said, reminds him of Larry Bird and Magic Johnson playing on the 1992 Olympic Dream Team. Paris marks the first time the two superstars have played together. Watching Mr. James and Mr. Curry in the game versus Canada earlier this month was beautiful. Basketball is my favorite sport. I love the language of body movements, jumping and cutting with no-look passes, windmill dunks, and long-range 3-point balls raining down. I fell in love with the language of the game like a hopeless romantic years ago. Watching this group, led by Mr. James, has been nothing short of spectacular. Every fast break. Every crisp pass. Every steal and block. Every dunk. Gold is again the goal for Mr. James and his fellow ballers - though it may not be as easy as in the past. In an unexpected nail-biter against South Sudan last weekend, Mr. James saved the day for Team USA with a game-winning layup with eight seconds left to play. The result was a 1-point win over a motivated South Sudan team appearing in the Olympics for the first time. "I'm going to be honest: I like those better than the blowouts," The Associated Press reported the forward saying. "At least we get tested." Another pre-Olympic exclamation point was when he put the team on his back in a win against the FIBA world champs, Germany, a few days after the South Sudan game. This is all much different from when Mr. James debuted at the Olympics 20 years ago as the NBA Rookie of the Year. That was before his grit and determination would be questioned. In time, he overcame those obstacles and won four championship rings. He honed his leadership chops by being consistent, working on his craft, and committing himself to getting better. Today he holds the record as the NBA's all-time leading scorer. "Team USA has given so much ... to me over the last 20 years," Mr. James said when accepting the honor of carrying his country's flag. He added an allusion to America's election-year atmosphere. "In a country that's so divided, I hope that moment will unite us or bring us together," he said in a video shared by NBC's "Today" show. It is special and sobering that this will be Mr. James' last Games. He told reporters during training camp for the Olympics that he doesn't have many years left in the NBA. He was clear that he won't be at the Games in 2028, when they come to Los Angeles, where he has played with the champion Lakers for the past half-dozen years. Like Mr. Hill, co-owner of the Atlanta Hawks, Mr. James wants to be an NBA owner. He has his sights set on a new team in Las Vegas. Men's basketball couldn't have had a better storyline this summer than Mr. James leading the charge in his final Games. Michael Jordan participating in 1992 inspired Mr. Hill to play in 1996. The snowball effect of those NBA All-Stars participating led to Mr. James and another Lakers great, Kobe Bryant, playing years later. One superstar stepping up to lead begets another. I'm happy to see this, because it means someone else great will come after Mr. James

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 READ MORE: Fans slam 'horrendous' opening ceremony coverage on NBC READ MORE: Simone Biles' parents reveal why she missed opening ceremony An image of LeBron James holding the American flag during the 2024 Olympics opening ceremony has already become a viral meme. The four-time NBA champ, who served as the nation's flag bearer alongside tennis star Coco Gauff, was seen standing on the front of a boat floating down the River Seine on Friday evening. Many quickly drew comparisons to the iconic image of George Washington crossing the Delaware River in 1776. ' LeBron James looks like he just founded America,' one fan joked on X alongside the image of James. 'General Lebron James crossing the Delaware,' another hilariously chimed in, while a third joked, ' LeBron James invented the United States.' In the snap, rained poured down on the basketball legend, who was surrounded by America's nearly 600 athletes. 'It's an incredible honor to represent the United States on this global stage, especially in a moment that can bring the whole world together,' James said when he was named a flagbearer earlier this week. 'For a kid from Akron, this responsibility means everything to not only myself, but to my family, all the kids in my hometown, my teammates, fellow Olympians and so many people across the country with big aspirations,' he went on. 'Sports have the power to bring us all together, and I'm proud to be a part of this important moment.' The gold medalist, who is playing in his fourth Olympic Games, was also interviewed live on NBC during the ceremony. 'For myself and Coco, for us to represent our country, us being black kids as well, representing our culture, representing where we came from - it gives everybody so much hope where we come from,' James said. 'That's all we can ask for. We take it with the utmost responsibility and honor.' fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The U.S. Dream Team lived up to expectations and won the gold medal in Basketball at the Paris Games with a 98-87 victory over France. In a packed Bercy Arena with more than 20 thousand fans united in one voice in support of France, the United States began by unleashing the first hoopla. Although France showed its response through Victor Wembanyama , LeBron James and Stephen Curry gradually tipped the scales in favor of the Americans. The first half favored the United States , as in the first quarter they went 20-15 up, while in the second they increased the advantage to go to the break 49-41. France took the third quarter by imposing conditions 25-23, finishing only 6 points behind the 'Dream Team' (72-66 ). For the last 10 minutes, the Bercy Arena was a complete ' hell ' in support of their country under the chant "Allez les bleus" (let's go blue), however, the efforts of the fans -despite infecting their compatriots- were not enough, having to resign themselves to the silver medal . Previously, Serbia beat Germany 93-83 to take the last place on the podium (bronze ) in Basketball of the XXXIII edition of the Games. UNITED STATES DREAM TEAM 2024 The 12-player roster that the United States put together to win the gold medal at the Paris Games was led by LeBron James and Stephen Curry, but these are all the players on the U.S. roster: LeBron James , Stephen Curry , Jayson Tatum , Joel Embiid , Jrue Holiday , Bam Adebayo , Anthony Davis , Kawhi Leonard , Tyrese Haliburton , Devin Booker , Kevin Durant and Anthony Edwards . The U.S. team became Olympic basketball champions for the fifth time in a row, raising its all-time record number of titles to 17. The commitment of its players and their celebration, proudly waving the American flag, give greater strength to a team that is, today, unbeatable. Victor Wembanyama, the young local star, was the top scorer of the game, with 26 points, however, it was not enough for an American team united by Stephen Curry and LeBron James . fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 This time I got whimsical, whimsical like a little child. As we were handing out the day's stories, I raised my index finger and said to the boss, "I want to go to the Dream Team finals: -I want to go to the Dream Team finals, I want to see the NBA giants. Silence in the room. The boss thought about it for a moment, I suspect he was saying to himself: "Doesn't this athletics moron want to go to cover the last day of athletics? And he looked at me and scrutinized me, and I gave him the face of a needy child and he gave up. -Come on, come on, come on, come on. Until now, I had never sat in the stands to watch the Dream Team, I had never seen these big guys fighting tooth and nail. And I think about it as I project myself back several decades. I go back to Barcelona '92, how young we were. In those prodigious years in which this story of the Dream Team was born, basketball tickets flew in a flash ( basketball and all major events, actually), and so I only managed to watch baseball at the Teixonera (yes sir, I saw the Dominican baseball players facing Cuba) and some morning athletics in Montju\'efc (Quincy Watts in the first round of the 400, pas mal). So, in those wonderful days when Jordan, Magic Johnson and Larry Bird were leaving their bubbly bubble to embrace the Olympics, I had only been able to do the usual: enjoy them on TV. Anyway, this Dream Team and I had never met. Never live and direct. But here I am. (...) It's funny, because from a bird's eye view, from the heights of the Arena Bercy, the most impressive guy I see down there is not playing with the Americans, but with the locals. I'm talking about Victor Wembanyama. Wembanyama (26 points in the end) is twenty years old and 2.24m tall, but I'm not going to unveil this praying mantis now. Wembanyama has already been in the NBA for a year (he plays for the Spurs). And he has been rookie of the year. Vintage basketball I've never been much of a LeBron James guy until I saw him live: the man squeezes like a juvenile. Tap-tap-tap. The baton is banged by Tony Parker, another Frenchman who played for the Spurs and shined in the NBA. And looking at this spectacle, at the festival of these Frenchmen who have unfolded a Games full of colossal stages, I feel like in the days of L\'e9on Marchand or Teddy Riner, French giants who have definitely made these Games the Games of les bleus. From a bird's eye view I can't check, but I would say that LeBron James ' beard is snowing. LeBron James (39) is twice Wembanyama's age, he could almost be his father, but if he has to slap the kid, he slaps him. (In fact, LeBron James is the father of Bronny James, who is 19 and already playing for the Lakers...). And while I'm on the subject of LeBron James , I'll take this opportunity to confess: I've never been much of a LeBron James guy. My apologies to millennials, but I'm more into vintage. I'm more into Dr. J. and Magic Johnson basketball . Those eighties were more artisanal, I've always told myself. More elastic, less prefabricated. Well, when the fight starts, I'm out of my mind. Down there, the big man is caracoleaing, he brings the ball up like a point guard, he squeezes himself in the pivot like a junior, he practices trash-talking like a badass and one-on-one like a dancer. And he demands court time from Steve Kerr. He wants to play all the time. He plays 32 minutes. He scores 14 points. This United States is a dream team because Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant (15 points) follow the roll of good LeBron James . Some, with more success than the other. For two quarters, Curry is not very good. He misses his first three three-pointers, making me question the videos he posts on social networks. Those impossible shots, aren't they fake? Curry (24 points, 8 of 12 on three-pointers) silenced my suspicious thoughts in the final stretch, when he made all his shots, even though he sometimes shoots from very far away. By then, the Dream Team rides with a margin of ten points, one up one down, and les bleus are trailing, and my soul is comforted, because when I go out there I can cross off another vital notch. I have had a daughter. I have published a book. I've seen the Dream Team play live. I have yet to plant the tree. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 READ MORE: Steph Curry puffs on a cigar as he joins USA teammates at party READ MORE: LeBron James gives USA men's team perfect two-word nickname USA has maintained its stranglehold on Olympics basketball after the women's team joined the men in beating France to claim gold on Sunday. LeBron James and two of his men's teammates, proudly boasting their gold medals at Bercy Arena, watched on in the crowd as the likes of A'ja Wilson and Kelsey Plum inspired their female counterparts to a hard-fought 67-66 final victory over the hosts. It means America has now swept up gold in men's and women's basketball at the Olympics for five consecutive Games, while the latter have claimed a record eight titles on the bounce. Wilson scored 21 points as the women's team survived the biggest challenge of an unprecedented run to eight straight Olympic gold medals against France. No team had been able to push them during an impressive streak of 61 consecutive wins. Only two of those victories had been by single digits before the game against France. The eight straight golds broke a tie with the American men's program that won seven in a row from 1936-68. Their victory came less than 24 hours after the U.S. men's team also beat France in the title game. This was the first time in Olympic history that both gold medal games featured the same two teams. Unlike the men's game, though, this one came down to the final minute and one last shot by France that was just inside the three-point line. The Americans were up 67-64 with 3.9 seconds left after Kahleah Copper hit two free throws. Marine Johannes brought the ball up the court to Gabby Williams who caught the ball just inside the 3-point line and banked in over the outstreched arms of Breanna Srewart for the final margin. There was a brief delay before the officials signaled that it was a two-point shot, which led to the beginning of a celebration and a lot of happy hugs for the Americans and left the French players standing in disbelief as they fell just short. Williams, who finished with 19 points, had hit a deep 3 a few seconds earlier to get France within one before Copper's free throws. LeBron attended the women's final with wife Savannah and daughter Zhuri Nova, 9, while his teammates Bam Adebayo and Derrick White were also sat alongside them. On Saturday night USA's men's team was able to hold off France behind 24 points from Steph Curry, as a star-studded group including LeBron and Kevin Durant thwarted a late rally from the hosts. The Americans led by as much as 14 in the fourth quarter but saw that advantage dwindle to just three after a Victor Wembanyama tip-in with just under three minutes remaining. From there, Curry caught fire as he hit four three-pointers from the 2:48 mark on to see USA to the gold. His final dagger came during a broken play with 35 seconds left, when he heaved in a three-pointerover the arms of Evan Fournier and Nicolas Batum to restore the lead to nine. There was no way back for France, and Curry knew it as he did his famous 'night night celebration' - with the last half-minute proving unclimactic for the USA. Later on Saturday night, he continued to taunt the host nation by wearing a hoodie featuring the words 'Nuit Nuit' - French for 'Night Night'. American journalist Rachel Nicols shared a video of Curry wearing the hoodie on Saturday night, and American fans absolutely loved his savage choice of clothing. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Fresh off a gold medal victory at the Paris Olympics 2024, LeBron James drew a flak from the netizens for his behaviour towards a young fan in the French capital. The American basketball legend LeBron James landed in a hot water after the video of him snapping at a kid went viral on social media following the USA's gold medal win at the Paris Olympics 2024 on Saturday, August 11. The defending Olympic champions the USA registered 98-87 win over the hosts France to clinch their fifth consecutive gold medal in basketball at the Summer Games. For the second time, the United States of America (USA) clinched five successive gold medals in basketball at the Olympics. Following the fifth Olympic win, the USA basketball players were completely soaked up in the celebration. However, what caught the attention amid the celebration was the behaviour of legendary LeBron James towards the kids outside the hotel. In a video that went viral on social media, James can be getting out of the car with the gold medal around his neck and the a young fan was approaching the veteran basketballer with a phone in his hand. However, the 39-year-old seemed to be a little as he can be heard sternly telling the kid 'STOP! STOP! and entered the restaurant with a little dance. After the video of his interaction with the kid went viral on social media, LeBron James received backlash and criticism from the netizners for his behaviour. People took to social media, especially on X handle (formerly Twitter) and slammed the legendary basketball player for being arrogant after winning the Olympic gold, while others feel that James would have behaved differently if the fan was a black kid. With the fifth successive gold medal in Olympics, the USA solidified their position as the most successful the basketball team at the Summer Games, with 20 medals, including 17 gold, a silver and 2 bronze. The only time the USA basketball team lost in the Olympic final was in 1972, defeated by the Soviet Union. The USA basketball team had a brilliant run at the Paris Olympics 2024. Firstly, the 17-time Olympic champions topped the group stage by winning all three matches against Serbia, South Sudan and Puerto Rico and qualified for the quarterfinals, where they defeated Brazil. In the semifinal, USA again met Serbia but defeated them again and to book their berth for the final. In the final, the USA overcame with a tough challenge from France in order to clinch their fifth Olympic gold medal on the trot. With this amount, Vin\'edcius J\'fanior would have an annual salary slightly above Cristiano Ronaldo at Al-Nassr, who receives 200 million dollars (around R$1.1 billion at the current price). Outside of soccer, golfer Jon Rahm earns the most per year, but the figure is no higher than that of the soccer duo - the Spaniard earns 198 million dollars. Karim Benzema, from Al-Ittihad, and Lamar Jackson, an American soccer player who plays for the Baltimore Ravens, round off the top-5. The information on the amounts received by the players comes from Forbes magazine, published in May this year, and takes into account the salaries that the players earn at their clubs, not counting off the field. The amount proposed for Vin\'edcius J\'fanior is three times higher than the salary of Lionel Messi, eight times the best player in the world. In addition, the Brazilian striker would be paid four times more than LeBron James at the Lakers. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 One of the biggest dreams of basketball star LeBron James has been to play with his son Bronny, and it is being fulfilled, as the 19-year-old was selected in 2024 for the 55th pick in the NBA draft by the same team as his father, the Los Angeles Lakers. Should they play in the same game, it would be the first time in NBA history that father and son play on the same court and on the same team. However, four-time NBA champion and four-time NBA Most Valuable Player LeBron James can't imagine the two of them running around shouting "Dad!" or "Son!" to pass the ball to each other during the game, which is why he established a rule as a condition for his offspring: He can't call him dad. ADVERTISEMENT "No, he can't," LeBron James said with a chuckle about the possibility of Bronny calling him "dad" on the court. That's what the 39-year-old said in an interview with 'The Shop,' Usa Today reported. "We've already established that. He can't call me 'dad' in the workplace, OK? Once we get out of the practice facility and the doors close, I could go back to 'dad.' In the car, if we go together, at home, it could be 'dad'. No, he has to call me like '2-3 or 'Bron' or 'GOAT', if he wants to. It's up to him. I mean, it's up to him," LeBron James expressed. LeBron James on his son Bronny: "It's going to be an adjustment for him." Usa Today recalled that LeBron James signed a $104 million contract for two years in the NBA, which means he will have another season to be able to play with his son Bronny. The champion acknowledged that for him it will be easy because he has always called his son Bronny, but he knows that for his son it won't be, since he has called him dad all his life, something he must adjust on the court. "It's easy for me because I've been calling him 'Bronny' for a long time...It's not like I've been saying, 'Hey son, hey son,' so it will be easy for me. It'll be an adjustment for him, but we can't run around the court with him going, 'Dad! Dad, I'm open! ' No, you can't do that," LeBron said. ADVERTISEMENT Bronny James has excelled in sports since his childhood and teenage years, playing soccer and basketball , but ultimately, he settled on his father's discipline. His 1.88 height is decisive on the court. He was elected in May 2023 by the University of Southern California. Bronny suffered a cardiac arrest in July 2023 during a workout at USC.48, so he was out of action for five months. He was reactivated in December with the Trojans. He entered this April's NBA draft averaging 4.8 points, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 assists last season in 25 games at Southern California. The rumor machine went to work. And it crossed paths with LeBron, of course . Some said the move would come with a four-way trade involving the Nets and Pistons to bring Curry to the Lakers' purple and gold. It didn't happen and Curry renewed for another year for more than $60 million. My plan is to play for the Warriors for life. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 There are still echoes in Paris and around the world of the gold won by the U.S. team at the Olympic Games . Of the show they put on, but also of how hard it was for them to win it. They needed the best version of their top stars, one of them, LeBron James , who in Paris won his third gold in the basketball tournament and his first MVP award. But what if LeBron had set his sights on winning an Olympic medal in something other than basketball ? After all, he is considered by almost everyone to be one of the greatest athletes of all time. Could he stand on the podium in other sports ? According to the Akron player, with proper preparation, yes. In what disciplines? While enjoying his last few days off before starting training camp with the Lakers, LeBron James starred in a new episode of The Shop in which he was asked in which discipline he believed he could compete in the Games besides basketball . LeBron had no trouble answering: "The first thing is there's no competition I can compete in without preparation ," LeBron said. "But I think maybe I could do high jump or long jump if you guys give me some time. I need like six months or maybe eight months of preparation to get it done ." When asked if he thought he could win a medal in those disciplines, his response was categorical: "I'm a guy who just gets on the podium ." It seems very pretentious to think of reaching the competitive level in six or eight months that athletes who dedicate their lives to it take years to achieve. However, let\'e2\'a4?s not forget that this is LeBron, one of the most powerful jump shooters the NBA has ever seen. This is fiction, but as always, betting against LeBron is a risky decision. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 India, Sept. 15 -- NBA superstar LeBron James ' birthday is approaching fast, and it will officially launch his early 40s chapter at the end of December. The American basketball legend has already attributed his Olympic farewell to the same age factor, saying he can't see himself playing in Los Angeles 2028. "I didn't see myself playing in Paris 2024, too. But four years from now, I can't see it," he said, per BasketNews' Donatas Urbonas. (Also read: LeBron James replacement reportedly cooking at Lakers amid team-up rumours with Steph Curry) The firm potential Olympic swan song ultimately puts a big question mark against his NBA career as well. Despite James' halcyon days behind him, he still edged out Michael Jordan on HoopsHype.com's GOAT rankings after his 2024 Olympic performance, celebrating an "increasing number of accolades and statistical categories." What takes the cake is that in a recent chat with the Los Angeles Times, Lakers owner Jeanie Buss also hailed him the greatest player in basketball history, owing to his stance as a captain and leader who "consistently delivers." With all that said and dusted, one still can't shake off the bittersweet feeling that James will no longer be listed as an active contemporary NBA player in the future. Nevertheless, his retirement timeline is as uncertain as changing tides. Weighing in on the issue of James' "final march," Dave McMenamin, who covers the Lakers for ESPN, said that whenever that hour finally arrives, it would be unmissable. The athlete's time in the big league would culminate in a "spectacular farewell," making for "must-see TV." The insider pushed back when asked if a "rough start" for the Lakers in the new season would ultimately send James packing early. "I wouldn't start looking at it like this is gonna be - the clock is starting to tick down on his career, where he would walk away from the Lakers and retire because they struggled throughout the season," McMenamin said. Countering the previous question, he pointed out that LeBron James "is gonna be on his own timeline." "I think when it's the final march of LeBron - perhaps final march rather than last dance - we're all gonna know. We're all gonna know, and it's gonna be must-see TV. It's gonna be the LeBron tour comin' to town throughout the 28 NBA cities, culminating in a spectacular farewell," the Lakers insider concluded. While numerous fans are hoping that James still has plenty of gas in the tank as he's "the only reason" many still watch the NBA, it remains uncertain whether Lakers would hit out of the park before he says his final goodbye to the court. It's been four years since the Los Angeles Lakers won the 2020 NBA Finals against the Miami Heat. The 2024 NBA Playoffs were an incredibly sour low point for the squad, as the Lakers were turned away in the first round. James was named the MVP of the men's basketball tournament at the Paris Olympics after he led Team USA's "Avengers" to a golden victory. In a picture-perfect ending, he would also stick his landing to the top before the end of his NBA tenure, but we'll cross that when we get there. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 HYPERLINK https://www.scotsman.com/arts-and-culture/film-and-tv/netflixs-sunderland-til-i-die-is-a-tale-of-a-regions-unrelenting-love-for-its-city-it-deserved-the-happy-ending-4517481 00 Netflix are set to release one of the most highly-anticipated sports documentaries of the year in the coming weeks, with the launch of explosive, in-depth, behind the scenes HYPERLINK https://www.scotsman.com/sport/who-is-the-richest-nba-player-of-all-time-nba-players-with-the-highest-worth-including-michael-jordan-net-worth-4576057 00 NBA documentary series Starting 5 hitting on the platform this October. Following on from the success of other Netflix sports documentaries such as Receiver, Quarterback and Full Swing, the popular streaming service has will now dip its toes into basketball as it provides unfiltered footage of the world s biggest basketball stars, such as HYPERLINK https://www.scotsman.com/sport/when-does-the-new-nba-basketball-season-start-nba-2425-fixtures-and-key-dates-4722763 00 LeBron James and Jayson Tatum. Taking fans into the lives of the each player, the documentary will show how some of the world s biggest sports stars deal with the pressure of on-court battles, combined with their often hectic personal lives away from the arenas. While the Michael Jordan documentary The Last Dance is one of Netflix s most successful TV series ever made, the streaming app has yet to delve into modern day NBA in the way they have done with NFL, Formula 1 and golf. However, Starting 5 will take viewers right into the heart of NBA, with an exclusive look into the daily lives of the league s biggest icons during the 23/24 season and fans are already buzzing with excitement. The official synopsis of the documentary series will take NBA fanatics into the world of five of basketball s biggest names as they experience the players' battles on and off the court as they chase greatness, push through injuries and balance the demands of family and legacy." Want to know when the series is released and how to watch it? Here is everything you need to know about Starting 5. Starting 5 cast on Netflix - which NBA players are featured? As mentioned above, LA Lakers star LeBron James is the biggest name featured, though he is far from the only big name involved in the series. Alongside him will be Boston Celtic s Jayson Tatum, Minnesota Timberwolves Anthony Edwards, Miami Heat s Jimmy Butler and Sacramento Kings Domantas Sabonis. The addition of LeBron to the series will add a real layer of intrigue. Viewed as one of the best, if not THE best, players to ever play in the NBA, the doc will offer viewers exclusive footage of the league s oldest active player as he continues to defy expectations and dominate the court. They aren t the only big names set to feature in the documentary though, with confirmed appearances from former American President Barack Obama and former First Lady Michelle Obama. NFL legend Peyton Manning is also set to feature. Netflix Starting 5 - how many episodes? It has been confirmed that the documentary will be split into 10 episodes, much like the NFL series Quarterback , which had a similar theme and focussed on a series of individual players throughout. While not yet confirmed, each episode is expected to be around 45 minutes long. Starting 5 Netflix UK release date The documentary will land on Netflix UK on October 9, the streaming service has confirmed. This means the sports doc will land mere days before the start of the 24/25 NBA season, which is set to begin on October 22. Each episode of the series will be available to watch at 8.01am on the morning of release. Watch the trailer for the show HYPERLINK https://x.com/netflix/status/277 00 here . You must have a Netflix subscription to watch Starting 5, which can be purchased HYPERLINK https://www.netflix.com/ 00 here . Prices start at \'a34.99 a month. Berlin, Sep 26 (EFE) - Former center Shaquille O'Neal has assured that he is "sure" that the United States team, champion of the last five editions of the Olympic basketball tournament, "will not win the gold" in the next Games to be held in the Californian city of Los Angeles in 2028.

In an interview with the German sports magazine 'Sport Bild', the former four-time champion of the American professional basketball league has stressed that "20 years ago, the United States was light years behind other countries", but that this situation "has changed" and "Europe has caught up, is just behind and will soon surpass the United States".

" LeBron James won't play anymore, neither will Kevin Durant and Steph Curry. Joel Embiid probably won't be there either. Then the supremacy is over. The European teams are really good: Croatia, Serbia and even Germany," said the man who won gold at Atlanta 1996.

Asked about the German national team, reigning world champion and executioner of the United States in the 2023 championship, the historic Lakers player stressed that point guard Denis Schr\'f6der "is good" and "different from other NBA point guards and plays in a very unorthodox way", with his "own style". Sometimes he goes to the basket when no one expects it.

"I like the Wagner brothers too! They play unbelievably. Franz and Moritz, they're both great! I hope they never split up and always play for the same club. Franz is a scorer and Moritz plays hard and is not afraid," he added.

Asked about his choice in the debate between Michael Jordan and LeBron James as the best player in basketball history, the one-time NBA season MVP went for the Chicago Bulls shooting guard.

"You have to look at the different eras. When I was a kid, Julius Erving and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar were the measure of all things. When I was in college, Magic Johnson and Larry Bird were the best. When I got to the NBA, Michael Jordan dominated. Then came Kobe Bryant and LeBron James . But for me the greatest of all time is Michael Jordan," he explained.

Asked about his all-time favorite team, 'Shaq' chose Michael Jordan himself, his former Los Angeles teammate Kobe Bryant, Julius Earving, Karl Malone and himself.

Of his one-time NBA career rival, Dallas Mavericks German power forward Dirk Nowitzki, O'Neal praised that, although in his first year in the NBA he "seemed a little immobile and rusty," he had "so much passion for the game and put so much work into it that he became one of the best players in history."

"I used to ... think Detlef Schrempf would forever remain the greatest German basketball player of all time, but Dirk has surpassed him. He's a good guy. It's always nice to talk to him," he concluded.

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Ethan York, Contributing Writer LeBron James is arguably one of the greatest basketball players in the history of the game. He has multiple impressive feats in the NBA, including winning four championships with three different organizations, winning four NBA MVP awards and holding the highest total point record of all time in NBA history, according to ESPN. However, one of his most controversial feats to date is playing on the same team as his son Bronny James. Bronny James played only one season for the University of Southern California before setting off on his NBA career this year. His short career in the NBA has stirred a lot of controversial debates on his legitimacy in the NBA. Some people believe he has earned his spot on the court, though some say it is nepotism, according to ESPN. I believe Bronny James being in the NBA is a perfect example of nepotism. His father is one of the greatest to ever play, but Bronny James' performance shouldn't impress anyone. In his college career, he only played 25 games, averaging 4.8 points per game and only 2.1 assists per game, according to Sports Reference. These stats are hard to impress anyone, especially an NBA recruiter. If Bronny's last name wasn't James, would he still even be considered a draft prospect? He was still a first-year and had an opportunity to continue to develop himself as a player by staying in college, but he instead jumped into the NBA to play with his father. I believe it would've been more beneficial for him and his credibility as a player to spend more time playing college basketball . While Bronny James is still highly controversial, you can't deny it is a great thing to see the first active father-son duo in the NBA, especially from a player like LeBron James . Unfortunately, the NBA likes to milk this opportunity. For example, when Bronny James checked into his first game against the Minnesota Timberwolves with LeBron James , the commentators made a big deal about it. When Bronny James and LeBron James both checked in together for the Lakers' first game of the season against the Timberwolves it was a historic moment. Unfortunately for Bronny James, that was his biggest highlight of the game. He played for three minutes and had one rebound, according to USA Today. It seems like Bronny James is just a testament to LeBron James ' legacy. It's just another achievement to play long enough to be teammates with your son. I mean, not many players have created their own teammates. The reality of it, though, is Bronny James is not going to be able to live up to his father. This isn't a bad thing for him, many great players won't be able to compare to LeBron James . Just because he is his son doesn't mean he has to either. Since Bronny James has chosen this path though, he must capitalize on it, especially since he didn't earn his spot on the Lakers himself - his name did. The best thing Bronny James can do now is develop his game and figure out who he is as a player. He's set to spend some of his time in the Lakers G League affiliate, according to Bleacher Report. This is a crucial moment for him as he needs the experience and some time away from the spotlight. While Bronny did make his way to the NBA through nepotism and hasn't performed in the way an NBA draft pick should, I think he can still develop into a great player, in his own way. He needs to form his own career, he is not his dad and will never be. Only time will tell if Bronny James can prove himself in the NBA. But a level of decency and humanity in a top leadership position is essential, and she has it," Curry remarked, highlighting Harris s leadership qualities and expressing confidence in her ability to lead the nation towards positive change. His fellow NBA figures, including Chris Paul and legends like Magic Johnson and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, similarly showed their support for Harris, advocating for a more inclusive and respectful leadership approach.On the women s basketball front, Harris also received significant support from WNBA stars like Sue Bird and Diana Taurasi, as well as from athletes outside basketball , such as tennis icon Billie Jean King and soccer star Megan Rapinoe.The Influence of Political Divides on the NBAThis year s election brought back memories of Trump s 2016 victory, a result that had deeply affected the NBA community. During Trump s previous term, numerous players and coaches spoke out against his policies, with figures like Popovich and Kerr voicing concerns about Trump's rhetoric on race and social justice. The league became a platform for activism, with events such as the Milwaukee Bucks boycott in 2020 marking a pivotal moment in sports-driven social justice advocacy.NBA's Engagement in Civic Participation: A New TraditionThe NBA's election day suspension reflects the league s ongoing push for civic engagement and its stance on social justice. The league has been a strong supporter of voter participation initiatives, like LeBron James 's More Than A Vote, which combats voter suppression and encourages youth turnout. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James ' wife Savannah James recently shared a story about a wild moment when she almost confronted a man over her son, Bronny James. Her motherly instinct was at its peak as she rushed to confront the heckler. Savannah revealed that the incident happened during one of Bronny s basketball games and there was tension in the game. Savannah s story is a definite account of a mother going to extreme lengths for their kids.Savannah James almost had a brutal fight with a man over her son Bronny JamesThere is no shortage of toxic fans in any sports league around the globe but some cross the limit which propels some stars or their family to respond. And you know how you say you gotta be an adult and talk to the people head on, I marched up them bleachers with Zhuri, little tiny Zhuri. She continued, "What am I going to do? I'm talking to a man, 'So you flicking my son off? That's what we doing at this game?' 'Oh, no. I was doing that to my son, that's what we do.' Okay, so now you crazy. You just randomly flipping your son off while playing the game, that's odd." LeBron James son has followed in his father s footsteps to become a basketball player. Reach PLC All Rights Reserved fLayoutInCell 1 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 75 Section: AMERICAN SPORTS; Version:1 Length: 467 words Byline: By, Alexander Abnos Highlight: Los Angeles Lakers icon LeBron James could be set to retire from the NBA and professional basketball as soon as next season -according to ESPN NBA insider Shams Charania Body_71 Body_71 Body fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James could call time on his basketball career as early as next season, according to ESPN NBA insider Shams Charania. The revelation was made during a live TV discussion with Stephen A Smith about the Los Angeles Lakers' prospects of clinching this season's NBA title. Currently in his 22nd NBA season, James has steered the Lakers to a promising start with a 7-4 record after 11 games. However, the LA team have been equally, if not more, in the spotlight due to the inclusion of James' son Bronny, who was drafted by the Lakers and became the first son of an NBA player to play alongside his father in a game. LeBron, who turns 40 at the end of this year, is believed by Charania to have one more season left before he retires for good. LeBron James does what Nick Bosa refused to with Kamala Harris comments Andy Carroll wants to emulate LeBron James as ex-Liverpool striker outlines new aim If James does retire then, he will be 41 years old and will have completed 23 professional seasons. The Lakers, despite accusations of nepotism from NBA observers, have assembled a squad that Charania believes could vie for a championship this season. With Anthony Davis as the linchpin, new head coach JJ Redick has implemented a system that appears to leverage the strengths of his superstars while creating opportunities for role players. James' decision to bring Redick on board has seemingly paid dividends, with the NBA star currently boasting the highest three-pointer shooting percentage .459 and free throw percentage .789 of his career. Although he's not on the court as much as in his early days, James still clocks about 35 minutes per game and is averaging the second-highest assists per game in his career. On the other hand, Davis is leading the Lakers in points per game and rebounds per game, remaining relatively injury-free after a series of setbacks over the years. Averaging just under 35 minutes per game, Davis has been hailed by James as a potential MVP candidate. Currently, Davis and James are on a mission to secure their second NBA championship as teammates, having clinched the 2020 title in the bubble following the suspension of that year's NBA season due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Another championship would see James bagging five total championships, making him the first player in NBA history to achieve this feat across three different teams. In related news, the Los Angeles Lakers are considering a trade pitch for a $60 million NBA centre to complement Anthony Davis. Reach PLC All Rights Reserved fLayoutInCell 1 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 75 82 Section: TOPIC -SUNDERLAND; Version:3 Length: 519 words Byline: By, Graeme Whitfield Highlight: Fulwell 73 has announced a "mega-merger" with the SpringHill Company, which was co-founded by one of America's biggest sports stars Body_181 Body_181 Body fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The company planning a \'a3450m film studio in Sunderland is to merge with a production company set up by American basketball legend LeBron James . Fulwell 73, the makers of sports documentary Sunderland 'Til I Die and the forthcoming reboot of the Byker Grove children's TV show, is merging with the SpringHill Company, which was set up by LeBron James and his business partner Maverick Carter and has produced a number of award-winning programmes. The deal has been described in the US as a "mega-merger" which will create a major player in the film and TV industry. It has come as work continues to set up the Crown Works Studio on Sunderland's riverside, which aims to create thousands of jobs and bring a number of lucrative TV and film productions to the North East. The Crown Works project, which has seen Fulwell 73 raise \'a3450m with finance partners Cain International, won backing from the previous Government in last year's Budget, with the scheme confirmed by new Chancellor Rachel Reeves last month. It is hoped that construction work can begin on the project before the end of this year. The merger between Fulwell 73 and SpringHill will also see the combined firm raise $40m \'a331.6m from its existing investors to help the new company grow. Fulwell 73 partners Ben Winston, Leo Pearlman, Ben Turner, Gabe Turner and James Corden released a statement saying: "Since Fulwell 73's inception, we've tried to make content that entertains, informs, and excites, while sparking conversations around the world. That's the mantra at The SpringHill Company as well. "Together, we'll continue doing what we do best, and now we'll do it as a global entertainment studio with more to offer our partners. We recognize the major shifts under way in entertainment and the world and we're excited to build a company with The SpringHill Company that's designed for the future." Maverick Carter, CEO of The SpringHill Company, added: "At The SpringHill Company, we've always given athletes, artists and brands a platform to tell meaningful and diverse stories in all forms and formats, from digital-native shows and IRL experiences to streamed series and theatrically released movies. "We found like-minded partners in Fulwell 73, partners who understand this business and the critical importance of building opportunities for talent across the media and entertainment landscape. Like everyone, we see the ways audience behavior is shifting and the demands on storytellers are evolving. We are excited for those changes and feel ready to adapt alongside the Fulwell 73 team." Fulwell 73 -which is named in homage to Sunderland's FA Cup win in 1973 -was founded in 2005 and has produced a number of high profile TV shows including The Kardashians, Carpool Karaoke and The Grammys. The SpringHill Company has produced programmes including Bafta-award winning drama Top Boy, a number of American sports documentaries and game show The Wall. LeBron James is one of America's biggest sports stars, winning three Olympic gold medals and being the leading points scorer in the history of the National Basketball Association. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 New Delhi, Nov. 23 -- LeBron James , American professional basketball player for the Los Angeles Lakers of the National Basketball Association, announced that he is stepping away from social media for the time being. The 39-year-old Lakers star shared his decision with his 52.9 million followers on X (formerly Twitter), saying, "And with that said I'll holla at y'all! Getting off social media for the time being. Y'all take care." LeBron James ' decision comes after he reposted a tweet from Rich Kleiman, the long-time manager of NBA player Kevin Durant, who criticized the negative nature of sports media coverage. Kleiman expressed frustration with the constant focus on negativity in sports journalism, arguing that sports should be a unifying force in society. "We can all acknowledge that sports is the last part of society that universally brings people together. So why can't the coverage do the same?" Kleiman wrote, calling on sports media to shift its focus away from "clickbait" negativity. James, in agreement, posted a screenshot of Kleiman's tweet with the caption, "AMEN!!" The LA Lakers star, the NBA's all-time points leader, has faced online criticism, particularly regarding his comments and opinions about other players. In a recent post-game interview, James addressed the backlash he often receives, notably about his comments on rookie Dalton Knecht's performance. Following the Lakers' victory over Utah on Tuesday, in which Knecht scored 37 points, James referenced the online critics who accuse him of lying about player performances. "Everybody on the internet calls me a liar all the time," James said. "They say I lie about everything. So what am I now? I've been said it. I watched him. I watched Tennessee a lot." LeBron James ' break from social media comes as the Lakers, currently 10-4 and riding a six-game winning streak, prepare for their next home game against Orlando on Thursday. James' hiatus signals his ongoing frustration with online negativity, and his break from social media suggests a desire to focus on his game and off-court pursuits without the distraction of public scrutiny. NBA stars must be worried because a new rival is coming from Japan. Its surname is Toyota, like the Japanese car brand, and its name is CUE6, an artificial intelligence-powered robot that is the product of years of development to create a machine capable of shooting from almost 25 meters.

The Toyota CUE6 is the result of years of research by the Japanese firm that have gone from building a robot that could only shoot from four meters, to a Guinness record for the longest drive to the basket by a humanoid robot, with an exact mark of 24.55 meters, just shy of the 29 meters of a regulation NBA court.

"We set out to surprise the world by shooting from long range. And so, we took on the challenge of shooting from this distance. Using Artificial Intelligence based on the robot's structure, it learned and thought about the style of shooting that would be most effective. As a result, I believe it gave rise to the current shooting form," said Tomohiro Nomi, head of the CUE project at Toyota.

The history of the basketball robot, the Toyota CUE project

The Toyota CUE project began in 2017 when inside the company the first prototype model was developed with simple Lego pieces. The CUE1 debuted on March 28 becoming the first robot to be registered as a professional player wearing the number 70. The first model weighed 75 kilos and measured 1.90 meters.

On November 24 of the same year, the CUE2 debuted, which could stand on its own feet and perform measured throws. Its weight had been reduced to 56 kilograms and its height was already 2.04 meters.

For 2019, two generations arrived: CUE3 and CUE4. With these models they achieved the Guinness World Record for most consecutive free throws for a humanoid robot (assisted) with 2 thousand 20 throws. The human record is 5,221, set in 1996.

In addition, thanks to an autonomous movement system, the throw time was reduced from 12 to 3 seconds.

The CUE5 arrived in 2021 and showed the ability to dribble past opponents.

Now, the CUE6 has been equipped with artificial intelligence and with this mechanism it is able to learn from its mistakes to better adapt its aim by correcting posture, hand position, strength, among other parameters, all in real time.

This is how the new Toyota CUE6 set the Guinness record for the longest distance shot for a humanoid robot at 24.55 meters.

But achieving these records, making the robot stand up on its own, hold the ball on its own, among other movements, is not the ultimate goal. The great ambition of the project is that it can perform a dunk and thus demonstrate that it can be like a real basketball player.

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James has set off alarm bells in the Los Angeles Lakers, in the United States, as the experienced forward will not play in the key game against the Timberwolves for an unprecedented reason. The Olympic champion was absent in the win against Portland last Sunday and suffered discomfort in his left foot against Miami Heat. Therefore, the coaching staff already has LeBron's replacement in mind for the game against Minnesota and the Grizzlies. Lakers fans are concerned about the team's irregular performance and now without their main star could have an impact on the outcome of the year. However, the absence of the legendary basketball player would not be so serious. In any case, Austin Reeves would be the replacement for LeBron James , after 5 games out due to a pelvic hematoma resulting from a fall against Oklahoma City. This John F. Kennedy 50-cent coin is worth US$10,000 in the U.S.: how to tell if you have one Why LeBron James would not play against Timberwolves for the NBA? Personal and physical reasons. JJ Redick , coach of the reigning NBA Cup champions, explained after Thursday's practice that the team must be careful with LeBron James ' minutes load as the season progresses. "We have to be aware, as we play more games, of the cumulative effect of playing a lot of minutes. And on Sunday, with the foot strike, we felt it was a good opportunity for him to rest," Redick revealed. The four-time NBA preseason champion said that playing 82 games in the season was a personal challenge, although the coach acknowledged that they will make the best decision so as not to physically harm the player or the team. In the 23 games he has played this season, the Olympic champion has averaged 23 points, 8.0 rebounds and 9.1 assists. When would LeBron James play again with the Los Angeles Lakers in the NBA? The legendary basketball player would return only next Sunday, December 15 against Memphis Grizzlies. Thus, he would have a rest of 8 days without playing between that game and the previous one he played. Likewise, he did not practice on Monday, as JJ Redick gave the whole team the day off, and on Tuesday he did not attend an optional training session either. "In games he's asked to be substituted a couple of times because he was exhausted," Redick revealed about the small forward, explaining that as a coach he has to be aware of the wear and tear that comes with the accumulation of games at 38 years of age. How are the Lakers heading into the key game against the Timberwolves? The Lakers will face the Timberwolves in the NBA on Friday night in a clash between the eighth and ninth teams in the Western Conference, two teams with very similar records (13-11 and 12-11) that, although they just played a little more than 25% of their season, look like they will be fighting all year for a spot in the Playoffs. What time do the Timberwolves vs. Lakers play in NBA 2024? The game will take place at 8:00 p.m. (U.S. Eastern Time) from the Target Center in Minneapolis, Minnesota . fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 With the buzz around the retirement of the Lakers star LeBron James , the 39-year-old athlete gave another reason to his fans and critics believe that he is still very much into the action. During a recent face-off with the Sacramento Kings, the NBA legend went on to add another feather to his cap. He created a noteworthy record after defeating the Kings with an overall score of 113-110. LeBron James Breaks NBA Record For The Regular Season MinutesThe Los Angeles Lakers whitewashed the entire team of the Sacramento Kings during a regular season match. LeBron James terrific performance on the court helped the Los Angeles Lakers register the victory under their name. The Lakers alum secured 19 points along with seven assists and six rebounds which led him to breaking the NBA record for the regular season minutes. His 34 minutes on the court against the Sacramento Kings helped him move past the Kareem Abdul-Jaffar s record. LeBron James 34 minutes added up to 57,471 whereas the reputed basketball athletes' record-breaking regular season minutes are 57,446. Los Angeles Lakers Star LeBron James Addresses His Retirement RumorsNBA star LeBron James retirement has been the talk of the sports world for a long time. From talking about his age to his under performance in a few matches during the 2024-25 NBA season- fans and sports analysts leave no chance when it comes to talking about the Lakers alum. During an interview with the Bleacher Report in November 2024, the NBA star opened up on the speculations around his retirement. LeBron James indirectly pointed out that very soon he would be hanging the Lakers jersey on its door. He said- "Obviously, it's the mind; wherever my mind is, is how the rest of my body is going to go, whatever the case may be, I don't know. I'm not going to play that much longer, to be completely honest," While talking about his NBA timeline, James revealed-"One year, two years, whatever the case may be. I said the other night that I'm not playing until the wheels fall off. I'm not. I'm not going to be that guy. I'm not going to be the guy disrespecting the game because I just want to be out on the floor."Bron Clearly pointed out that he probably has only 1-2 years left as a basketball athlete. But as we all know all good things come to an end and even LeBron James would have to bid goodbye to the basketball court someday. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 READ MORE: LeBron James and son Bronny attend star-studded dinner in Miami LeBron James had a proud girl-dad moment through social media on Friday, posting a roll of photos of his 10-year-old daughter Zhuri as she begins her volleyball career. In the photos, Zhuri can be seen with a tribute to her dad's NBA career, wearing No 6 in the blue and white uniforms. Her NBA legend father wore the number as a member of the Miami Heat, Los Angeles Lakers, and Team USA. LeBron and Savannah James know their children are under the spotlight by having one of the best athletes of all-time for a father. Yet, they have not tried to hide their kids from the camera, partially because of their athletic careers. Oldest child Bronny James was a star at USC before being drafted by the Lakers this year to be on the same team as his did. Bryce James is a star at prep powerhouse Sierra Canyon and has an offer from Ohio State men's basketball . Bronny wore No 6 with the Trojans, but has worn No 9 in the NBA. Bryce has never worn one of his dad's jersey numbers, opting for Nos 5 and 30 with the Trailblazers. 'YOU GO GIRL!!!!!! @allthingszhuri LET'S GO BABY Z!! Best of luck this season and GO CRAZY!! #JamesGang,' LeBron said in the caption of the photos. Zhuri James is listed in the Class of 2023, meaning Division-I schools could have her on official visits in five years. If Zhuri excels, the phrase 'blocked by James' could take on a whole new meaning. Instead of stuffing Andre Iguodala, it would be Zhuri denying an opponent across the net. On Thursday night, LeBron James set an NBA record for most minutes of any player in league history, surpassing former Los Angeles great Kareem Abdul-Jabbar to seal another incredible all-time mark. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Los Angeles Lakers star and NBA legend LeBron James has always been highly supportive of his children and he has never been shy to show it in public. He has once again shown his love and support for his children and this time, it s for his youngest, Zhuri James. While his sons, Bronny and Bryce James, have chosen basketball as their path, Zhuri has chosen to walk a different route and entered the world of volleyball. This marks a new chapter for the James family as Zhuri begins her journey in a sport outside of her father s legendary NBA career.On December 20, 2024, LeBron posted a series of photos on his Instagram of Zhuri in her volleyball attire, expressing his excitement and pride. He captioned the post: "YOU GO GIRL!!!!!! @allthingszhuri LET'S GO BABY Z!! Best of luck this season and GO CRAZY!!" LeBron fully supports his daughter s aspirations, no matter the sport and he made it clear with this Instagram post.Zhuri, who celebrated her 10th birthday earlier this year, is the third and youngest child of LeBron and his wife, Savannah. Despite the massive athletic legacies of both her parents, Zhuri has decided to make a name for herself and create her own identity in sports, choosing volleyball over basketball . This is completely different from what her elder brothers chose for themselves. Her decision has been met with full backing from both LeBron and Savannah, who are both dedicated to supporting all their children in their individual pursuits. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Christmas Lottery Write your number AmountCheck tenthOFFERED BY:Rodrigo Terrasa MadridLuis Blasco MadridIsmael Poveda Logro\'f1oMadridMadridLogro\'f1oUpdated Sunday,22December2024-16:46 Between the Do\'f1a Manolita lottery administration, right in the center of Madrid, and the Club Deportivo Distrito Ol\'edmpico, in the San Blas-Canillejas district of the capital, there are approximately 10 kilometers of distance, about 20 minutes by cab. Enough time for the cab driver who was taking a journalist from EL MUNDO to the basketball club where half of the Gordo jackpot has fallen to discover that he is also a millionaire all of a sudden. "It's unbelievable. I was bringing you here to see the people who have won the prize, my wife was calling me on the phone all the way, and when I arrive I discover that I also had a ticket, that I have won 40,000 euros! ", said Ra\'fal -that's the driver's name- as soon as he parked the cab at the zero zone of the prize. "I'm really sorry, but I'm leaving you here working and I'm going home to celebrate". Ra\'fal bought a ticket from a basketball club on the other side of town "by chance", through the daughter of a friend of his wife's daughter. And today he is the perfect example of one of the day's quintessential clich\'e9s: with you, a very well distributed Gordo. The first prize in the Extraordinary Christmas Lottery Draw, number 72,480 - worth 4,000,000 euros per series - was sold entirely in Logro\'f1o's administration number 6 , known as Loter\'eda Mar\'eda del Carmen, but approximately half of the prize traveled to the basketball club Distrito Ol\'edmpico , in one of Madrid's poorest neighborhoods. "We are one of the biggest clubs in the Community by number of licenses - more than 900 in 54 teams - but we are very humble, a club with hardly any assets ," says I\'f1aki del Castillo, the club's director of operations. "Many of the families who play here are working-class families and there are many people who needed it." El Gordo came out at 11:27 a.m. and in barely half an hour the club's doors were already full of club directors, players, coaches, family members and bottles of champagne. Here is Alejandro, coach of one of the teams and student of Law and Criminology, who says he will use his 120,000 to pay for his degree. Or Ra\'fal, head of the club's office, who tells us that for more than 40 years they have been buying their ballots at the same office in Logro\'f1o, even though no one really knows why . "It's been done that way all our lives, but we don't know what the origin was". Or Nicole, a 1.87m power forward, who signed up last year from Logro\'f1o, like El Gordo: "I've come back home and brought luck". Or \'c1ngel, the vice-president, who says that the biggest prize they had won before the Gordo was the two euro jackpot. "Today's prize was not part of our plans and it is the best thing in the world. We have been working for more than 40 years, trying to educate and generate good habits through basketball and, despite the money now, we don't want to be anything else". How much money has the club been able to distribute? "We don't know. We tried to calculate it with our cell phone, but it came out to be a very large number, eight ," jokes one of the coaches. \'c1ngel Francisco Iglesias, the vice-president of the District, dreams of being able to build his own pavilion for his teams and, who knows, maybe make an addition: "Here the players don't get paid, but maybe now we can sign Lebron James , if only to play a quarter". The C.D. Distrito Ol\'edmpico sold more than 1,000 tickets at four euros with two numbers. One of them was 72,480. A few years ago they played with four and here God blesses the "genius" who decided to go down to half. Javier, father of one of the players, explains that now it is time to "plug holes and create new ones" . And Miguel, probably the most dedicated father in the club, says that he will use his millions to buy another hype because the one he has is going to burst. We can vouch for that. All the people in the club, most of them wearing the official tracksuit, chant that next year they will play in the ACB, repeat the chants of the matches in a loop and bellow a song that goes like this: "The shirt I wear, I wear it stuck to my skin because I love it and I love it and I'll never take it off". Everyone is a little richer today than yesterday. Well, not everyone. Here, in the middle of the festival and the hype and under a shower of cava, is Daniel. He introduces himself: "Yes, I'm the one who didn't get it" . Daniel is a trainer, he says that this year he was short of money and decided not to play. "Now I'm really pulling my hair out..... But well, we have health, we have happiness and that's what matters," he says. "Seeing all these people happy is my prize." -No one in your family bought? -My girl does have ballots. I already loved her very much, but

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 EfeUpdated Thursday,26December2024-11:13 The Minnesota Timberwolves defeated the Dallas Mavericks 99-105 on Wednesday, who lost Luka Doncic to injury and nearly came from 28 points down in the fourth quarter. Doncic left the court in pain with 2:31 left before halftime with an apparent left calf strain. Dallas (19-11) was already down 38-51 at that point. For the Timberwolves (15-14), Anthony Edwards scored 26 points and collected 8 rebounds. Julius Randle collected 10 rebounds and dished out 8 assists. Minnesota got to +28 (62-90) with 1:23 left in the third quarter, a difference that seemed insurmountable for the Mavericks without Doncic, but led by Kyrie Irving and with a 37-11 run, the Mavericks were only 2 down (99-101) with 1:05 to go. In the final moments, Edwards made a basket to make it 99-103 with 19 seconds left. Klay Thompson missed the three-pointer on the next possession, forcing Dallas to foul Randle, who made both of his free throws for 99-105. Irving scored 39 points in the loss for Dallas. Doncic made 14 before leaving the court and Klay Thompson made 12 with 4 three-pointers. Mavericks-Timberwolves was one of 5 games the NBA scheduled this year for Christmas Day, the highlight of the basketball league's regular season. Warriors-Lakers A layup by Austin Reaves with one second to play gave the Los Angeles Lakers a vibrant 115-113 win at the Golden State Warriors , in a Christmas game marked by a real 'show' between LeBron James and Steph Curry . Reaves crashed a recital between two basketball legends like LeBron and Curry, who added a new spectacular chapter, in their 53rd meeting, to a rivalry that marked the history of the NBA. LeBron James signed a double-double of 31 points and ten assists and Curry almost worked a miracle in the fourth period with a three-pointer festival that ended without reward. His game ended with 38 points (8 of 15 from the arc) and six assists. In the other games, the Knicks beat the Wembanyama Spurs (117-114) at Madison Square Garden in New York, the Sixers beat the Celtics (118-114) and the Suns beat the Nuggets (110-100). He said- But hopefully, the future s bright, and I m going to Keep Pounding all the way through it, Steph Curry has been a die-hard fan of the Carolina Panthers ever since his childhood. For him, the franchise is still one of his biggest passions, and reportedly he was in talks to become one owner of the NFL team. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The 'King' LeBron James celebrates his 40th birthday on Monday, a symbol of the unique longevity of the Los Angeles Lakers star, who continues to explore multiple horizons while his career seems to have no end in sight. - Retirement with no date set - After starting his twenty-second NBA season in October, the word 'retirement' is not yet part of LeBron James ' vocabulary. In July, he signed a new two-year contract with the Lakers, worth around 100 million dollars, with the second season worth 52 million dollars at his discretion. And the Lakers, where he has been playing since 2018, are of course doing everything possible to pamper their star. This summer they selected his son Bronny James in the draft, a young man whose future in elite basketball is still uncertain, opening up the possibility of forming the first father-son duo to share the same court in the NBA. After two uneven seasons, the franchise with 17 titles fired coach Darvin Ham in June to hire JJ Redick, a former player with no previous coaching experience but a close friend of LeBron James , with whom he hosted a podcast. LeBron only mentioned the possibility of his retirement in the summer of 2023, after the heart accident suffered by his son Bronny. "At that point I was exhausted, mentally I wasn't there anymore," he confessed. - New records in sight - The "King" left an indelible mark on the history of the NBA by overtaking Kareem Abdul-Jabbar in February 2023 to become the all-time leading scorer, before reaching the symbolic figure of 40,000 points in March this year. The counter continues to advance (as of December 27, he has accumulated 41,131 points), with the intention of taking this record to unattainable heights. At the start of his 22nd season, he equaled Vince Carter's record and will surpass it if he decides to extend his career. His longevity also allows him to dream of the record for the most games played. He currently ranks fifth (1,520), not far off Robert Parish's record (1,611), which he could also surpass if he extends his career for another season. The fourth best passer in history (11,261), James, who has averaged 9 assists this season, will have a hard time getting on the podium, as Jason Kidd ranks third with 12,091 assists, far from John Stockton's unattainable record (15,806). Although he holds the record for points, LeBron still doesn't have the record for baskets made, a record that seems attainable: he has scored 15,088, behind Kareem Abdul-Jabbar (15,837). - Still a title contender? - Four-time champion LeBron James hasn't won a new ring since 2020, when he became champion in the NBA bubble in Orlando during the Covid pandemic. Since then, the Lakers have been disappointing in the regular season (7th, 11th, 7th and 7th), reaching the Western Conference Finals in 2023 but being eliminated in the first round of the playoffs at the end of April. This season, after a good start, he's back to his usual level, suffering some humiliating defeats, both in play and in attitude. "I'm embarrassed," declared JJ Redick after a humiliating defeat in Miami at the beginning of December (134-93). According to leading sports media outlet The Athletic, "the Lakers need one or two player trades to have a shot at the title this season". Despite continuing to be essential with an average of 23.5 points, LeBron's 'ratio' (average overall performance) has nevertheless been largely negative this season. His star duo with Anthony Davis lacks support and is criticized for its intermittent defensive involvement. - Las Vegas ambition - After his career, LeBron James has made no secret of his desire to become the owner of an NBA franchise, especially in Las Vegas, where he plans to obtain a team in the event of an expansion that could take place following the signing of a new television rights agreement. LeBron spoke to ESPN about this project as his "ultimate goal". A partner in the Fenway Sports Group fund, which owns Liverpool in the English Premier League, James already has stakes in the northern English club, the Boston Red Sox (baseball) and the Pittsburgh Penguins (field hockey).
