Independent Digital News and Media Limited All Rights Reserved fLayoutInCell 1 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 75 82 Length: 643 words Byline: Janie McCauley Highlight: When he was younger, Stephen Curry always heard the scrutiny about his size and stature and how he looked years younger than most everybody else coming out of Davidson College Body_178 Body_178 Body fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Oh yes, HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/stephen-curry 00 Stephen Curry always heard the noise, the constant scrutiny about his size and stature and how he looked years younger than most everybody else coming out of college. He decided to embrace it, to make it his mantra: Underrated. And now one of the NBA s biggest stars is sharing the story of his basketball beginnings and how being doubted eventually ended up fueling his pursuit to become one of the greatest players ever for the HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/golden-state-warriors 00 Golden State Warriors . He still carries that chip when he takes the court today. That underrated kind of mindset and being undersized and all that was a badge of honor at a certain point and you kind of flip it on the head, Curry said this week ahead of Friday s release of his HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/apple 00 Apple TV+ documentary, " Stephen Curry : Underrated. That s where the faith of, OK if I apply myself I can be as good as the next guy even though I don t look the part. He led Davidson College's improbable run to the NCAA Tournament Elite Eight in 2008. A year later when Curry went head-to-head with Patty Mills in a second-round NIT game as a junior at Davidson and lost at Saint Mary s College, little did the basketball -crazed Bay Area realize the dynamic point guard was simply providing a preview of what he had in store at the next level right here in Northern California. Curry believed he could be a star. He was driven by the chatter everywhere he went that he was too small and might not make it in the HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/nba 00 NBA he d already earned The Baby-Faced Assassin nickname years earlier as a schoolboy in Toronto. I definitely knew, I definitely heard it, he said. I fought it for a while, but then you finally embrace it and then you turn it into kind of an unlock of, OK, this is my challenge, this is my reality,' but what can I do to kind of overcome the challenges of trying to be successful at this craft where in theory it might require you to look a certain way or have a certain physical attribute or whatever the case was. "It unlocked for me a work ethic in developing my skill set that has carried me through my entire career. The national scoring leader in his final college season, Curry finished that showdown against Mills with 26 points, nine rebounds and five assists on March 23, 2009, in Moraga. All these years later he plays across the bay from the suburb in the East Bay hills. Now a two-time MVP, NBA Finals MVP last year and four-time NBA champion, Curry s latest big project will focus on his time before turning pro. I feel like it s a great moment of reflection. We talk a little bit about the NBA accomplishments, but it all is through the lens of what helped me develop that underrated mindset while I was at Davidson and why I still talk about it and still refer to it even now," he said. "It s always a part of my DNA because that s how I truly learned how to approach the game and find my own identity in this world. In May of last year, Curry completed his Bachelor of Arts degree in sociology at Davidson and received his diploma. He still cherishes everything he learned from Davidson coach Bob McKillop, who typically comes to see Curry play about once a season. Reflecting on his college years and even hearing others recall their memories of those special seasons has meant a lot to the 35-year-old Curry, the league s all-time 3-point leader who has emerged as one of the biggest faces in professional sports. Curry hopes that his story could motivate others who have been overlooked in pursuing whatever their passion might be. It was always the question of Why now? Curry said of making the film. It was having the opportunity to inspire people from all different walks, not even just a basketball or a sports documentary but something that can be applicable to life and any walk of life and people of various backgrounds." ___ HYPERLINK https://www.independent.co.uk/topic/ap 00 AP NBA: HYPERLINK https://apnews.com/hub/nba 00 https://apnews.com/hub/nba and HYPERLINK https://twitter.com/AP_Sports 00 https://twitter.com/AP_Sports Classification_178 Classification_178 Classification fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Language: ENGLISH Publication-Type: Newspaper; Web Publication Journal Code: WEBI Subject: BASKETBALL (90%); COLLEGE & UNIVERSITY SPORTS (90%); ATHLETES (89%); BASKETBALL TOURNAMENT

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 When it comes to stories of self-improvement, the story of one of the NBA's favorite stars, Stephen Curry , is at least unparalleled. So appealing is his biography, both for basketball fans and documentary film buffs, that the trailer, Underrated, directed by Peter Nicks and starring Stephen Curry himself, grabs you in just one minute. Curry was selected by the Golden State Warriors with the seventh overall pick in the 2009 NBA draft with a style of play based on three-pointers and speed. A four-time NBA champion, he won two consecutive NBA MVP awards, an annual award given by the NBA to the most valuable player in the regular league, in one of which he became the first player in history to win the award unanimously with 125 votes. In addition, he was selected nine times to play in the annual NBA All-Stars Weekend exhibition. As if all this were not enough, Curry holds the record for most three-pointers in league history. But his is a story of effort and achievement that is inspiring for those who put off their desire to develop in elite sports because they don't meet the expected standard. At just 6-foot-4 and boyish in appearance, the documentary chronicles his start as a college player, helping lead the Warriors to their first NBA championship in 40 years in 2015. The film is produced by Peter Nicks and Ryan Coogler through Proximity Media, Erick Peyton through Curry's Unanimous Media and Sean Havey, Ben Cotner and Marissa Torres Ericson. Sev Ohanian, Zinzi Coogler and Emily Osborne are executive producers. The series is currently available exclusively on the Apple TV+ platform. Read also: NBA: Los Angeles Lakers to retire LeBron James' number when he retires from basketball These players have been playing together for several seasons now and have gradually become so comfortable with each other that they know how to move their teammates to create plays and get baskets. Undoubtedly, Curry's understudy will be Paul, who has a lot of basketball experience and will be a great help for the bench, in short, another mentor who could also be included as a starter on some occasions. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The two-time MVP appeared in the music video; the feature for his documentary Stephen Curry : Underrated premiered on Apple TV+ July 21 and is still available DailyMail.com provides all the latest international sports news It turns out Stephen Curry can do it all. The Warriors phenom starred in a music video, lip-syncing rap lyrics for Tobe Nwigwe's latest single 'Lil' Fish, Big Pond'. Curry shared the official video for the song Wednesday -- which is the featured track for his Apple TV+ documentary; Stephen Curry : Underrated. In the video, Curry first appears on a boat, with his head down while sporting a bucket hat. The four-time NBA champion then lifts his head to reveal his identity before lip-syncing Nwigwe's icy bars, written specifically for the sharpshooter. 'My my my, look how things have changed,' the verse begins. 'They know I'm the one. Daddy taught me how to flick my wrist, I'm my father's son. 'Think I'm pistol packin' how that ratchet on me like a gun. They should put the basket in the casket after I am done.' Naturally, the song is grounded in a celebration of Curry and his journey from an unheralded recruit at Davidson to an overlooked NBA prospect, and of course, his status as the greatest shooter of a basketball in human history. The chorus aptly exhibits this process from No. 7 overall pick in the 2009 Draft to four-time champ and two-time MVP (one of those unanimous). 'Lil' fish, big pond, big splash, big pond. Look like the lil' fish in the big pond, made the big splash and got the big bag.' The 109-minute documentary premiered July 21 and is currently available on Apple TV+. According to ESPN sources, the Lakers small forward plans to join the USA Basketball team for the Paris 2024 Olympics. In addition, it appears that James will not be returning alone, but will recruit other big-name players. The rumors surfaced after James reacted to a Sportscenter post on Instagram. The post showed a layout of the possible team for the tournament in Paris, and among the featured figures were names such as Stephen Curry , Devin Booker, Damian Lillard, Kevin Durant, Jayson Tatum, Jimmy Butler, Kawhi Leonard and Anthony Davis. This ambitious project to form a dream team has generated great expectation among basketball fans. Possible names to join LeBron James in Paris In addition to LeBron James, several big-name players could return to represent the United States in Paris. Anthony Davids and Stephen Curry are among those mentioned as possible additions to the U.S. Olympic team. James, 38, won a bronze in 2004 and gold in 2008 and 2012. Davis won gold in 2012 and was part of the World Cup-winning team when he last played for Team USA in 2014. Other players have also mentioned their interest in joining Team US at the Olympics: Jayson Tatum Devin Booker Draymond Green Damian Lillard Devin Booker De'Aaron Fox Kyrie Irving Chris Paul Kevin Durant Team USA looks to overcome obstacles The U.S. basketball team has dominated the Olympics, winning all but one gold medal since 1992. However, changes in the schedule and the COVID-19 pandemic have made it difficult for elite players to participate in recent international tournaments. In addition, their disappointing performance at the FIBA 2023 World Cup leaves them wanting more for Paris 2024. Despite these circumstances, Grant Hill, executive director of USA Basketball , stated that playing in this year's World Cup is not a requirement to be considered for the Olympic team. Bobby Marks reports that Doncic would be in line to sign a five-year super extension projected at $318 million . What does Doncic have to do to become the first $70 million NBA player? In the United States, they highlight that in order to get that historic, million-dollar salary Luka Doncic has made merits such as being a four-time member of the NBA's Ideal Five (2020, 2021, 2022 and 2023) before the age of 25 (something only Kevin Durant, Tim Duncan and Max Zaslofsky achieved) and being the only player next to LeBron James to reach 9,000 points and 2,500 assists before the age of 25. Luka Doncic theoretically could not sign that contract extension, as he would have been in the NBA for just six years, but the former Real Madrid star meets an exception. If Luka Doncic were selected in the 'NBA's Ideal Five', he would meet the criteria of being 'All-NBA' in two of the three immediately preceding seasons, being eligible to sign a contract extension in 2025 that would take effect for the 2026-27 season. The U.S. network notes that if the salary cap increases by 10% over the next three years, Doncic's extension would be $367.5 million and his final year of the contract would be $83.6 million. His last season Luka Doncic signed averages of 32.4 points, 8.6 rebounds and 8 assists in the NBA. The 5 highest paid players in the NBA for the 2023-2024 season 1. Stephen Curry - 51.9 million 2. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The 2023/2024 NBA season, the world's leading basketball league, begins next week. Ahead of its start, the franchises have made some important moves: Damian Lillard was signed by the Milwaukee Bucks, while Jrue Holiday moved to Boston and will play for the Celtics. Even so, at least in the list of the highest-paid athletes, two names continue to reign supreme: LeBron James, of the Los Angeles Lakers, and Stephen Curry , of the Golden State Warriors, top the ranking devised by Forbes. LeBron tops the list with US$117.6 million a year. The figure takes into account his salary with the Lakers and his businesses away from the court, such as sponsorships and private investments. He had already become the first active athlete to make Forbes' list of billionaires. In 2023, he will be behind only Roger Federer and Cristiano Ronaldo as the world's highest-paid players - in a ranking that also includes those who have retired. No other player in NBA history has earned more on the court than the US$432 million he has accumulated in his 20 seasons as a professional. If he fulfills his contract and remains in the league next season, he will exceed US$530 million by 2025. Following him, Stephen Curry , four-time NBA champion with the Warriors, pockets US$101.9 million annually. Of this amount, US$50 million comes from his off-court business. The point guard has one of the most lucrative contracts in the league and became the first athlete to receive more than US$50 million a year in a single contract. Kevin Durant, now with the Phoenix Suns, completes the Top 3. With stints with the Warriors, Oklahoma City Thunder and Brooklyn Nets, he amasses more than US$90 million a year. The Bucks' new duo of Giannis Antetokounmpo and Damian Lillard make up the Top 5. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The records left for Stephen Curry to break are fewer and fewer. The man who changed the way we see and play basketball . The player who has changed the history of the NBA and the Golden State Warriors has a new record in mind. His great start to the season and his excellent success from three-point range ( 47.5% with 5.9 attempts per game ), invites us to think that we are on the verge of witnessing something historic . The big goal? The 500 three-pointers that seem impossible. But the point guard doesn't know that word. Looking at his numbers, he has made 47 three-pointers in seven games . 4 against Suns, 7 against Kings, 6 against Rockets, 7 against Pelicans, 4 against Kings, 5 against Thunder, 7 against Cavaliers and 7 against Pistons. These 47, if he were to hypothetically play the games, could be projected as 482 at the end of the regular season. Record after record Stephen Curry changed the way we look at basketball . The Warriors' system, with the point guard and Klay as the main culprits, targeted the three-pointer as the main threat. Taking into account the data , the five seasons with the highest number of successful three-pointers are as follows: - Stephen Curry (2015/2016): 402. - James Harden (2018/19): 378 - Stephen Curry (2018/19): 354 - Stephen Curry (2020/21): 324 - Stephen Curry (2016/17): 301 His start is devastating. We don't know if he will be able to keep up the pace, but projecting it over time he can break all existing records. At 35 years old , and in his 15th season in the NBA , he continues to show why he is one of the best players in history. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 content"class="sno-story-body-content sno-no-cap"> As the semester enters the straightaway toward our long awaited winter break, many UNI students are beginning to put their efforts into high-gear and setting winter finals into focus. This seemingly endless stretch of exams, while strenuous, offers necessary feedback to students as an indicator for how they've progressed throughout the semester. For most students, they really look at these exams to find out one thing: "Am I successful?" It's a good question to ask, but also a sensitive topic to some that may feel nervous about the future. In light of the pending conclusion to the semester, it might be helpful to refine the definition and answer the million dollar question: What is success, really? Many answers could attempt to sum this up simply; A good salary? A spotless final transcript? A 4-car garage? Being the best at what you do? Being happy? All of these are commendable achievements, but it may not fully cover the truth of success. Put success into the perspective of career accolades. More specifically, in the eyes of Stephen Curry , the best shooter of all time (objectively). Without much argument, many would easily describe Curry's career as a professional basketball player a wild success. His shooting abilities put his face on the front of ESPN articles on a regular basis. He's used his abilities to assist the Golden State Warriors to four championships within his career, but when you dive into statistics, it makes you really question the meaning of success. He has made incredible strides as a basketball player, but there are interesting things to unpack about his success. Within his entire career, Curry has shot 24,104 shots, excluding free throws. Out of those 24,104 shots? He made 11,065. That means that Stephen Curry , a renowned basketball player known for his shooting abilities, makes 45% of his shots according to ESPN's stats. And yet, we don't doubt for a second that he is successful. So, to touch base back with the initial question at hand, ask yourself: "Am I successful?" To answer this question, circle back again and define: "What is success, really?" But this time, take your personal life into account, not a distant extrapersonal example of a basketball player. Success is not all about numbers. The more you internalize that, the more you learn to appreciate the value of wins and losses. When you get your scores back over break, while they are still important, they are not direct indicators of your success as a student. Furthermore, they do not define who you are as a person. Think about this. If you hadn't been enrolled at the University of Northern Iowa, you would not have had the opportunity to take that exam. So, in that context, simply being a student is a step ahead from getting a good grade back on an exam. Additionally, if you hadn't been motivated to make the ambitious decision to go to college after graduating high school, to then decide to apply to UNI, get accepted and from there enroll in classes, you would not be in this position now awaiting test scores for a final exam. You can go further and further back to find much more success hidden in your nervousness this winter break. All of these versions of success point to a much simpler answer to the question of success. Arguably, success is waking up knowing that you are going somewhere and why. Bring this back to Stephen Curry . While his career as a professional athlete is not in direct relation to being a college student at UNI, the path he took may look eerily similar. Again, he made 45% of his shots taken. The inverse: he missed 55%. Also, this is a statistic pulled from 24,104 shots. This shows that he did not stop simply because he missed one. He had a reason why he should keep shooting. He had a motivation to keep on going somewhere even if success wasn't guaranteed after every shot. That, in itself, is success. So, following finals week, somewhat ironically keep Stephen Curry in your thoughts when you reflect on your semester. Maybe you took a lot of shots, maybe you missed a lot of them too. Take time to understand why you kept shooting. If you are going somewhere, and you know why, then your failures should hold as much value to you as your successes. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 India, Dec. 14 -- Tonatiuh Ramirez, a special needs student from Arizona, hit the headlines on Tuesday, when he was invited by Golden State Warriors superstar Stephen Curry for a game in San Francisco. Ramirez and his mother were assigned court side seats, and Curry gave him a signed jersey, allowing him to be present for the pregame warmups. Ramirez caught everyone's attention last month after he hit a game-winning half-court shot in his high school's first-ever unified intramural game. Ramirez and Curry also shared a moment before the game, with the NBA superstar giving some encouraging words to the youngster, who wants to become a professional basketball player in the future. "My dream is to be a professional basketball player and be like you," said Ramirez. Taking to X, formerly known as Twitter, Golden State Warriors shared a moment of the interaction and captioned it as, "Last month, Tonatiuh Ramirez made a game-winning half-court buzzer at his high school basketball game. "T" is on an inclusive basketball team providing opportunities for students with disabilities to pursue their passions. Steph saw T's story and invited him to tonight's game." Here is the video: Ramirez studies at the Liberty High School in Arizona and during the match, he made a shot from just the half-court line in a crowd of defenders as the game came to an end. Speaking after his viral shot, Ramirez said in an interview to 12News, "I do the best shot ever. The crowd and everything, it was amazing." After Curry saw the video and found out Ramirez idolised him, he decided to give him a surprise, which were two tickets to a Warriors game, and a personal visit from the superstar himself. "You and your family are gonna get to go to the game but you're gonna get to arrive early because there's somebody that wants to meet you there. Do you know who that is? His name's Steph Curry," a staffer at Ramirez' school said. Published by HT Digital Content Services with permission from Hindustan Times. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 There are few surprises in USA Basketball 's men's player pool for the Paris Olympics announced Tuesday, with most of the big names like LeBron James, Joel Embiid, Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant on the list. The surprise may have been who was missing. Draymond Green, who helped the U.S. win gold at the 2016 Rio de Janeiro Games and then another title at the rescheduled Tokyo Olympics in 2021, was not among the 41 names released by USA Basketball as candidates for the team that will compete in Paris this summer in search of a fifth consecutive gold medal. The naming of the pool is the first official phase in the process of assembling a 12-player Olympic roster that will be coached by Golden State's Steve Kerr and assisted by Miami's Erik Spoelstra, the Los Angeles Clippers' Tyronn Lue and Gonzaga's Mark Few. Team selection will be finalized this spring. The pool includes 13 players who already have Olympic gold medals Durant has three, James and Chris Paul each have two, while Anthony Davis, Bam Adebayo, Damian Lillard, Devin Booker, James Harden, Jayson Tatum, Jimmy Butler, Jrue Holiday, Kyrie Irving and Paul George each have one. The other 28 players on the list are headlined by a pair who may make their Olympic debuts Embiid and Curry among them. Also picked: Jarrett Allen, Paolo Banchero, Desmond Bane, Scottie Barnes, Mikal Bridges, Jaylen Brown, Jalen Brunson, Alex Caruso, Anthony Edwards, De'Aaron Fox, Aaron Gordon, Tyrese Haliburton, Josh Hart, Tyler Herro, Chet Holmgren, Brandon Ingram, Jaren Jackson Jr., Cam Johnson, Walker Kessler, Kawhi Leonard, Donovan Mitchell, Bobby Portis, Austin Reaves, Duncan Robinson, Derrick White and Trae Young. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 After the failure of the last Basketball World Cup, where they finished fourth in the Philippines, the United States wants to put together a real "Dream Team" for this year's Olympic Games in Paris. The Americans want to regain the top of this sport and that is why they released a preliminary list for the Olympic Games. There are 41 basketball players and there are big names such as LeBron James, Stephen Curry , Kevin Durant, Kawhi Leonard, Joel Embiid, Kyrie Irving, Chris Paul and Jimmy Butler, which makes fans dream, because the rest are of high quality. Now, it remains to be seen which of these superstars will decide to put their names on the flight that will take them to the French tournament. USA Basketball director Grant Hill announced the roster and it will be in May or June when coach Steve Kerr will announce the 12 chosen players. The United States has not given the importance in recent years to the World Cup, as they were seventh in 2019 and fourth just last year, but they have given the necessary weight to the Olympic Games. The NBA stars have won the gold medal in the last four Olympic Games. The last failure they had in that tournament was in Greece 2004, when they only managed to take bronze. LeBron, Durant, Curry and others who are really tough to beat on the court are expected to give it their all, otherwise another failure could be looming. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The next All Star Game in Indianapolis is coming up and promises to be the best three-point contest in the history of basketball . The protagonists of this exciting duel will be Stephen Curry and Sabrina Ionescu. Curry, a Golden State Warriors player, has won the three-point contest twice, while Ionescu took the competition in her league last year with an impressive performance: she scored 37 points out of a possible 40. With this feat, he broke the record set by Curry in 2021, who had scored 31 points. In addition, she surpassed the previous best mark in the women's league, which was 30 points scored by Allie Quigley. Challenge accepted In a conversation with teammate Brandin Podziemski, Curry expressed her intention to challenge Ionescu to take the title of the world's best three-pointer from her. "I think I have to put it to the test," Curry said. Ionescu accepted the challenge and responded on social media, "Let's do it! See you at the three-point line." The ultimate showdown This exciting showdown between two basketball legends will take place on February 17. It will be then when it will be possible to see who is really the best shooter in basketball history. Undoubtedly, this event guarantees an unparalleled spectacle for sports fans. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Just as 50 years ago (September 20, 1973) the famous tennis "Battle of the Sexes" took place, in which the legendary Billie Jean King defeated Bobby Riggs 6-4, 6-3 and 6-3, captivating the public present at the Houston Astrodome, now this show comes to basketball and it will be Stephen Curry and Sabrina Ionescu who will star in a much-anticipated clash . As a prelude to the traditional All Star Game , which pits the ideal teams of the two NBA conferences, the Golden State Warriors point guard and the New York Liberty shooting guard will face each other in the three-point contest that Curry has already won in 2015 and 2021. He will look to equal Larry Bird (1986, 1987 and 1988) and Craig Hodges (1990, 1991 and 1992) as the only ones to win three times. Ionescu, meanwhile, won last year among women in what is known in the United States as the 3-point challenge , setting an unprecedented record on that occasion: she scored 25 three-pointers out of 27 attempts (92.5% efficiency) and made 20 in a row. He finished with 37 of 40 possible points. After finishing the first round with 26 units, the native of Walnut Creek, California, completed an (almost) immaculate final round and thus surpassed Curry himself, who held the combined NBA and WNBA record with 31 points since 2021. The challenge will replicate the conditions under which they compete in their respective leagues. That is, Curry will shoot from the NBA three-point line with NBA balls, while Ionescu will do it under the conditions stipulated by the WNBA. The Golden State Warriors', ring winner in the 2014-15, 2016-17, 2017-18 and 2021-22 seasons, is the NBA's all-time leader in three-pointers with 3,577 (42.9% effectiveness), while the New York Liberty's has an average approaching him (37.7) and his career is on the rise. A show not to be missed. All-Star Game Schedule Stephen Curry and Sabrina Ionescu will be on the court at the Gainbridge Fieldhouse in Indianapolis for a weekend that will have plenty of basketball to watch. Both players are playing for charities, Curry's Eat.Learn.Play and Ionescu s SI20 Foundation will receive donations from the NBA and WNBA. The one-on-one matchup between Curry and Ionescu will take place after the league's three-point contest on Feb. 17. Both players, known for their lethal ability behind the arc, will be shooting from different distances, following specific NBA rules. Curry vs. Ionescu in the All Star Game 2024 According to the rules provided by the NBA, Curry will use NBA basketballs from the league's three-point line, while Ionescu will use WNBA balls from the WNBA three-point line. All eyes will be on him when he faces Ionescu in this highly anticipated showdown, an event that will undoubtedly highlight NBA All-Star weekend. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Stephen Curry is headed back to NBA All-Star weekend. Sabrina Ionescu will be waiting for him. The shootout that both of them wanted is officially a reality, the NBA announced Tuesday. "Stephen vs. Sabrina" will happen as part of All-Star Saturday Night in Indianapolis on Feb. 17. Curry, the NBA's all-time 3-point king, will take on the reigning WNBA 3-point shootout queen and single-season record holder Ionescu. "Stage is set! Let's get it," Curry posted on social media. Ionescu responded simply: "Let's go!" This matchup was months in the making. Curry was wired with a microphone for Golden State's game last Thursday night against Sacramento and had a discussion with Warriors teammate Brandin Podziemski about Ionescu, who scored an almost-perfect 37 points in the WNBA's 3-point contest last summer. That topped Curry's NBA 3-point contest best of 31. "I think I've got to challenge her," Curry told Podziemski. The NBA was more than happy to make it happen. Curry will shoot from the NBA 3-point line with NBA basketballs , and Ionescu will shoot from the WNBA 3-point line with WNBA basketballs . RISING STARS - No surprise here: Victor Wembanyama is headed to his first All-Star weekend. The San Antonio rookie and No. 1 overall pick in last year's NBA draft was one of 28 players picked for the Rising Stars games, to be held on the Friday of All-Star weekend, Feb. 16. No. 2 pick Brandon Miller of Charlotte and No. 3 pick Scoot Henderson of Portland are joining Wembanyama among the rookies selected. Also picked from the rookie class was Oklahoma City's Chet Holmgren, the former Gonzaga standout. Among the second-year class players are Orlando's Paolo Banchero, a Seattle native, and Portland's Shaedon Sharpe. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 03 Feb 2024 (Philippines News Agency) The 2024 NBA All-Star Game reserves including Golden State Warriors superstar Stephen Curry were announced on Friday. The reserves will join the starters at the 73rd All-Star Game in Indianapolis on Feb. 18. Last week, the starters led by LeBron James and Giannis Antetokounmpo for the Western and Eastern Conferences, respectively, were announced. The basketball league announced seven reserves for each conference selected by 30 head coaches. Eastern Conference reserves Bam Adebayo (Miami Heat), Paolo Banchero (Orlando Magic), Jaylen Brown (Boston Celtics), Jalen Brunson (New York Knicks), Tyrese Maxey (Philadelphia 76ers), Donovan Mitchell (Cleveland Cavaliers), and Julius Randle (New York Knicks) Western Conference reserves Devin Booker (Phoenix Suns), Stephen Curry (Golden State Warriors), Anthony Davis (Los Angeles Lakers), Anthony Edwards (Minnesota Timberwolves), Paul George (Los Angeles Clippers), Kawhi Leonard (Los Angeles Clippers), and Karl-Anthony Towns (Minnesota Timberwolves) The NBA will return to its traditional East vs. West format in the All-Star Game. Joel Embiid (Philadelphia 76ers), Jayson Tatum (Boston Celtics), Tyrese Haliburton (Indiana Pacers), and Damian Lillard (Milwaukee Bucks) are the Eastern Conference starters and will be captained by Antetokounmpo of the Bucks. Kevin Durant (Phoenix Suns), Nikola Jokic (Denver Nuggets), Luka Doncic (Dallas Mavericks), Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (Oklahoma City Thunder) are the Western Conference starters and the team will be captained by Los Angeles Lakers forward James, an All-Star starter for the 20th straight year. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 CALIFORNIA, U.S.A.- Trade rumors surrounding LeBron James and the Golden State Warriors have rocked the basketball world. What prompted the Warriors to pursue the Lakers star? Let's find out the details behind this intriguing episode that kept fans on edge until the Feb. 8 trade deadline. As it turns out, LeBron James was pursued by the Warriors, who actively sought to persuade both James and the Lakers to consider a trade. While James and the Lakers were not interested in exploring the offer, the Warriors' attempt to acquire the superstar added a touch of drama to the season. LeBron James: Firmly with Lakers Despite rumors and speculation, LeBron James made it clear that his loyalty is with the Lakers. His agent, Rich Paul, stated flatly that James would not be traded. In addition, James kept the intrigue going by posting an hourglass emoji on his social media, which sparked speculation about his future. His actions and subsequent words indicated that his commitment to Los Angeles remains solid, despite flirtations with other teams such as the New York Knicks. A Potential Twist in the Rivalry. Playing for the Warriors would have been a dramatic career turnaround for LeBron James, especially considering his historic rivalry with Stephen Curry . Over the years, matchups between James' teams and the Warriors have defined the competition in the NBA. Although the opportunity to join the Warriors did not materialize, Golden State's interest added a new dimension to the dynamics of professional basketball . fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Basketball 's biggest stars will gather from Friday to Sunday in Indianapolis (Indiana) for a date with sensitive casualties such as Joel Embiid (Sixers), the current MVP, and in which LeBron James (Lakers) will break the record of participations with 20. With audience figures at an all-time low, the NBA wants to recapture the magic of the All-Star Game this weekend with a revamped format and innovations such as the duel between triple monarchs Stephen Curry and Sabrina Ionescu. The event, however, has lost much of its former luster. The stars' disinterest in participating in the legendary dunk contest and their complete lack of intensity in the All-Star game eventually bored the audience, and viewership plummeted until it hit rock bottom last year. What time is the NBA All Star Game 2024? The NBA All Star Game 2024 is scheduled to be played from 19:00 (Peruvian time). We leave you the schedules of other countries so you don't miss this important game. Mexico: 18:00 hours Ecuador: 19:00 hours Colombia: 19:00 hours Bolivia: 20:00 hours Venezuela: 20:00 hours Chile: 9:00 p.m. Argentina: 21:00 hours Paraguay: 21:00 hours Uruguay: 21:00 hours Brazil: 21:00 hours Where to watch NBA All Star Game 2024? The NBA All Star Game 2024 will be broadcasted by NBA League Pass, STAR Plus and Amazon Prime Video for all America, while in Spain, the signal enabled to broadcast the game is Movistar Plus. You can also follow the minute by minute on the DT El Comercio website. The following is the schedule and participants of the 2024 NBA All-Star Game weekend, which starts on Friday in Indianapolis (Indiana). NBA All-Star 2024 players: Eastern Conference Team Starters: Damian Lillard (Bucks), Tyrese Haliburton (Pacers), Jayson Tatum (Celtics), Giannis Antetokounmpo (Bucks), Joel Embiid (Sixers, will not play due to injury and his place will be taken by one of the reserves. Alternates: Trae Young (Hawks), Jalen Brunson (Knicks), Tyrese Maxey (Sixers), Donovan Mitchell (Cavaliers), Jaylen Brown (Celtics), Scottie Barnes (Raptors), Paolo Banchero (Magic), Bam Adebayo (Heat). Coach: Doc Rivers (Bucks) Western Conference Team: Starters: Luka Doncic (Mavericks), Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (Thunder), Kevin Durant (Suns), LeBron James (Lakers), Nikola Jokic (Nuggets). Alternates: Stephen Curry (Warriors), Devin Booker (Suns), Anthony Edwards (Timberwolves), Paul George (Clippers), Kawhi Leonard (Clippers), Karl-Anthony Towns (Timberwolves), Anthony Davis (Lakers) Coach: Chris Finch (Timberwolves). Contests: Skills: Team Pacers: Tyrese Haliburton (Pacers), Bennedict Mathurin (Pacers), Myles Turner (Pacers) All-Number One Draft Team: Paolo Banchero (Magic), Anthony Edwards (Timberwolves), Victor Wembanyama (Spurs) All-Stars Team: Scottie Barnes (Raptors), Tyrese Maxey (Sixers), Trae Young (Hawks) Three-pointers: Malik Beasley (Bucks), Jalen Brunson (Knicks) Tyrese Haliburton, (Pacers), Damian Lillard (Bucks), Lauri Markkanen (Jazz), Donovan Mitchell (Cavaliers), Karl-Anthony Towns (Timberwolves), Trae Young (Hawks) Dumps: Jaylen Brown (Celtics), Jaime Jaquez Jr. (Heat), Jacob Toppin (Knicks), Mac McClung (G League - Osceola Magic) NBA All-Star 2024 Schedule: Friday 16 Rising Stars Game - from 02H00 GMT on Saturday Saturday 17th Skills Contest - from 01H00 GMT on Sunday Three-point contest Three-point challenge between Stephen Curry and Sabrina Ionescu Dunk contest Sunday 18th All-Star Game - from 01H00 GMT on Monday J\'e1quez Jr. for dunk title The festivities will kick off on Friday with the Rising Stars Game, where French phenom Victor Wembanyama will live his All-Star experience. The Spurs' center, the NBA's newest hotshot, is dazzling in his first season in the league (20.5 points, 10.0 rebounds and 3.2 blocks on average) but did not earn a spot in the competitive Western Conference for Sunday's big game. Mexican-American rookie Jaime Jaquez Jr. (Heat) will also compete in the 'Rising Stars,' where promising freshmen, sophomores and G-League affiliate players face off divided into four teams, one of them led by Spain's Pau Gasol. Wembanyama, 2.24m tall, will be back on the court for Saturday's first contest, the skills contest, teaming up with two other young first-round draft picks, Paolo Banchero and Anthony Edwards (Timberwolves). Afterwards, Damian Lillard will defend his title as the winner of the three-point contest against seven contenders: Haliburton, Brunson, Malik Beasley (Bucks), Lauri Markkanen (Jazz), Donovan Mitchell (Cavaliers), Karl-Anthony Towns (Timberwolves) and Trae Young (Hawks). Saturday's highlight will be the duel between Stephen Curry (Warriors), the best shooter in NBA history, and Sabrina Ionescu (New York Liberty), one of the stars of the Women's National Basketball Association (WNBA). Ionescu signed last year a record-breaking three-point contest in the WNBA, missing only two of her 27 shots, unleashing praise from the baske

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 On September 20, 1973, sports history was made. Bobby Rigss, a former Wimbledon champion (1939) but with a big mouth like no other, was summoned to a tennis match against Billie Jean King, the historic player of the women's circuit. It was a match between men and women, a match known as the 'Battle of the Sexes' and in front of 30,472 people - and 90 million on television - and King, in much better shape and with motivation, cruised past Riggs (6-4, 6-3, 6-3). "If I lost it was going back 50 years," he confessed. It was history... and the NBA, on a positive and different note, had its similar chapter . Much more enriching. With Stephen Curry (Golden State Warriors) beating Sabrina Ionescu (New York Liberty) in the fight between the best shooters in the world. One in the NBA, one in the WNBA. If I lost [in the 1973 battle of the sexes] I would go back 50 years. Billie Jean King It was a brilliant fight. Ionescu asked to shoot from the NBA line as well and she did not disappoint. Sabrina billed 26 points and forced Curry to wait until the last shot. She scored 29 and took the 'championship belt'. A glorious battle. "I don't know if anyone will be able to take our places, but maybe it's something we need to repeat more often," recounted the Warriors leader . I don't know if anyone will be able to take our places Stephen Curry Beyond the result, it confirms that shooters don't understand gender or leagues. "Shooters shoot," they say in the language of the United States. It applied. With Ionescu getting off to an impressive start (13 of his first 15 shots). And Curry signing at least four five-ball hits in his last four carts. Two tremendous contests. Curry's would be champion, with three difference, over the one won by Damian Lillard in the traditional one. Ionescu's, too. Although his 26 were matched by 'Dame', Trae Young, Karl-Anthony Towns or Tyrese Haliburton. No battle of the sexes. It's a basketball party. "Never discount whoever you have to your right or left. If you can shoot, you can shoot. Women's basketball is amazing. It's a small step in the right direction," Curry confessed. And it elevates Ionescu, already a WNBA star and star of campaigns for the multinational Nike. From his sneaker line, the Sabrina, to his game in New York . "It only matters the heart you have in wanting to be better than you can be," she buckled. Basketball for the ages. Jonathan Kuminga added 25 points for the Warriors, who extended their road winning streak to seven games, their longest since winning 11 in a row in the 2018-19 season. Curry was 0 for 7 in the first half of a victory in Washington on Tuesday, the first time he's been scoreless through two quarters since Nov. 23, 2012. He finished with eight 3-pointers, while Klay Thompson added 16 points. Iowa's Clark turning pro Iowa star Caitlin Clark, who is on the verge of becoming the all-time NCAA scoring leader in college basketball , announced Thursday she will leave the Hawkeyes after this season and enter the WNBA draft. "While this season is far from over and we have a lot more goals to achieve, it will be my last one at Iowa," Clark wrote on social media. The guard, with one more year of eligibility, became the all-time leading women's scorer in major college basketball by scoring 33 points to pass Lynette Woodard and post her 17th career triple-double in a 108-60 victory over Minnesota on Wednesday night. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The U.S. national basketball team has almost finalized its roster for the Olympic Games in Paris . According to the website "The Athletic" on Monday night, coach Steve Kerr 's (Golden State Warriors) committee has decided on 11 of the 12 players who will go to France in the middle of the year, including NBA stars LeBron James and Anthony Davis (Los Angeles Lakers), Stephen Curry (Warriors) and Kevin Durant (Phoenix Suns). They are joined by point guards Devin Booker (Suns), Jrue Holiday (Boston Celtics) and Tyrese Haliburton (Indiana Pacers); wings Jayson Tatum (Celtics) and Anthony Edwards (Minnesota Timberwolves); and forwards Joel Embiid (Philadelphia 76ers) and Bam Adebayo (Miami Heat). The mystery is over the final spot on the team, once dubbed the "Dream Team" for bringing together the best of the NBA, the world's top basketball league. At the end of January, a list of 41 names in the running for a place at the Olympics was released. Among these, the favorite to be chosen is forward Kawhi Leonard (Los Angeles Clippers), but there are also a number of big names, such as Jimmy Butler (Heat), Damian Lillard (Milwaukee Bucks), and his teammates James Harden and Paul George (Clippers). Here's a list of 11 names: LeBron James (Los Angeles Lakers) Stephen Curry (Golden State Warriors) Kevin Durant (Phoenix Suns) Joel Embiid (Philadelphia 76ers) Jayson Tatum (Boston Celtics) Devin Booker (Phoenix Suns) Jrue Holiday (Boston Celtics) Anthony Davis (Los Angeles Lakers) Anthony Edwards (Minnesota Timberwolves) Bam Adebayo (Miami Heat) Tyrese Haliburton (Indiana Pacers) After the last Basketball World Cup, won by Germany last year, in which the USA came fourth, the NBA stars started a movement to confirm their presence at the Olympic Games. The idea is to show the strength of the national team on the world stage, just like in 1992, with the "Dream Team", and in 2008, with the "Redeem Team". Kerr's coaching staff will include Erik Spoelstra (Heat), Tyronn Lue (Clippers) and Mark Few (Gonzaga University). According to "The Athletic", the team's training period will begin on July 5 in Las Vegas, after the end of the current NBA season. The basketball tournament in France will begin on the 28th, when the Americans face Serbia. Both are in group C, alongside South Sudan and a team yet to be determined. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Since the original Dream Team, that of the 1992 Barcelona Games , many U.S. teams have been called that without deserving it. The USA Basketball team that USA Basketball has put together for this summer's Games in Paris and that will be led by Steve Kerr from the bench can indeed live up to that name . The goal is to win a fifth consecutive gold medal after Beijing, London, Rio de Janeiro and Tokyo. Eleven of the 12 names that will make up the team are already known and they are fearsome: Stephen Curry , LeBron James, Kevin Durant, Jayson Tatum, Joel Embiid, Devin Booker, Tyrese Haliburton, Anthony Edwards, Jrue Holiday, Bam Adebayo and Anthony Davis . For the remaining spot there are plenty of candidates: Jalen Brunson, Paolo Banchero, Jaylen Brown.... Only the final touch is missing and the decision will be made before the team's training camp in July and the preparation games in Las Vegas. Since the United States missed out on the podium at last year's World Cup, LeBron James began to sound out the possibility of making a star-studded selection for the Paris event and, as Americans are interested in the Games, the response was massive. He was the first to sign up. At 39 years of age, everything points to this being his last dance with the national team . In his fourth Games, he will be trying for his fourth Olympic medal after golds in 2008 and 2012 and bronze in 2004. It could also be the last tournament for Kevin Durant, who is 35 and will be chasing his fourth gold (winner in 2012, 2016 and 2020), something no one has ever achieved in history. He is now tied with Carmelo Anthony. Stephen Curry , who has already turned 36 will dispute his first Olympic appointment , believe it or not. The Warriors star has won two World gold medals (2010 and 2014), but he has never been to the Games. Embiid adds morbidity The three of them will be the most representative figures of the team, but the morbidity will be provided by Joel Embiid, who will debut with the U.S. national team after having been flirting with the French Federation for a possible nationalization . He finally opted for USA Basketball and received harsh criticism from the rival, which will probably also be so on the court because of its host status and the presence of Victor Wembanyama. In the draw for the Olympic tournament, the U.S. team was drawn in Group C along with Serbia, South Sudan and the champion of the Pre-Olympic to be played in Puerto Rico . Basketball will kick off on July 27, but the U.S. team will make its debut a day later against Serbia, presumably with Nikola Jokic . Embiid's choice While all eyes will be focused on LeBron, Durant and Curry, the presence of Joel Embiid will also be special, as the Cameroonian-born giant will debut with the United States national team after being tempted by the French Federation for a possible nationalization. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The United States made official the list of 12 players that will make up the basketball Dream Team that will seek the gold medal at the next Olympic Games in Paris 2024, which is headed by veteran LeBron James . Led by the NBA's all-time leading scorer, the Americans have assembled a roster full of stars, as is customary in each edition of the great event. Kevin Durant , Stephen Curry , Anthony Davis and Joel Embiid are also on the list of basketball players who will represent the United States in Paris 2024 . View this post on Instagram A post shared by USA Basketball (@usabasketball). Steve Kerr , who has won multiple titles as coach of the Golden State Warriors, is pleased with the makeup of the roster he will lead in Paris 2024 . "We plan to represent USA Basketball with the highest class and hope to make our country proud," the also former Chicago Bulls player in the 1990s said in this regard. All 12 members of the U.S. Dream Team have played in the NBA All-Star Game, and six of them have been champions and four have been MVP of the most important basketball league in the world. The 12 players of the U.S. Dream Team LeBron James (Los Angeles Lakers) Anthony Davis (Los Angeles Lakers) Joel Embiid (Philadelphia 76ers) Stephen Curry (Golden State Warriors) Kevin Durant (Phoenix Suns) Anthony Edwards (Minnesota Timberwolves) Jayson Tatum (Boston Celtics) Jrue Holiday (Boston Celtics) Bam Adebayo (Miami Heat) Devin Booker (Phoenix Suns) Kawhi Leonard (Los Angeles Clippers) Tyrese Haliburton (Indiana Pacers)? View this post on Instagram A post shared by USA Basketball (@usabasketball). Who will the Dream Team play against in Paris 2024? The United States is in Group C of the men's basketball tournament at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. The basketball tournament begins July 27 and concludes Aug. 10. The Dream Team makes its debut on July 28 against Serbia. Its second game will be against South Sudan on July 31 and on August 3 it will close the first phase against the winner of an Olympic qualifying tournament to be held in Puerto Rico. The quarterfinals will take place on August 6, the semifinals two days later and the grand final on August 10. Since the Dream Team led by Michael Jordan in Barcelona 1992, the United States has won Olympic gold in all editions of the event except for Athens 2004, when Argentina took first place. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 LeBron James plans to go back to the Olympics. Stephen Curry is planning on going for the first time. Kevin Durant will go there eyeing history. And they're just part of a star-studded roster the Americans have assembled for the Paris Games. USA Basketball has gotten confirmations from 12 players "" James, Curry, Durant, Bam Adebayo, Devin Booker, Joel Embiid, Jayson Tatum, Anthony Davis, Jrue Holiday, Tyrese Haliburton, Anthony Edwards and Kawhi Leonard "" that they will accept invitations to be on the Olympic roster, three people with knowledge of the decisions said. That group includes seven previous gold medalists. It was believed USA Basketball was planning to release the names on Wednesday. Leonard "" a finalist for each of the last two Olympic teams "" was the last piece of the roster puzzle to be finalized. The amount of talent on the U.S. roster is staggering. Of the 12 commits, seven finished the season ranked among the NBA's top 15 scorers. James is the league's all-time scoring leader, Curry the all-time leader in 3-pointers, Haliburton won the assist-per-game title and 10 were All-Stars this season as well. Players have been told of the decisions USA Basketball made over the past several days. , one of the people said. USA Basketball managing director Grant Hill has led the task of assembling the roster for the past several months, and the plan all along was to put together the team well in advance of training camp starting at Las Vegas in early July. Tryouts were never part of the plan for this team. Durant has said since last year that he plans to play, which means he will pursue becoming the first men's player in Olympic history with four basketball gold medals. He and Carmelo Anthony are the only men's players with three Olympic golds; there are six women, all American, with at least four Olympic basketball golds. Sue Bird and Diana Taurasi each have five, while Teresa Edwards, Tamika Catchings, Sylvia Fowles and Lisa Leslie each won four. James will seek a fourth medal, after winning bronze in 2004 and golds in 2008 and 2012. The other past gold medalists on the roster are Davis (2012), Adebayo (2020), Booker (2020), Tatum (2020) and Holiday (2020). The 2020 gold medals were won in 2021, since the COVID-19 pandemic forced a one-year delay of the Tokyo Games. Curry will play in the Olympics for the first time, as will Embiid "" who chose to play for the U.S. last year after becoming an American citizen. The Cameroon-born center also could have chosen to represent France at the Paris Games. The other first-time Olympians on the current roster are Haliburton and Edwards, both of whom played for the U.S. team that finished fourth at the World Cup in Manila last year. Many of the commitments are not surprises: Durant and Curry said in October that they wanted to play this summer, Adebayo said then that he had already committed to the team, and it's been assumed for some time that if players like James want to play, then all they have to do is say so. The U.S. men have competed in basketball at the Olympics 19 times, winning 19 medals "" 16 gold, one silver and two bronze. The Paris team will be coached by Steve Kerr, assisted by Erik Spoelstra, Tyronn Lue and Mark Few. It's still possible that the roster changes before the summer, if injuries or deep runs in the NBA playoffs force players to change their minds about committing to the national team. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 HYPERLINK https://i0.wp.com/eltiempolatino.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/NBA-playoffs-4.jpg?fit=1024%2C576&ssl=1 00 Link to Image The NBA playoffs 2024 marks the first time in more than two decades that LeBron James , Stephen Curry or Kevin Durant will not compete in the second round. This season represents a pivotal moment in basketball history, with new talent emerging to prominence. Unexpected eliminations Stephen Curry 's Golden State Warriors were eliminated in the Play-In by the Sacramento Kings. Simultaneously, Kevin Durant , with the Phoenix Suns, and LeBron James , with the Los Angeles Lakers, fell in the first round to the Minnesota Timberwolves and Denver Nuggets, respectively. NBA power realignment The absence of these superstars in the second round undoubtedly affected the excitement of the playoffs. In the last decade each has advanced to the second round: LeBron James 14 times Kevin Durant 12 times Stephen Curry seven times New talent shines in NBA playoffs 2024 With the departure of the big stars, new talent emerges ready to take their place as the faces of the NBA. Bleacher Report lists the following: Leading this new wave is the Oklahoma City Thunder's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (25), who steps confidently onto the stage vacated by the veterans. Hot on his heels is the dynamic Anthony Edwards (22) who leads the Minnesota Timberwolves with new energy and ambition. In Denver, Nikola Joki? (29) takes the reins, quickly establishing himself as a dominant force on the court, filling the void left by the veterans. In the Eastern Conference, the Boston Celtics are looking to Jayson Tatum , 26, to lead the team to new heights. Meanwhile, Jalen Brunson (27) is revitalizing the New York Knicks, propelling them to their most convincing performances in recent memory. The departure of James, Curry and Durant represents the end of an era and marks a crucial shift in the NBA narrative. The torch is being passed to the next generation of players, redefining the power dynamic in the league. Young athletes are not only participating in the game, but actively transforming it as the playoffs move forward without the familiar superstars. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Never before in the past 12 months have the top ten highest-paid athletes each pocketed more than $100 million before taxes and agent fees. This was 24 percent higher than last year's record, when it had only been the top eight, totaling $1.11 billion. However, the increase in the amount of these athletes' revenues is mostly due to the investments that Saudi companies have made in the different disciplines. Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has assets of $925 billion and owns several teams in the country's First Division, including Cristiano Ronaldo's Al Nassr, Neymar's Al-Hilal and Karim Benzema's Al-Ittihad. In addition, Lionel Messi has supported some of their initiatives as a tourism ambassador. But soccer is not the only sport in which they invest their funds. In 2022, the Spanish golfer Jon Rahm (today number 3 in the world ranking of his discipline), defended before the media the PGA Tour, putting it above the LIV Golf, which would be inaugurated in the middle of that year in London. He stated that money would not make him abandon the main U.S. professional golf circuit because, in his words: "I have never played golf for economic reasons. I play for the love of the sport and I want to play against the best in the world". He also indicated that this new competition, backed by Saudi Arabia, had a format that was not for him. However, in December 2023, he appeared on Fox News wearing a LIV-branded jacket, which confirmed that he had migrated to this competition. Consequently, he acquired the promise of $350 million of which he received half upfront, positioning him as the second highest paid athlete in the world. Even so, there are off-field factors that have also boosted athletes' incomes: the explosion of media rights and sponsorship portfolios. These now multi-million dollar contracts allow basketball players such as LeBron James, Giannis Antetokounmpo and Stephen Curry to be among the top ten highest paid in the world. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Boston (USA), Jun 8 (EFE) - Caitlin Clark, the great sensation of women's basketball , will not be with the U.S. team at the Olympic Games in Paris 2024, said Saturday the portal The Athletic. Shams Charania, one of the best-sourced journalists in U.S. basketball , told The Athletic that the officials of the Stars and Stripes have opted for a selection full of big names such as A'ja Wilson, Breanna Stewart, Diana Taurasi, Brittney Griner, Alyssa Thomas, Napheesa Collier, Jewell Loyd, Kelsey Plum, Jackie Young, Sabrina Ionescu, Chelsea Gray and Kahleah Copper. The 22-year-old Clark is the all-time leading scorer in college basketball history and was selected with the number one pick in the WNBA draft by the Indiana Fever. Called to change women's basketball and already a true star of the sport in her country, Clark has an uncanny ability to score from the perimeter that may remind one of NBA genius like Stephen Curry . The Iowa native tied the WNBA record for most three-pointers made from the outside by a rookie player in the WNBA with seven in a game on Friday. The U.S. women's national team dominates international women's basketball with enormous force, so much so that it has won the gold medal in every Olympic Games since Atlanta 1996. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 There are many reasons why the NBA is the best basketball league in the world. The best teams are there, and the best players are eager to land in the American league just so they can show audiences their basketball skills... but also to get paid. In the end, where the best players are, so should be the best salaries. Stephen Curry He is the highest paid basketball player in the world. The Golden State Warriors point guard is one of the best scorers in history and his salary this past season is a very good reward. The nearly $52 million annual payroll comes from the renewal he signed in 2021. That's $261 million for the five years he has extended his contract. Everything points to the fact that this may be the last big contract of the player from Akron, who is already 36 years old. Kevin Durant The second highest paid player in the NBA is another great scorer who is always among fans' picks when they make predictions on platforms like Betsson... and he is also a former teammate of Stephen Curry . Kevin Durant left San Francisco for Brooklyn, where the draft never took off. Now with the Phoenix Suns alongside Devin Booker and Bradley Beal, he has once again drawn a blank despite the more than $47,600,000 million he has been paid this season. With two more years left on his contract and a fairly advanced age as well, he should be looking at his last big contract as well. LeBron James 'The King' in his 20th season has also been among the highest paid in the NBA. The Los Angeles Lakers player has earned approximately $47,600,000 in what has been his last year with his current contract with the Los Angeles team. Will he renew with the Lakers? Will he retire? Everything points to the fact that he could stay one more year, with the intention of sharing the court with his son Bronny. Nikola Jokic and Joel Embiid The last two NBA MVPs have exactly the same salary, also slightly above $47,600,000 as LeBron James. Nikola Jokic (Denver) and Joel Embiid (Philadelphia 76ers) are two modern centers, with a huge influence on the court, but with mixed fortunes in every way. The Serbian, a three-time MVP with a ring on his fingers, has avoided injury without much difficulty in his career. The Cameroonian, meanwhile, is undoubtedly talented, but a lack of continuity due to physical problems and no playoff results have many wondering if he's too high a salary for what he can bring in the final moments. Bradley Beal Another player who has ended up with a contract probably higher than he deserves. The second member of the Phoenix Suns on this list has earned $46,741,590 this season, in a rather disappointing result. Next season will be his last year of his contract, in which he will be paid more than $50,000,000... but his performance and results suggest that he is not a player who deserves such a high salary. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The U. S. men's basketball team will field a star-studded NBA squad for the Olympics . A new 'Dream Team' will seek Olympic gold number 17 in basketball at Paris 2024. The U.S. squad features iconic figures such as LeBron James, Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant . Meanwhile, the coaching staff will be led by Steve Kerr and will be assisted by Erik Spoelstra and Tyronn Lue, all NBA champions. The focus will be on the stars on the court, led by LeBron James , who is seeking his third Olympic gold at the age of 40. In contrast, Kevin Durant, now with the Phoenix Suns, could become the first player to reach four consecutive gold medals. Stephen Curry , the greatest three-point shooter in history, will make his Olympic debut in Paris . The U.S. Dream Team The union of LeBron James, Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant evokes memories of the 1992 Dream Team and the 2008 Redeem Team. This team wants to redeem itself from the failure at the 2023 World Cup, where the U.S. could only finish fourth. The team will also feature Jayson Tatum, Jrue Holiday, Joel Embiid, Anthony Edwards and Tyrese Haliburton. The roster is rounded out by Anthony Davis, Bam Adebayo, Devin Booker and Kawhi Leonard. The United States has scheduled a series of friendlies beginning July 10 in Las Vegas against Canada. They will also face Australia, Serbia, South Sudan and Germany in their preparation for the Olympic Games . At the Olympic tournament, the U.S. could face host France and its young star Victor Wembanyama , a basketball prodigy. When does the U.S. make its debut? The U.S. men's basketball team is in Group C of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games basketball tournament. The new 'Dream Team' will debut in the Olympic tournament next Monday, July 29, against Japan. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Stephen Curry was relatively quiet offensively during the U.S. Olympic team's first two exhibitions before the Paris Games. He got a lot more assertive on Wednesday with his official Olympic debut less than two weeks away. Curry scored 24 points, Bam Adebayo added 17 and the U.S. beat Serbia 105-79 at Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, to improve to 3-0 in its five-game slate of exhibitions in advance of the Paris Olympics. Anthony Davis finished with seven points, six rebounds and six blocks, helping to lead a U.S. defensive effort that limited Serbia throughout. Anthony Edwards had 16 points. LeBron James added 11. Curry scored the first nine points for the U.S. in a flurry that James said was by design. "We drew it up for that particular reason, to get him going," James said. "He sees one go through the hoop, you see what it opens up for the rest of his game, for the rest of the game for all of us. He set the tone." The U.S. led by as many as 31 points in the last of a pair of warm-up games in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates. The Americans go to London to play two more tuneups before heading to France. The first is Saturday against South Sudan, followed by a matchup with Germany on Monday at London's O2 Arena. U.S. Coach Steve Kerr likes the progress his team has made and said its ability to overwhelm teams with different defensive looks will be vital. "I think the identity of the team is our depth, the strength of the team is the depth," Kerr said. "If we can play in 4- or 5-minute bursts of intense defense, hitting bodies, rebounding, being physical, then it makes sense to play that way. We'll see if we can keep doing it." The U.S. had an uneven start before using a 16-2 run in the second quarter to take a 56-42 advantage and led 59-45 at halftime. Nikola Jokic had 16 points and 11 rebounds for Serbia. Aleksa Avramovic added 14 points. Serbia was playing its second game in two days, coming off a loss to Australia on Tuesday in which it rested captain Bogdan Bogdanovic. Though Serbia was without Bogdanovic for the second straight game, it struggled offensively, shooting just 41% (29 of 71). The U.S. also held a 30-21 rebounding edge. Adebayo and Davis combined for 14 rebounds. "Bam and AD together are really something," Kerr said. "Just the switching, but they can also protect the rim and be in a drop if we go to that coverage." Wednesday's matchup was an important preview for the Serbians and U.S., which will both compete in Group C at the Olympics. They open their quests for gold against each other on July 28. After nearly squandering a big lead in its narrow victory over Australia on Monday, there was no such letdown for the U.S. this time. The Americans led by 25 after three quarters and quickly increased it to 30 in the final quarter. LINEUP SHUFFLE The U.S. used its third different starting lineup, going with Curry, Jrue Holiday, Jayson Tatum, LeBron James and Joel Embiid. The only constants over the three exhibitions have been Curry, James and Embiid. One of the reason's Embiid's presence on the roster was a desire for the U.S. coaching staff this cycle was to counter bigger teams like Serbia, which features a trio of 7-footers. Embiid's conditioning still isn't at its peak. But he was active on both ends, finding cutters on the offensive end and being an active deterrent in the lane on defense. ENERGY SHIFTERS For the second straight game, the U.S. reserves provided a spark. Edwards, Davis, Tyrese Haliburton, Adebayo and Devin Booker entered the game for the first time with 4:54 remaining in the opening quarter and the U.S. trailing 16-13. The U.S. proceeded to outscore the Serbians 15-12 the rest of the quarter to tie the game at 28 entering the second quarter. The American reserves accounted for 28 points off the bench in the first half. STILL NO DURANT Kevin Durant sat for the third straight game as he continues to recover from the calf strain he suffered early during training camp. But guard Derrick White made his debut after joining the team over the weekend as Kawhi Leonard's replacement. White had four rebounds and an assist in just over nine minutes. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Stephen Curry and LeBron James will hope to win Olympic gold this summer READ MORE: Russell Westbrook and DeMar DeRozan join Lamar onstage Stephen Curry and LeBron James have great chemistry on the court, but their music tastes aren't quite synced up - as they showed after an Olympic tune-up game vs. Serbia on Wednesday. Following the Americans' 105-79 win in Abu Dhabi, Kendrick Lamar's hit song 'Not Like Us' blared from the speakers - but Curry was tired of hearing it. 'Again with this song!,' he appeared to say. 'It's not the only song in America.' James, however, said 'I love it' as he stood near Curry, Anthony Davis and Nigel Hayes-Davis. His USA teammate Anthony Edwards could then be seen dancing to the song as he slid up behind him. The track, which was released by Lamar as a diss against fellow rapper Drake, is currently No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100. Drake previously called James 'my brother' and the pair have had a longstanding relationship, as they've known each other for at least 15 years. Drake also on the basketball star's 'The Shop' TV show. However, James appeared to pick his side in the heated Drake-Lamar feud when he attended the latter's LA show in June. The 'Pop Out' concert was also attended by NBA stars Russell Westbrook and DeMar DeRozan, who even joined Lamar onstage. Despite their differences in musical taste, Curry and James will hope to lead Team USA to a gold medal this summer in Paris. On Wednesday, Curry led the team with 24 points, while James had 11 in the comfortable win over Serbia. Nikola Jokic had 16 points and 11 rebounds for his home country. The Olympics kick off on July 26. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The United States basketball 'Dream Team' is in the final stage of its preparation for the Paris 2024 Olympic Games, to be held from July 26 to August 11. With a team full of NBA stars, the 2024 Dream Team is looking to retain its Olympic championship title and establish itself as the best team in the world. Among the most prominent players are LeBron James, Stephen Curry , Kevin Durant, Joel Embiid, Jayson Tatum and Devin Booker. View this post on Instagram A post shared by USA Basketball (@usabasketball) Friendly games As part of their preparation, the Dream Team has played several international friendly matches. In their first match, they defeated Canada 86-72. Then, they beat Australia, bronze medalist in Tokyo 2020, 98-92. In their last friendly match, they defeated Serbia by a score of 105-79. View this post on Instagram A post shared by USA Basketball (@usabasketball) The U.S. team will continue its friendly tour with two more games in London: against South Sudan on July 20 and Germany on July 22. U.S. schedule for the Paris 2024 Olympic Games: The Dream Team is part of Group C along with Serbia, South Sudan and Puerto Rico. The top two teams from each group will advance to the knockout stage. July 28: vs. Serbia (9:15 AM Central Mexico Time) July 31: vs. South Sudan (1:00 PM Central Mexico Time) August 3: vs. Puerto Rico (9:15 AM Central Mexico Time) You might be interested in: Paris 2024: Calendar of the Mexican athletes with the highest expectations for the Olympic Games A team with experience and talent Dream Team 2024 combines the experience of veteran players like LeBron James and Kevin Durant with the young talent of stars like Jayson Tatum and Joel Embiid. This mix of experience and youth makes the U.S. team a favorite to take home the gold medal in Paris. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 After suffering another failure at the 2023 World Cup in the Philippines, the new version of the U.S. Dream Team will be looking to redeem itself in front of its fans in Paris 2024. The upcoming Olympic Games are the perfect scenario for the U.S. team to return to the top, a position they have taken very seriously after the fall they suffered in Athens 2004. After a pitiful fourth place finish at the last World Cup, USA Basketball set itself the task of reorganizing and sending the best possible team to the summer tournament. The United States knows that there is no bad fifth, as it has won four consecutive gold medals in this discipline, and that is why the superstars have been called upon for the tournament to be played in the French capital. Led by LeBron James and Kevin Durant, who are the most veteran of the team, accompanied by Stephen Curry , Anthony Davis and Jayson Tatum, will be in charge of returning USA Basketball to its place in the world. Durant won the gold medal at the 2010 World Cup and is the U.S. Olympic leader in total points (435), scoring average (19.8 points per game), field goal shooting (146), three-point shooting (74) and free throws (69). Meanwhile, the "King" has scored 273 points (third best in the U.S.). He will be participating in his fourth Olympic Games and first since 2012. He won gold in 2008 and 2012, and made his first appearance in 2004 (bronze). The Stars and Stripes have a lifetime Olympic record of 16 golds, one silver and two bronzes. X: @DMartinezCANCHA ONLY ONE MISSION The Dream Team goes to Paris with the sole mission of taking the Olympic gold. The last time the United States failed in its goal was in Athens 2004, when it took the bronze medal, after being defeated in the Semifinals by Argentina. Despite having won four consecutive gold medals, they will not have an easy road in these Olympic Games, as they could face teams like Canada, Germany, Spain or Slovenia, which have a lot of talent and will try to frustrate USA Basketball 's aspirations. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The Olympic Games are about to begin, and in basketball the great favorite is once again the United States , in a team that has many NBA stars such as LeBron James, Stephen Curry , Anthony Davis, Joel Embiid and Anthony Edwards. However, in their most recent friendly match they have left many doubts. On Saturday night, the United States faced South Sudan in London, in what was the fourth preparation game for the Americans before the Olympic Games in Paris. Although it was only a friendly, it served as a warning to the U.S. team: they came very close to losing against the African team, which they will also have to face in the group stage of the Olympic Games. South Sudan, the youngest sovereign state in the world (it became an independent country in 2011), managed to reach halftime 14 points ahead on the scoreboard, and with 13 minutes left in the game still had an 11-point lead , thanks in large part to Marial Shayok's three-pointers (currently playing in the Chinese league) and the lack of success of the American players beyond the three-point line. However, in the final stretch of the game, the U.S. team managed to come from behind to take a hard-fought victory. Winning basket by LeBron James Late in the third quarter, the U.S. went on a 16-0 run, including a pair of three-pointers by Stephen Curry , and looked like they were finally in control of the game. But the South Sudanese did not give up , and the last quarter was a thrilling one. JT Thor, the only South Sudanese player currently playing in the NBA, hit a three-pointer with 20 seconds left to put the South Sudanese up by one point. But that's when LeBron James , who at 39 had arguably been the best of the game, scored the final basket to make the final score 100-101 for a narrow U.S. victory. This agonizing basket is being widely shared on networks by users like @ElCesar_Guerra. In this way, LeBron James saved his national team from a defeat that would have been painful despite being a friendly, and the veteran star finished with 23 points, 6 rebounds and 6 assists. Of course, in the final seconds there was still time for a controversy: the referee did not signal a foul by Anthony Edwards on Wenyen Gabriel, which could have changed the final outcome. The U.S. has one last friendly before the start of the Olympic Games: this Monday against Germany, also in London. After that, in Paris, Serbia, Puerto Rico and South Sudan , who have made it so difficult for them in this preparation match, await them in the group stage. For its part, Spain shares a group in the Paris Games with Australia, Greece and Canada . The first match of the Spanish team in the Olympic Games will take place next Saturday, July 27, against the Australians. The Olympic Games are about to begin, and in basketball the great favorite is once again the United States , in a team that has many NBA stars such as LeBron James, Stephen Curry , Anthony Davis, Joel Embiid or Anthony Edwards. However, in their most recent friendly match they have left many doubts.
