fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Click here to listen to the news. The basketball team that will represent the United States at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games has been unveiled. A group full of stars from different NBA teams will seek to win the gold medal, led by LeBron James, Kevin Durant and Stephen Curry . In the history of the Olympics, the U.S. men's basketball team is the most winning team with 16 gold, one silver and two bronze medals. In addition, many of the stars that today make up the team have been NBA champions and participated in different editions of the All-Star Game, a friendly event to which only the best players of each year arrive, representing the Eastern and Western conferences. Grant Hill is the general manager of the men's team and one of the people in charge of selecting this select group of players, who each year stand out in their different teams that compete for the championship ring in the professional basketball league in the United States. Hill feels confident with the squad of players that has been assembled: "I am grateful to these 12 men for their commitment to represent us. The United States is home to some of the best basketball players in the world and I appreciate the strong interest in making this roster...we have the utmost respect for the level of competition we will face this summer. The Olympic Games represent the pinnacle of the sport," said the former basketball player and now coach. The new "Dream Team That's what they call today the team that has formed the U.S. team, which will go to Paris for its fifth consecutive Olympic gold medal. The list of players is made up of: LeBron James (Los Angeles Lakers), Stephen Curry (Golden State Warriors), Kevin Durant (Phoenix Suns), Anthony Davis (Los Angeles Lakers), Joel Embiid (Philadelphia 76ers), Devin Booker (Phoenix Suns), Anthony Edwards (Minnesota Timberwolves), Jayson Tatum (Boston Celtics), Bam Adebayo (Miami Heat), Jrue Holiday (Boston Celtics), Derrick White (Boston Celtics) and Tyrese Haliburton (Indiana Pacers). James and Durant, in addition to being the most veterans, are the most winningest of the current team, both with three medals; Durant, three golds, and LeBron, two and a bronze. The group will be led by the experienced 58-year-old coach and also former player, Steve Kerr. The 1992 team, the original "Dream Team". The U.S. group that competed in the 1992 Barcelona Olympics was from another galaxy. They dominated the Pre-Olympics held in Portland, qualifying for the Games along with the Venezuelan team. Christian Laettner, David Robinson, Patrick Ewing, Larry Bird, Larry Bird, Scottie Pippen, Clyde Drexler, Karl Malone, John Stockton, Chris Mullin, Charles Barkley, Magic Johnson and Michael Jordan were the heroes who "annihilated" their rivals and regained prestige four years after the United States won the bronze medal at the 1988 Seoul Olympics. Barkley was his team's leading scorer in Barcelona, averaging 18 points, three more than Jordan, who ended up playing point guard because of Stockton's injury. This "Dream Team" was led by coach Chuck Daly. Now, it will be the turn of Steve Kerr's team to continue making history for American basketball , with LeBron James still in force and leader of the NBA's all-time points scored chart with 40,474. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 U.S. basketball is already in Paris with all its stars to face the Olympic Games Basketball . LeBron James and Stephen Curry , the NBA' s top stars , who recently renewed their contracts in the American league, will be present. Both LeBron and Curry, plus Kevin Durant and Joel Embiid agreed to earn in the next NBA season figures that place them in the Top 20 of the highest paid basketball players in the world. The Golden State Warriors player will earn $55 million while the Philadelphia 76ers player will receive $51.4 million . Durant will have an annual salary of 49.9 million dollars and LeBron James will receive "only" u$s 48.7 million . And how much will they earn for the Olympic Games? Although the IOC does not pay athletes to appear at the Olympic Games and the U.S. Committee only gives them money for expenses during the competition, these elite players will receive extra money from their sponsors. The figure is estimated to be between u$s5 and 15 million . The U.S. Committee will also give a medal prize. The gold medal will give each athlete u$s37,500 in Paris 2024. The silver medal will leave them u$s22,500 and the bronze medal will mean u$s15,000 for each athlete who manages to climb the last step of the podium. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 "If something happens at the opening ceremony, can you swim?" The unusual question made Stephen Curry , 36, and Kevin Durant, 35, laugh at the US basketball press conference in Paris on Thursday morning (25). "Man, you sound like a flight attendant giving safety instructions!" said Curry, provoking laughter from the audience of over a hundred journalists. "It's going to be okay," he added. "It's going to be special. Paris is one of my favorite cities," agreed Durant. LeBron, Curry and Durant team up to secure another gold for the United States In addition to the risk of a terrorist attack at this Friday's ceremony, the polarization of American politics also featured in the interview. Curry is a close friend of US Vice President Kamala Harris and said he was "excited about the journey she has ahead of her". "VP [Vice President] Harris is going to bring energy to this election and hopefully win. It's going to be a monumental few months, an interesting time for our country," said Curry, speaking about politics with an ease unusual among Olympic athletes. Durant did not speak on the subject. US President Joe Biden withdrew this week from running for re-election, and Harris is expected to be the Democratic Party's candidate on the November ballot. Despite their similar ages, their Olympic experience is very different. Durant already has three gold medals, while Curry accepted the call-up for the first time. "I'm going to lean on guys like him, that learning experience is contagious," said Curry. The Americans won all five of their warm-up matches, against Canada, Australia, Serbia, South Sudan and Germany, but suffered in their last two games. Durant is recovering from a calf injury and did not play. The best three-point shooter in history, Curry said that the friendlies helped him adapt to the international federation's shooting line, which is closer to the basket than in the NBA (6.75 m compared to 7.24 m). "You get used to it in five games. I know where I am in relation to the line. The thing is not to think and just shoot." The pair of basketball legends are part of what is perhaps the most anticipated team since the Dream Team of Barcelona-1992. Also in the group are LeBron James, 39, Anthony Davis, 31, and Jayson Tatum, 26. Curry and Durant mentioned only two players from rival teams when asked about the American favoritism: Victor Wembanyama, from France, and Nikola Jokic, from Serbia, the USA's opponents in the opening game on Sunday (28). "Wemby is one of the guys who can make basketball evolve. On a big stage [like the Games], he's going to grow as a person. Everyone is curious to see him," Durant said of the 20-year-old, 2.22m Frenchman from the San Antonio Spurs. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The United States will face Serbia in its first men's basketball game at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games in Group C on Sunday, July 28. Team USA makes its debut at Stade Pierre-Mauroy Stadium in the city of Villeneuve-d'Ascq as it begins its quest for another gold medal, following four consecutive gold medal wins from Beijing 2008 to Tokyo 2020. LeBron James, standard-bearer of the U.S. delegation in the French capital, will lead his team accompanied by other NBA greats such as Stephen Curry , Anthony Davis, Jayson Tatum and Joel Embiid. For its part, Serbia, silver medalist in Rio 2016, returns to the Olympic Games after failing to qualify for Tokyo 2020 and will count on Nikola Jokic , three-time NBA MVP (2021, 2022 and 2024) and Bogdan Bogdanovic, one of the figures who led the team to the world runner-up in Philippines 2023. Both teams come in with a good run of friendly games under their belts. Team USA has won five consecutive games, although its performance has been inconsistent, most notably a close 101-100 win over South Sudan. The Serbs, under coach Svetislav Pesi, won their last two games against Japan and Greece, but suffered a 105-79 loss to the United States on July 17 in the United Arab Emirates. Zone C also includes South Sudan and Puerto Rico , who will meet on July 28 at 11 a.m. local time. Three days later, Puerto Rico will play Serbia (5:15 p.m.) and the United States will play South Sudan (9 p.m.). On August 3, the United States will play Puerto Rico (5:15 p.m.) and Serbia against South Sudan (9:00 p.m.). Names like LeBron James , Kevin Durant , Stephen Curry , a true constellation of stars that have already reached the Paris Games , and already have their first victory on their way to the fifth consecutive gold . DURANT AND LEBRON, THE PROTAGONISTS Durant scored 21 flawless points with 5 three-pointers in just eight minutes in the first half, crazy what he was doing. And who would have thought that on this occasion his best partner would be LeBron James , who by the way became the oldest player to play for Team USA, 39 years old . A scandal the 'Dream Team ' roster, which even had the luxury of leaving on the bench the Pacers' wizard, Tyrese Haliburton, and the Celtics' leader, Jayson Tatum; a crazy thing to do. Team USA 's next game at the Paris Games will be against South Sudan next Wednesday, July 31 at 14:00 hrs. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Basketball has been one of the most high-profile events at the Olympics ever since Michael Jordan, Magic Johnson and Larry Bird teamed up on the Dream Team more than 30 years ago. Yet megastars like LeBron James, Stephen Curry , Kevin Durant, Victor Wembanyama, Giannis Antetokounmpo, Nikola Jokic and Canada's own Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, plus many of the world's best women's players, are playing large chunks of this year's hoops tournament more than 200 kilometres from Paris. Those athletes who wanted to participate in the opening ceremony (including some like James and Antetokounmpo who served as flag-bearers) had to spend hours on buses or trains to get to Paris. Some countries, including Canada, opted to skip the opening festivities, because they played the next day and prioritized preparations. That might not have been necessary had the basketball tournament gone on as originally intended. Basketball got booted north to Lille, close to France's border with Belgium, even though Paris has a basketball -specific stadium, Accor Arena, for the action, and Lille's massive soccer stadium needed to have air conditioning added in the late stages, leading to talks between NBA commissioner Adam Silver and French President Emmanuel Macron, according to The Athletic. Basketball was moved because pandemic-related cost increases scuttled the building of a swimming venue and forced some event shuffling. That included moving gymnastics to Accor Arena, sending hoops to Lille until the medal round, at which point the games will be played at Accor Arena. A new NBA calibre arena, Adidas Arena, just opened in Paris, but badminton and rhythmic gymnastics were placed there. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Two-thirds of LeBron James' annual income is not earned playing basketball . On the court, the Lakers forward pockets a salary of close to $48 million per season, but when he's not playing, he's a savvy businessman and sports icon who generates more than $81 million in sponsorships, with Nike being his main sponsor, with a $30 million per year deal until retirement. Always in the middle of figures such as CR7 and Lionel Messi in the list published annually by Forbes magazine on the sportsmen with the highest annual income, The King has license to boast about everything: from his bank accounts and multiple businesses, to the fact that he only uses his right hand for basketball , because he signs checks, contracts and autographs with his left hand. In 2023, Forbes announced that LeBron had become the first billionaire basketball player in NBA history. Today, at 39, he leads the mighty USA Dream Team in search of his third gold medal in an Olympic Games (he won gold in Beijing 2008 and London 2012, while in Athens 2004 he won the silver medal). Yes, although no one is saying it, Paris 2024 will be their last time at the Olympics and perhaps that is also why Team USA has set out for the Pierre-Mauroy stadium in Lille, determined to overwhelm all their opponents with the NBA All Stars, the best basketball league in the world. Stephen Curry , Kevin Durant, Jayson Tatum, Joel Embiid and Anthony Davis stand out in the dream team that seems to find no rival on its way to the golden medal and, incidentally, opens the debate on whether it is comparable to the Dream Team led by the legend of Michael Jordan in the Olympic edition of Barcelona 1992. A duel of Dream Teams Dream Team 1992AgeDream Team 2024Age Magic Johnson 32 Stephen Curry 36 Michael Jordan 29 Jayson Tatum 26 Charles Barkley 29 Kevin Durant 35 Karl Malone 28 LeBron James 39 Patrick Ewing 29 Joel Embiid 30 LeBron, who wears No. 23 in honor of Jordan, can score, assist and defend as naturally as he has been making his fortune for the past 21 seasons. That versatility, in addition to his physical strength, exquisite vision and tactical intelligence, make the NBA's leading scorer the ideal leader for a Team that looks indestructible with one of the best three-point shooters in Curry, the explosive dribbling of Durant, the multiple resources of Tatum and a sensational offensive repertoire of Davis. And if that's not enough, there's Embiid's mind-blowing dunks to immediately challenge any quintet. This Saturday, Steve Kerr's team crushed Puerto Rico 104-83, which added up to its third crushing victory and a perfect 3-0 record to advance to the quarterfinals. Team USA had opened with a 110-84 win over Serbia and strengthened its bid with a 103-86 win over South Sudan. Hateful comparisons The Dream Team's breakthrough at Barcelona 1992 was colossal. Not only because it was the first time the United States participated with its NBA stars, but also because of the overwhelming ease with which it overwhelmed all its rivals to reach the gold medal, with an average difference of 43.8 points per game, with the biggest thrashing against Angola (48-116). The expertise of Magic Johnson and Larry Bird exploded with the flatness of an intractable Michael Jordan, who also had outstanding players such as Scottie Pippen, Charles Barkley (who would end up as top scorer) and Karl Malone. It was the golden generation, the precise moment in which Magic's hegemony gave way to Jordan's splendor. Another no less important detail: unlike the 1992 Dream Team, the team led by LeBron does not arrive at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games at its peak of performance or at the ideal age. Of course, each of the members exert superiority from their records and trophies, but some years after reaching their "prime", those in which LeBron was the real The King and figures like Kevin Durand and Stephen Curry were the terror of all rivals. Like the 2016-2018 Real Madrid or Tom Brady's Patriots, Jordan's Dream Team is remembered with glory for the accumulation of stars that made it up, but mainly for its impeccable ability to play as a team. And it is in this last detail where LeBron's All Stars have -still- some unfinished business. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 HYPERLINK https://www.nacion.com/resizer/v2/M47YYNCQAJBMBPD2MRZIBO6HHU.jpg?smart=true&auth=&width=7458&height=4972 00 HYPERLINK https://www.nacion.com/resizer/v2/M47YYNCQAJBMBPD2MRZIBO6HHU.jpg?smart=true&auth=&width=7458&height=4972 00 Stephen Curry HYPERLINK https://www.nacion.com/resizer/v2/M47YYNCQAJBMBPD2MRZIBO6HHU.jpg?smart=true&auth=&width=7458&height=4972 00 lucha contra Ognjen Dobric en la intensa semifinal del baloncesto masculino entre Estados Unidos y Serbia en los Juegos Ol\'edmpicos Par\'eds 2024. Stephen Curry was decisive for the United States, which will fight for the gold medal against France in men's basketball at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. (AFP/AFP) With 36 points from Golden State Warriors star Stephen Curry , the United States managed to reverse the score against Serbia, beating it 95-91 and moving into the men's basketball final of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. The Dream Team had to accelerate in the last quarter, and with a 32-15 run, was able to overcome the 63-76 lead that the Europeans held at the end of the third quarter. Serbia dominated the first three quarters with a 31-23, 23-20 and 22-20 lead. With quick transitions and great precision in long distance shots, the Serbians complicated the actions of the United States, which gave too many liberties in defense, very well exploited by the Europeans. Nikola Joki?, the Denver Nuggets star, had 17 points, 11 assists and five rebounds, keeping the United States at bay along with Bogdan Bogdanovi?, who was the best offensive player with 21 points. However, the Dream Team reacted and, with 3:39 left in the game, managed to even the score and, in the next play, took the lead and did not lose the advantage until the end. At the end of the game, the hierarchy of Curry, the physical power of LeBron James and the contribution on the boards of Kevin Durant finally gave the victory to the United States, which will face France in the fight for the gold medal. Meanwhile, Serbia will face Germany for the bronze medal in the Parisian Games. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Basketball world comments with amazement on U.S.-Serbia game. Jul. 31July. 31 'What a great game,' is what everyone is saying. Long live basketball ! Stephen Curry , the hero of the Olympic semifinal against Serbia with 36 points, tried to downplay his personal performance by noting that "that's the beauty of the U.S. team, every day it's someone different." "I did what the game needed, I had a lot of confidence," he said in remarks at the end of the intense game that his team won in a hurry 95-91 after trailing for almost the entire duel. Read here: After hitting 9 of 14 three-point attempts, he explained that he made the first few shots and from there "I got into a rhythm." In fact, he scored 17 of his team's 23 points in the first quarter. Curry, acknowledged that after reaching halftime with a 13-point deficit, in the third quarter "we changed everything, with great defense." "They played a great game, with great shots in the first half (...) but we found a way to pull through," Curry, who at 36 is the best three-point shooter in NBA history and has won four NBA rings with the Golden State Warriors, acknowledged about the Serbians. Kerr, very proud U.S. coach Steve Kerr said after their Olympic men's basketball semifinal win over Serbia (95-91) that he is very proud of the team and it was an unbelievable game. "Serbia played a very good perfect game and they forced us to the highest level of competition we could find and in the end in that last quarter they did it. I am very proud of the team. Like I said, I'm honored to be part of this. It was just an unbelievable game," he said. The man with 9 NBA championship rings, 5 as a player and 4 as a coach all with the Golden State Warriors, emphasized that all the U.S. players "were incredible" and that it was an "unbelievable game to watch". Kerr noted that Stephen Curry " did it again": " I think everyone on our team and coaching staff knew he was going to have a game. It was just a matter of time. I wasn't surprised that he was like that tonight that we needed him, the night we desperately needed him, He's the ultimate competitor." fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Little by little the last days of activities in Paris 2024 are approaching, and one of them is the men's basketball final, where on August 10 the so-called 'Dream Team' will face France to decide the winner of the gold medal in these Olympic Games. During the semifinals, the U.S. team suffered on more than one occasion thanks to the pressure exerted by Serbia, its rival, which came within a hair's breadth of knocking the team from the neighboring country out of the competition. After defeating Serbia and Puerto Rico in the group stage, Brazil in the quarterfinals and the Serbians again, the Dream Team's last rival, made up of basketball players like Stephen Curry , will face the hosts for the gold medal. Paris 2024: How did USA vs. Serbia fare in men's basketball semifinals? Stephen Curry scored 36 points, including the three-pointer that gave them the lead for good with 2:16 to play. The U.S. trailed by as many as 17 points in the first half and went into the break with an 11-point deficit (the largest the team has been able to turn around since it began calling NBA stars in 1992). Serbia led by 11 with 7:19 on the clock. The rest was all USA. The game turned completely around in the fourth period in a crazy sequence in which the U.S. scored six points in two seconds. Kevin Durant sank a three-pointer at the same time teammate Anthony Davis was fouled. The U.S. was awarded another possession and Devin Booker (after receiving the pass) sank another three-pointer. In the blink of an eye, what was a 78-67 lead for Serbia became 78-73. The comeback was on and Curry's three-pointer put the U.S. up for good. He also sank a pair of free throws with 8.2 seconds to play and the U.S. finally breathed a sigh of relief. Paris 2024 Men's Basketball Final: Where and when to watch it from Mexico for free? One day before the closing of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games, the men's basketball final will be played between the two teams that have shown the most skill, strategy and, above all, courage to win the gold medal. Also, that same day, the bronze medal will be disputed between Serbia, who lost against the United States, and Germany. If you don't want to miss these matches, here are the details. United States vs. France: Fight for the gold medal Date: Saturday, August 10, 2024 Time: 1:30 p.m. Central Mexican Time. Broadcast: You will be able to watch the match on Claro Sports' YouTube channel. Germany vs. Serbia: Fight for the Bronze Medal Day: Saturday, August 10, 2024 Time: 3:00 a.m. Central Mexican Time. Broadcast: You will be able to watch the match on Claro Sports' YouTube channel. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The U.S. men's basketball team, led by NBA superstars LeBron James and Stephen Curry , will face Serbia and its ace Nikola Jokic in a thrilling semifinal matchup of the . The "Dream Team", with its constellation of stars, will seek to maintain its undefeated record in the tournament and ensure its passage to the final, while the Serbians will try to pull off a surprise with the help of the three-time NBA MVP. Therefore, in this article we tell you everything you need to know in the run-up to this exciting match: when Team USA and Serbia play, date and time, all the previews and how to watch the game live. When do USA and Serbia play? The clash between the United States and Serbia in the semifinals of the men's basketball tournament of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games will take place on Thursday, August 8 at 4:00 pm (Argentine time) at the Bercy Arena. The game will be broadcasted by TyC Sports and can also be watched via streaming through Claro Sports on YouTube. The American team arrives at this stage with a perfect record in the tournament, showing their power in each presentation. In the quarterfinals, they swept Brazil with a convincing 122-87, making it clear that they are going for everything. The Dream Team is seeking its fifth consecutive Olympic gold medal, an achievement that would further consolidate its dominance in international basketball . The U.S. roster is a true "who's who" of world basketball . LeBron James, looking to extend his Olympic legacy, leads a team brimming with talent. Stephen Curry , with his masterful three-point shooting, Anthony Davis dominating in the paint, and the emerging Anthony Edwards, are just some of the weapons coach Steve Kerr has at his disposal. Team USA is expected to display its fast and explosive play, taking advantage of its roster depth to maintain an intense pace throughout the game. Aggressive defense and the ability to generate points in transition will be key in their strategy to neutralize the Serbian threat. For their part, Serbia comes into this semifinal after an epic comeback in the quarterfinals against Australia, where they overcame a 24-point deficit to win in overtime. This triumph demonstrated the grit and determination of the Balkan team, which now faces its toughest challenge in the tournament. The Serbian team revolves around the imposing figure of Nikola Jokic, reigning NBA champion with the Denver Nuggets and considered by many to be the best player in the world today. His ability to score, rebound and assist makes him a constant threat. Alongside him, players like Bogdan Bogdanovic and Vasilije Micic complete a talented and experienced team. Recent history between the two teams favors the United States, which won two previous meetings this summer: an exhibition game in Abu Dhabi and in the group stage of these same Olympic Games. However, Serbia will be looking to reverse this trend and strike the big blow at the most important moment. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 On the way to the comeback. On course for victory. On the road to another final for the gold medal in men's basketball at the Olympic Games. France and the United States will face each other for the coveted medal in a duel that promises great emotions. For the hosts to fight for their dream final , especially to stand up to the almighty United States , which, also considered as the big favorite from the first game, has seen (and suffer) in the last hurdle against Serbia. France and the USA complete their road to Paris 2024 by reaching the finals of the tournament. On the USA side of LeBron James, Adebayo, Durant and company, Stephen Curry scored 36 points , including the three-pointer that gave them the lead with 2:16 to play, and the Americans will seek gold at the Paris Games after beating Serbia 95-91 on Thursday. But that's because the U.S. was down by as many as 17 points in the first half. They went into halftime with an 11-point deficit, the largest the team has been able to reverse since NBA stars began being called up in 1992. With the United States, however, you can never rest easy. The Serbia squad, which lost its chance to reach the final in the last three minutes of its preliminary round game, knows this firsthand. Stephen Curry and LeBron James combined for 52 points. Thus, they arrive in their best moment of the tournament alongside high-danger teammates like Kevin Durant and Joel Embiid. France's dream final in Paris 2024 For the Gallic side, France will face the United States after having had a road that has gone from strength to strength . Their Olympic campaign in group A and stumbled in their debut against Germany. They lost 85-71 and questions began to be raised as to whether this team would really be up to the task of leading France to the medal race, at home and in front of their own fans. But good games against Japan, Brazil, Canada and again Germany, that bogeyman that filled them with doubts in their Olympic debut, led their qualification to finally fight for the coveted gold in the men's basketball final. The gold between France and the United States will be on Saturday, August 10, at 9:30 pm. The men's basketball final between France and the United States will be next Saturday , August 10, at 21:30 , at the Accor Arena in Paris, venue of the water polo finals. On the same day, Germany and Serbia will play for the bronze medal. France will play its fourth Olympic final after losing the 1948, 2000 and 2021 finals, all precisely against the United States. Where to watch on TV The match for the gold medal in men's basketball in Paris 2024 will be broadcast on RTVE channels , on La 1 and Teledeporte. And also with the option of watching it through its on-demand streaming platform RTVE Play. As an alternative to public television, Eurosport will also broadcast the France - United States with its two channels and the MAX application, available on DAZN. You can follow all the information about the day of the Olympic Games through the website of El Independiente. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 PARIS They scored six points in two seconds. And just like that, against what looked like overwhelming odds, the U.S. Olympic men's basketball team was on its way. To a comeback. To a win. And to another Olympic gold medal game. Stephen Curry scored 36 points, including a go-ahead 3-pointer with 2:16 left, and the U.S. will play for gold in the Paris Olympics on Saturday night after beating Serbia 95-91. The U.S. trailed by 17 points in the first half, plus faced an 11-point halftime deficit the biggest one successfully overcome by an American team since NBA players were added to the Olympic mix in 1992. "I've seen a lot of Team USA basketball ," Curry said. "And that was a special one." Serbia led by 11 with 7:19 left. The rest of the way, all U.S. The Americans won a game where they led for 3 minutes and 25 seconds. Serbia led for 35:12 nearly 90% of the game. But it'll be the U.S. against host France for gold in a dream matchup for the host nation on Saturday, while Serbia will face Germany earlier Saturday for bronze. The gold-medal game is a rematch from the Tokyo Games three years ago, where the Americans prevailed 87-82. "I'm really humbled to have been a part of this game," U.S. coach Steve Kerr said, tipping his hat multiple times to Serbia. "It's one of the greatest basketball games I've ever been a part of. They were perfect. They played a perfect game. Our coaches were saying, Villanova-Georgetown, for all of our older readers and viewers out there. "But they played the perfect game, and they forced us to reach the highest level of competition that we could find. And our guys were incredible in that fourth quarter, and they got it done." Joel Embiid scored 19 points on 8 for 11 shooting and LeBron James added 16 for the U.S., which is now assured of its 20th medal in 20 Olympic appearances. "Joel was everything," James said. "Made every big shot." Bogdan Bogdanovic scored 20 points for Serbia, which got 17 from Nikola Jokic and 15 from Aleksa Avramovic. The Serbians were 0-3 against the U.S. this summer, getting blown out in the first two meetings and then looking poised for a win of their own on Thursday. The whole game changed in the fourth after a wild sequence saw the U.S. score six points in two seconds. Kevin Durant made a 3-pointer while Anthony Davis was getting fouled. The U.S. got another possession, and off the inbounds Devin Booker made another 3-pointer. Just like that, a 78-67 lead for Serbia was down to 78-73. The comeback was on, and Curry's 3 put the U.S. up for good. He added a pair of free throws with 8.2 seconds left to make it a two-possession game and the Americans escaped. Curry's 36 points were the second-most in a game by a U.S. men's player in Olympic history, one shy of Carmelo Anthony's record 37 against Nigeria in 2012. And Anthony, sitting courtside, was Curry's biggest cheerleader. When the Americans tied it with about 3:35 left, Curry gave Anthony a big smile. When the go-ahead 3 rattled home, Anthony leapt from his seat. And when it was over, the two shared a long embrace. "That was a godlike performance," Durant said of Curry. Serbia coach Svetislav Petic kept saying the same thing all summer, that the team the U.S. put together for the Paris Games was better than any in Olympic history. Even the Dream Team, he insisted. His mind wasn't changed after this one. "They're that good," he said. A barrage of Serbian 3-pointers had the Americans squarely on the ropes, until the game changed in the fourth. But in the end, the U.S. improved to 144-6 all-time in Olympic play, 63-4 since NBA players were added to the Olympics in 1992. "Perseverance, hard work dedication, Chef Curry and Joel 'Process' Embiid," James said. "Big-time win for us. We knew we were going to be challenged. We knew it was going to be the toughest game to date. Total effort." The comeback kept Durant's hopes of becoming the first four-time Olympic men's gold medalist very much alive, and gave the U.S. a chance to return to the international basketball mountaintop. The Americans were fourth at the World Cup last summer, a result that spurred some NBA stars like James and Curry to be part of this team. And of all the U.S. semifinal moments, this was like few others. The four-point final margin was the second closest in American history in the Olympic semifinals; the U.S. beat Lithuania by two in the 2000 semifinals in Sydney, on the way to gold there. They're one win away from another. "I'm 39 years old, going into my 22nd season, I don't know how many opportunities and moments I'm going to get like this to compete for something, compete for something big and play in big games," James said. "And tonight was a big game." Highlight: A flurry of Stephen Curry 3-pointers helped the United States survive a French challenge and win a fifth straight gold medal. Body_1 Body_1 Body fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Stephen Curry had French defenders draped all over him like shrink wrap, but it hardly mattered. Fans across the world had seen this show before; only the stage was different. Playing in his first Olympics, Curry made the most of the moment, sinking a series of late 3-pointers each more preposterous than the last to lead the United States to a 98-87 win over France in the men s basketball gold medal game. Curry, the longtime face of the Golden State Warriors, scored 24 points as the United States won its fifth straight gold medal. He had plenty of help from fellow N.B.A. stars like Devin Booker and Kevin Durant, who scored 15 points apiece, and LeBron James, who added 14 points and collected his third gold medal this time with flecks of gray in his beard. The United States has now won gold in men s basketball at eight of the last nine Olympics, a stretch of dominance that dates to 1992 with the formation of the so-called Dream Team at the Barcelona Games. Since then, the sport s global growth has meant that the talent gap has closed. Yes, the Americans won all six of their games in Paris. But they were threatened in the semifinals by Serbia, a team headlined by Nikola Jokic, the three-time N.B.A. most valuable player, trailing by as many as 17 points before escaping with a victory. That left the United States with a chance for gold against France, which was led by Victor Wembanyama, 20, one of the N.B.A. s emerging stars. At 7-foot-4, Wembanyama caused problems for a host of American defenders. He scored a game-high 26 points, but it was not quite enough not against Curry, who made 8 of 13 3-pointers, and not against a U.S. team that was pushed but was not about to be broken. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 READ MORE: Doug Emhoff and Megan Rapinoe take in USA game vs. France READ MORE: LeBron James finds himself on the wrong end of WILD dunk France had the homecourt advantage and rising star Victor Wembanyama ; but the USA had Stephen Curry , and that was all that mattered in the dying stages of Saturday's gold medal match as Team USA won its fifth straight Olympic gold medal in men's basketball . The USA held off France 98-87 behind 24 points from Curry, as a star-studded group including LeBron James and Kevin Durant thwarted away a late French rally. The Americans led by as much as 14 in the fourth quarter but saw that advantage dwindle to just three after a Victor Wembanyama tip-in with just under three minutes remaining. From there, Curry caught fire as he hit four three-pointers from the 2:48 mark on to see the Americans to the gold. His final dagger came during a broken play with 35 seconds left, when he heaved in a three-pointer over the arms of Evan Fournier and Nicolas Batum to restore the lead to nine for the USA. There was no way back for France, and Curry knew it as he did his famous 'night night celebration' - with the last half-minute proving unclimactic for the USA. James, who was later named the MVP of the men's basketball competition, finished the game with 14 points, six rebounds and nine assists while Durant and Devin Booker both added 15 points for the eventual winners. 'I've been blessed to play basketball at a high level for a very long time,' Curry told NBC's Mike Tirico afterwards. '... This ranks very high in terms of the excitement and the sense of relief getting to the finish line.' USA head coach Steve Kerr, who also coaches Curry with the Golden State Warriors, added, 'It\'b4s right up there with all of the greatest games of his career. 'The shot-making was just incredible. But under the circumstances, on the road, in Paris, against France for a gold medal, this is storybook stuff. But that\'b4s what Steph does. He likes to be in storybooks.' Wembanyama impressed in the loss with 26 points and seven rebounds but his individual brilliance wasn't enough, and he was visibly upset after the game as he covered his face in a towel. The USA lead was 14 early in the third, looking poised to pull away. But the offense quickly went cold and when Evan Fournier connected on a 3-pointer with 3:05 left in the quarter the lead was down to 65-59 - a 12-4 run by the hosts. And with a chance to go up double-digits headed to the fourth, a big USA blunder gave France another jolt of momentum. Anthony Edwards and Durant got their signals crossed on a pass that led to a turnover, Nando De Colo scored to beat the buzzer and the U.S. lead was only 72-66 going into the final 10 minutes. From there, though, Curry and the heavy favorites heading into these Olympics did enough to avoid the ignominy of a loss in the finals. 'I think we might be the only team in the world whose fans are ashamed of them if they get a silver medal,' Kerr said afterwards. 'That's the pressure that we face. 'But our players, and you saw Steph, they love the pressure. They appreciate this atmosphere and they were fantastic.' The win also makes Durant the first four-time men\'b4s gold medalist in Olympic basketball history, while James now has three medals and Curry claimed his first gold medal. 'It is special,' Curry said. 'Two years ago, when we [the Golden State Warriors] won our last championship, I knew this was going to be on the horizon. Any mission that you take on, you give it everything you have.' fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The "Dream Team," led by LeBron James, managed to reach the podium once again and win the basketball championship at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. After a historic comeback against Serbia , the toughest task for Team USA came: the home team France. However, the United States would impose its hierarchy and thus beat the French team by 98-87 points. Who is Stephen Curry ? Wardell Stephen Curry II, commonly known as Stephen Curry , is a renowned American basketball player and member of the Golden State Warriors . He has also been a key part of the United States team that recently won the gold medal at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. Since his early days in the NBA, Curry demonstrated unquestionable talent by breaking one record after another. For example, in 2015, with only 369 official games, Stephen Curry reached the historic mark of 1,000 three-pointers scored , becoming the fastest player to do so. In reaching this milestone, Curry proved to be the missing piece in the Warriors' puzzle, leading them to their first title in 40 years. In addition, his ability to make three-pointers at 42.8% efficiency not only gave him unprecedented recognition, but transformed the way basketball is played, making him one of the most influential players of his generation. Why was Stephen Curry instrumental in winning the gold medal at Paris 2024? Although the "Dream Team" is made up of American basketball stars such as LeBron James, during the latter stages of the tournament the quality and hierarchy of more players like Stephen Curry , who played an equally important role in both the semifinals and the final of the tournament in Paris 2024, was noticeable. Curry and LeBron James were the architects of the memorable comeback against the very tough Serbia team; overcoming a 17-point deficit , they managed to turn the scoreboard around and come within one step of the gold medal with a final score of 95-91. However, the Golden State Warriors star would not end his participation in the tournament with the incredible feat of 9 three-pointers. During the game against France , Curry was the undisputed protagonist of the final, with a stellar performance that included 8 three-pointers and 24 points, sentencing the game in favor of the "Dream Team". fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The United States won the gold medal in men's basketball for the fifth consecutive edition of the Olympic Games, after a 98-87 victory over host France in the final of this discipline in Paris 2024. Stephen Curry led the Dream Team , which has won the gold medal in this sport in every edition since Beijing 2008, as the last other champion was Argentina in Athens 2004 . This is the 17th gold medal for the United States in men's basketball at the Olympic Games. Munich 1972, Moscow 1980, Seoul 1988 and Athens 2004 are the only editions in which the northern neighbors did not win the gold medal. The United States confirmed its status as the favorite in Paris 2024 by bringing its best possible squad with elements such as LeBron James, Jrue Holiday, Joel Embiid, Kevin Durant, Bam Adebayo and Stephen Curry , among others. Stephen Curry was unleashed with four three-pointers in the last 2:43 minutes of play, including the shot that sealed the win with 1:19 to play. He put the U.S. up 93-84, and moved all over the Bercy Arena court to shout and stick out his chest showing the initials "USA" on his jersey. If that wasn't enough, the point guard proceeded with another three-pointer (with 30 seconds to play) and followed with the "go to sleep" gesture in which he puts his hands to the side of his face. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The Dream Team has done it again. The great stars of the United States have defeated the host France and have been proclaimed as the new Olympic Champions . LeBron James, Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant led LeBron James, Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant to a 98-87 win and the gold medal. The start of the grand final in Paris 2024 was very intense, both teams thrilled their fans with every play. France, as the home team, had the obligation to look for the victory, but in front of them they had the "Dream Team", also with the obligation to look for the title after several Olympic Games without being at the top, and even more now that they had a real "trabuco" in their ranks. The United States, at the moment they looked for it, positioned themselves as the team to beat, although France also put up a fight. In the first quarter they were very evenly matched with a 20-15 in favor of the Stars and Stripes. This far from making a difference in the following quarters only became more evident, both had everything to win. With numbers of 29-26, 23-25 and 26-21 the duel was completed to achieve the final figures of 98-87. And although the numbers were even, the United States had on their side a Stephen Curry who made at least three 3-point shots in the last quarter that confirmed the title. The victory for the United States represents their 17th title in the history of the Olympic Games. In history only 3 teams have been able to beat them: USSR, Yugoslavia and Argentina. The silver went to the French team, while the bronze went to Serbia, who hours earlier defeated the German team, thus consummating the Olympic basketball of Paris 2024. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Luis Miguel Pascual Paris, Aug 10 (EFE) - He has been one of the stars of the Paris Games, adulated by the public, acclaimed by the country that looks at him as one of the greats of basketball , but Victor Wembanyama has failed to dethrone the United States in the final for the gold. The young player, 20 years old, has signed the best numbers of the Olympic runner-up, with 26 points in the final he was the top scorer and surpassed his own record with the national team, but it was not enough to counteract the thrust of Stephen Curry , 24 points, more decisive in the duel. His tears at the end of the duel show how high his own expectations are and the illusion with which he defends his country's jersey. Wemby' lacked the experience and maturity to take France to the next level, although he was a key player for the hosts to reach the final. After signing stratospheric numbers in his first year with the San Antonio Spurs, the No. 1 pick in the last draft arrived as the great hope of French basketball , to try to overcome a step that eludes the Gauls, like the rest of the world in front of the NBA stars. No one thought that Wembanyama's magic alone would be enough to derail the locomotive of Stephen Curry , Lebron James or Kevin Durant, but it was enough to raise the level of play and, above all, to arouse enthusiasm for the basketball team. The young player, who until a few months ago distilled his talent in a small pavilion on the outskirts of Paris, where he was under the orders of coach Vincent Collet, has achieved both objectives. Although his numbers are below expectations, both the coach and the players recognize that he has influenced the way they play, and has been able to integrate himself into the team, bringing a higher level of play. Since the games prior to the start of the Games, Wemby has managed to seduce a mass of French people eager to witness, in the flesh, their new international star. Meanwhile, Wembanyama himself has remained in his own bubble. He has played the game of the media star, attending presentations of the fashion brand that sponsors him and that commissioned the singer and designer Pharrel Williams to create a custom-made suit for him, or signing autographs for fans. But he has also been able to take refuge from that expectation, inside the team's bubble, outside the Olympic Village to avoid the hullabaloo affecting the preparation. In his now legendary precocious maturity, 'Wemby' let slip his dissatisfaction with his own performance, but he also knew how to contextualize it. The player, of exceptional size, 221 centimeters (although the NBA lists 224), with a wingspan of 234 centimeters, and unexpected electric movements for his profile, has taken another step in the process of becoming a planetary star. Born in Le Chesnay, a quiet town on the outskirts of Paris into a family of athletes, mother a former player and gymnastics teacher and daughter of former basketball players, father a former triple jumper of Congolese origin, 'Wemby' did not wait many years to start saying at home that he dreamed of being a legend. Precocious in all youth categories, the first Frenchman to be number 1 in the draft, he has been giving success to France's youth categories for years. His leap to the senior team has been spectacular and in a dozen games he has already become the benchmark. Along with Antoine Dupont, L\'e9on Marchand, Tedy Rinner, 'Wemby' is, by his own merit, one of the French stars of these Games, even if he is a silver medalist. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 PARIS Stephen Curry was thinking about this two years ago, after winning his fourth NBA title with the Golden State Warriors. The only thing left for him to win was Olympic gold. And in the ultimate moment, he made sure that medal would be his. The U.S. is atop the international men's basketball world once again, after Curry scored 24 points and led the way to a 98-87 win over France in the final at the Paris Games on Saturday night. It was the fifth consecutive gold medal for the U.S. and the 17th in 20 all-time appearances for the Americans at the games. Curry made four 3-pointers in the final 2:43, including the one that just sealed the win with 1:19 remaining. It put the U.S. up 93-84 and he skipped down the court letting out a yell, shaking his jersey so everyone could see the "USA" across the front. If that wasn't enough, one more followed with about 30 seconds left with the "go to sleep" move where he puts his hands on the side of his face. Good night. Game over. Gold won. Again. "For me to get a gold medal is insane, and I thank God for the opportunity to experience it," Curry said. Kevin Durant the first four-time men's gold medalist in Olympic basketball history scored 15 for the Americans, as did Devin Booker. And LeBron James, wearing metallic gold shoes that needed no explanation, scored 14 for the U.S. as he won his fourth Olympic medal and third gold. For the second consecutive Olympics, the French had to watch the Americans hold out U.S. flags in celebration after the title game. The French lost to the U.S. 87-82 in Tokyo three years ago, and this one was down to the final minutes. That is, until Curry took over. "I think we might be the only team in the world whose fans are ashamed of them if they get a silver medal," said U.S. coach Steve Kerr, the Golden State coach whose run with the U.S. ends now with a 21-3 record and Olympic gold 11-0 this summer. "That's the pressure that we face. But our players, and you saw Steph, they love the pressure. They appreciate this atmosphere and they were fantastic." Victor Wembanyama, in his first Olympic final, was brilliant for France, scoring 26 points the second-most ever against the U.S. in a gold-medal game, one point behind the 27 that Drazen Dalipagic scored for Yugoslavia in 1976. Wembanyama covered his face in a towel afterward as the Americans celebrated. Guerschon Yabusele scored 20 for the hosts. "For sure, it's a disappointment because we expected we could do it," France coach Vincent Collet said. "But we have to recognize at the end that they are better. We are very close "\'a6 When they make fantastic shots, that's the difference." The U.S. lead was 14 early in the third, looking poised to pull away. But the offense quickly went cold and when Evan Fournier connected on a 3-pointer with 3:05 left in the quarter the lead was down to 65-59 a 12-4 run by the hosts. And with a chance to go up double-digits headed to the fourth, a big U.S. blunder gave France another jolt of momentum. Anthony Edwards and Durant got their signals crossed on a pass that led to a turnover, Nando De Colo scored to beat the buzzer and the U.S. lead was only 72-66 going into the final 10 minutes. It got as close as three. No closer, thanks to Curry. It was four 3-pointers in a span of 2:12, and they immediately went into Olympic lore. "A big shot to put us up six. That kind of settled everything," Curry said. "And then the rhythm, the avalanche came, and thankfully the other three went in. That was an unbelievable moment. I've been blessed to play basketball at a high level for a very long time. This ranks very high in terms of excitement and the sense of relief, getting to the finish line." For James, it was one more thing for the neverending list that is his legacy. For Durant, it was history with four golds. For Booker, Anthony Davis, Jayson Tatum and Bam Adebayo, it was a second gold. For Jrue Holiday, it was a second gold to match his wife soccer great Lauren Cheney Holiday for the family lead. For Derrick White, Tyrese Haliburton, Joel Embiid and Anthony Edwards, it was the first Olympic title. And for Curry, it was a long time coming. The Olympics never fit into his schedule until now. The Americans couldn't have been more thrilled that he was there for this one. "It's everything I wanted them to be," Curry said. "And more." He likened it to a Game 7 on the road, which it basically was. He's had enormous success in those moments: a 50-point outburst to lead Golden State past Sacramento in 2023, and a 27-point, nine-rebound, 10-assist game to win a do-or-die in Houston in 2018. And now, this. "It's right up there with all of the greatest games of his career," Kerr said. "The shot-making was just incredible. But under the circumstances, on the road, in Paris, against France for a gold medal, this is storybook stuff. But that's what Steph does. He likes to be in storybooks." Gra

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Someone will tell you years later, as time goes by, that one day he saw Stephen Curry play in an Olympic basketball final. The NBA is seen in Spain in the wee hours of the morning, except on Sundays, and although we are facing a generational athlete, capable of inspiring a different kind of basketball and of 'changing the rules of the game' with his shooting and his huge production of three-pointers, it is true that perhaps many fans of the sport, taking advantage of the great Olympic showcase, are now discovering the prodigious 36-year-old point guard after his memorable performance in the game for the gold against France in Paris Bercy. As everyone knows by now, Curry quelled the French revolution with four three-pointers in four consecutive plays that decided the game and the Olympic title in the last five minutes . For a player who was making his debut in the Games despite his brilliant career (four rings, two World Championships, two times NBA MVP , leader of the Warriors, one of the already historic franchises in the American professional championship), his impact has been brutal , as if it were a wonderful discovery, according to the dimension of the player who made the triple the most deadly weapon in basketball and, therefore, redefined the offensive and defensive concepts of the game . There is a basketball before Curry and another after the point guard. Speaking in silver: we recognize this sport because it still has two baskets , but since the arrival of a historical shooter of Curry' s caliber (3,747 three-pointers, only in regular season), you have to defend at nine meters, the devotion for this shot has multiplied because mathematics does not deceive (three is more than two), the court has become longer, and therefore there are more spaces, we start talking about a four-point line and shots from the logo, the giants shoot from beyond the line (see Wemby ).... And, perhaps most importantly, new stars want to be like him, transcending gender and age. Just look at the phenomenon called Caitlin Clark . Paying attention to detail (see the photo of @canchanba), watching the four three-pointers with which Curry sent to sleep all of France, the last one defines the unrepeatable career of the Warriors player: competitiveness, determination, courage and talent, a lot of 'morro', with apologies. His success is insolent: 17 three-pointers between the semifinals and the final . After scoring three three-pointers in three consecutive plays , France paid more special attention to his defense. There was a two-on-one. Fournier and Batum pounced on him as he was arming his arm. On the other side of the court, with no defender on him, a Kevin Durant was calling for the ball. Two meters away was LeBron James , also unleashed. They may be, along with Kobe and Stephen himself, the two best players of the 21st century. Curry didn't even see them. What for? He threw in a bad position, possessed by his streak that is infinite, and he put it in, lighting up the final with his blessed madness. Don't say it was a dream . fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 11 Aug 2024 (Philippines News agency) Team USA won the Paris Olympics men's basketball gold Saturday, 98-87, against host France. Stephen Curry scored a team-high 24 points at Bercy Arena, while Lebron James posted a double-double with 14 points and 10 assists. Devin Booker and Kevin Durant added 15 points each. Despite Victor Wembanyama's 26 points and seven boards, to go along with Guerschon Yabusele's 20 points, the French national team could not avoid the loss. Team USA has now won five straight gold medals since 2008 and eight of the last nine Olympic tournaments, picking up their 17th overall gold. Since 1992, when the Olympics first allowed professional players to participate, the US has claimed gold in all the Games except in 2004, when it settled for bronze after losing to Argentina, which managed to capture gold and its first Olympic medal in basketball after defeating Italy 84-69. France brought home silver for a second straight Olympics as it also lost the Tokyo 2020 final to the US, 87-82. Serbia gets bronze Earlier, Serbia upset world champion Germany 93-83 to claim a third Olympic medal. After finishing second in Atlanta in 1996 and Rio de Janeiro in 2016, the Serbian squad took bronze behind a triple-double from three-time NBA MVP Nikola Jokic, who scored 19 points, 12 rebounds and 11 assists. Franz Wagner was the high scorer for Germany with 18 points and nine boards. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Team USA Basketball once again captured the gold medal with a 98-87 victory over France in the final of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. This triumph adds to the impressive collection of seventeen gold medals in the history of the U.S. team. After the victory, the statements of the protagonists were not long in coming, with LeBron James leading the way, who christened his squad with a very special name. During a conversation with the press, LeBron James was asked about the nicknames of previous historic teams such as the 'Dream Team' of Barcelona 1992 and the 'Redeem Team', which won the gold medal in Beijing 2008. And James shunned those aliases and decided to christen his ensemble with NBA stars like Stephen Curry , Kevin Durant and Joel Embiid, confidently stating, "This team is the Avengers." According to Bleacher Report, James had already been added the nickname "Captain LeMerica" during the Paris Games, reflecting the superhero theme. "It's not exactly clear which members of Team USA were given the roles of Thor, Iron Man or the Hulk, but fans can certainly make their own inferences," the outlet stressed. The team counted eight NBA MVP awards among its members, with the likes of Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant bringing their experience to the team at key moments. As for the impact of this star-studded team, the aforementioned media outlet also notes that even some of the top players struggled to get playing time. One such example is Jayson Tatum, who despite leading the Boston Celtics to the NBA championship in 2024, had multiple DNPs (Did Not Play) during the U.S. team's Olympic campaign. The team's success goes beyond the stars on the field. On the coaching staff, Steve Kerr, Tyronn Lue, Erik Spoelstra and Mark Few brought their knowledge and skills to lead the team to the top of the podium. This victory represents the fifth consecutive gold medal for U.S. men's basketball at the Olympics, a feat that secures its dominance in the sport internationally. James achieved something unprecedented: winning a gold medal in three different decades. LeBron was present in Beijing 2008 at age 23, in London 2012 at age 27, and now, at age 39, he has once again reached the top of the podium in Paris 2024. In the midst of celebrating this historic achievement, the former Miami Heat and Cleveland Cavaliers is already looking to focus on the Los Angeles Lakers, where he can fulfill his dream of playing alongside his son Bronny James. LeBron is about to enter his 22nd NBA season, a true testament to his longevity and talent. Team USA's success at Paris 2024 further cemented its status as a role model for future generations. The ability of the stars to put aside their egos and work together for a common goal highlights the professionalism and commitment of the team, something that didn't always happen. This resumption of their dominance had a trigger: what happened in Athens 2004, when Argentina humbled them and ended up winning the gold medal. B3 Length: 391 words Byline: KRISTIE RIEKEN Associated Press Highlight: PARIS - The U.S. collected gold in men's basketball and earned three more golds in a huge night at the track Saturday at the Paris Olympics. Stephen Curry scored 24 points to lead Team USA over France 98-87 at Bercy Arena to win its fifth straight gold medal and 17th overall. France got 26 points from star Victor Wembanyama, but it wasn't enough to earn the host nation its first gold medal in the sport. Body_172 Body_172 Body fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 PARIS - The U.S. collected gold in men's basketball and earned three more golds in a huge night at the track Saturday at the Paris Olympics. Stephen Curry scored 24 points to lead Team USA over France 98-87 at Bercy Arena to win its fifth straight gold medal and 17th overall. France got 26 points from star Victor Wembanyama, but it wasn't enough to earn the host nation its first gold medal in the sport. The U.S. needed a rally to beat Serbia 95-91 in a compelling semifinal. In the final, Curry hit four 3-pointers in the last three minutes. When he made the last one, which pushed the lead to 96-87 with 35 seconds left, he put his hands to the side of his face in a "go to sleep" gesture. "I think we might be the only team in the world whose fans are ashamed of them if they get a silver medal," U.S. coach Steve Kerr said. "That's the pressure that we face. But our players, and you saw Steph, they love the pressure. They appreciate this atmosphere and they were fantastic." In the final night of events at Stade de France, the U.S. won both 4x400 relays and Masai Russell took gold in a photo finish in the 100-meter hurdles. The U.S. won 34 track medals in the Paris Games for the country's most at a non-boycotted Olympics since the early 20th century, when there were more events and fewer countries. The 14 golds are the most in a non-boycotted Olympics since 1968. Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone and Gabby Thomas led the Americans to a 4.23-second win in the women's 4x400 relay. The gold medalists in the 400 hurdles and 200 meters took care of legs two and three for the United States, handing a 30-meter lead to Alexis Holmes, who didn't lose any ground. The U.S. finished in 3 minutes, 15.27 seconds, only .1 short of the world record. The American men won gold in the same race in a much closer finish about 15 minutes earlier. Rai Benjamin held off Letsile Tebogo on the anchor leg to give the men an Olympic-record time. Benjamin added this Olympic title to the one he claimed in the 400-meter hurdles a night earlier and prevented 200-meter champion Tebogo from giving Botswana another triumph over the Americans. It was Tebogo, the 21-year-old sprinting sensation, who stole the spotlight - and the gold - from the U.S. in the 200 on Thursday, relegating Kenny Bednarek to silver and Noah Lyles, who tested positive for COVID-19, to bronze. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 READ MORE: France FUMES over Steph Curry's Paris Olympics heroics READ MORE: Steph Curry puffs on cigar as he heads to Team USA afterparty READ MORE: USA women seal record EIGHTH straight Olympics basketball title Stephen Curry 's wife, Ayesha Curry, broke down in tears after Paris police after they stopped her from leaving the men's Olympic basketball final with her infant. Ayesha and her mother-in-law, Sonya, had a tense exchange with police on Saturday after Steph and Team USA won gold at the 2024 games. Video published online by Hollywood Fix showed Ayesha holding her four-month-old son baby Caius Chai and wiping away tears while Sonya begged officers on the street to allow the family access to their vehicle. Sonya is heard yelling that someone touched Caius, the couple's fourth child who was born prematurely in May. Steph's Golden State Warrior teammate, Draymond Green, is also heard saying that someone hit the boy's head. A man, who appeared to step in as translator, then explained to the group that police said the 'the president' was coming and 'nobody's allowed to cross the street right now until he's passed'. French President Emmanuel Macron had attended the match between the United States and host nation France, so it is possible that he could have been exiting the arena around the time of the encounter. A visible upset Ayesha, 35, is seen holding Caius in a chest-worn baby carrier with her six-year-old son Canon Jack, who was draped in an American flag, by her side. 'Look, they won't let us go back over there where we came from,' Sonya said in the video. 'They won't let the driver come here and they won't let us go back over there.' At one point a man begins translating for police and offers an apology: 'Sorry about the baby.' Steph, 36, was not seen in the footage, but his teammate certainly made his presence felt in the gold medalist's absence. 'So, even after him hitting the baby in the head, there's still nothing y'all can do to get them out of here?' Green asked, trying to intervene. At one point an irate Sonya can be heard yelling at others off camera: 'What? Stop saying my name. You should be helping us with the baby!' Later, a tearful Sonya simply tells officers: 'Let us get to our car.' Although Ayesha is clearly crying, she remained silent throughout the confrontation. The couple has not responded to the incident publicly. Steph and Ayesha announced the birth of Caius in late May. 'Our sweet baby boy decided to make an early arrival!!' the four-time NBA champion wrote on Instagram alongside a photo of the newborn's hand. 'He's doing great and we are finally settling in at home as a family of 6! So grateful!' It's unclear when Caius' original due date was scheduled, but the couple's first three children were all born in July - the height of the NBA offseason. Eldest daughter Riley Elizabeth was born in July of 2012, while daughter Ryan was born three years later, followed by their first son, Canon, in 2018. The son of former NBA guard Dell and brother of Charlotte Hornets reserve Seth, Stephen Curry is entering his 16th season, and already stands as the most prolific 3-point shooter in league history. In addition to his four NBA titles, he also boasts a NBA Finals MVP award, 10 All-Star nods, and two Most Valuable Player honors. Steph met Ayesha as a teenager at church in his native Charlotte, where his father was playing at the time. The two ultimately married in July of 2011. Since then, Dell and his long-time wife Sonya have divorced. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Massive audiences tuned in to watch Team USA capture gold on Saturday READ MORE: Team USA men's basketball WINS the Olympic gold medal NBC has announced massive viewing figures for the men's basketball and women's soccer gold medal games at the Olympics, with Team USA winning in both contests. In men's hoops, Stephen Curry and LeBron James led Team USA to a dramatic 98-87 victory victory over France. And NBC said that the game, which tipped off at 3:30ET, averaged 19.5 million viewers and peaked at an incredible 22.7 million. NBC said that the game was the most-watched basketball gold medal game since the 1996 Atlanta Olympics. The network added that - through its broadcasts on NBC, Peacock and other platforms - it achieved a 'Total Audience Delivery' of 30.7 million viewers for its Olympic action on Saturday, according to information from Nielsen and Adobe Analytics. A massive part of those figures was also the USWNT's appearance in the gold medal match. Emma Hayes' squad earned a 1-0 victory in the final vs. Brazil, which was contested at 11am ET. And despite that early kickoff, the game averaged a whopping 9 million viewers according to the network - the most for a gold medal women's soccer game since the Athens Olympics in 2004. The game was another tightly-contested affair for the USA women, who won each of their three knockout games in Paris by one goal. Mallory Swanson took the lead with a cool finish in the 57th minute after being played in by Korbin Albert and the Americans were able to hang on from there. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 HYPERLINK https://i0.wp.com/eltiempolatino.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/LeBron-y-Curry.jpg?fit=800%2C550&ssl=1 00 Link to Image The Paris 2024 Olympic Games gave us a unique sporting spectacle: the union of two NBA legends, LeBron James and Stephen Curry , under the same uniform. Their historic rivalry gave way to a collaboration that captivated the basketball world. However, are we witnessing the last dance of this dynamic duo? Golden duo dominates in France In the French capital, 39-year-old LeBron and 36-year-old Curry proved that age is just a number. Together, they led Team USA to yet another gold medal, the fifth in a row for the U.S. team. Their statistics were impressive: LeBron averaged 25.7 points, 8.3 assists and 7.3 rebounds per game, while Curry delighted the crowd with his accurate three-pointers. Their combination of strength, skill and leadership proved unstoppable. A friendship that grew out of a rivalry The rivalry that characterized the clashes between LeBron's Cavaliers and Curry's Warriors during the NBA Finals was left behind. In Paris, both players demonstrated great chemistry and mutual respect. " Basketball doesn't last forever. You don't want to lose the opportunity to have a good relationship with someone," LeBron said in an interview with The Athletic. This statement reflects the maturity and personal growth of both players. Together in the NBA? Despite the success in Paris, the possibility of seeing LeBron and Curry together on an NBA team seems increasingly remote. An anonymous league executive flatly ruled out this possibility, assuring reputable basketball analyst Sam Amico that "it will never happen." LeBron is tied to the Lakers for 2 more seasons and his goal is to retire alongside his son, Bronny. Curry, meanwhile, is the Warriors' cornerstone and is under contract through 2026. Last season, the Golden State Warriors tried to acquire LeBron James in a trade, but the Lakers showed no interest in parting with their top star, according to Marca. This news generated great expectation among fans, who dreamed of seeing Curry and LeBron facing each other in the playoffs. LeBron James and Stephen Curry , undisputed kings of the NBA LeBron and Curry's careers are replete with individual and collective achievements. Between them, they have won eight NBA championships, six regular season MVP awards and five Finals MVP awards. Their names are written in golden letters in the basketball history books. Although the possibility of seeing LeBron and Curry together on an NBA team seems distant, the sport always surprises us. The league's rules are constantly changing and the free agent market is unpredictable. For now, however, fans will have to settle for reliving the magical moments these two players gave us in Paris. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Cleveland Browns head coach Kevin Stefanski said Tuesday that Patrick Mahomes, a champion with the Kansas City Chiefs, will change the NFL like Stephen Curry has changed the NBA. "Just as Curry changed basketball , Mahomes is changing soccer, you notice it with the quarterbacks coming up in the league," Stefanski said. The Browns coach acknowledged the influence on NBA basketball of Stephen Curry , a four-time champion with the Golden State Warriors and a gold medalist at the last Olympic Games in Paris with Team USA. A similar influence, he detailed, to that forged by Mahomes, a three-time champion and Super Bowl MVP. "You see quarterbacks go out and play with that style, they act like him. So I wouldn't be surprised if we start seeing that more and more in our league and beyond. You'll see more and more guys in high school trying what he does," Stefanski added. Curry, 36, brought a different dimension to NBA offense with his highly effective three-point shooting, something he wowed NBA fans with his gold medal-winning performances at the recent Paris games. Mahomes, who will turn 29 on September 17, is already being compared to Tom Brady, an NFL legend who has seven championship rings in his career, the most for a player and one more than those won by his former team, the Patriots, and the Steelers. For the 2024 season, which will start next September, Mahomes has said he will seek what no one else in the NFL, including Brady, to win a third consecutive Super Bowl, something that, given the potential shown by Kansas City, would not be far-fetched. Kevin Stefanski's Cleveland Browns will face the Chiefs, led by Patrick Mahomes, on December 15 in Week 15 of the 2024 NFL season. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 HYPERLINK https://i0.wp.com/eltiempolatino.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Stephen-Curry-2-scaled.jpg?fit=1024%2C683&ssl=1 00 Link to Image Stephen Curry and the Golden State Warriors have been synonymous for more than a decade. However, a recent change in the star point guard's Instagram bio put fans on alert. Will this be the beginning of a new chapter in Curry's career? Farewell to the Warriors... for now? After winning the gold medal at the Paris Olympics with Team USA, Curry decided to update his Instagram bio. What used to read "Guard of the now simply reads "Olympic Gold Medalist." This modification generated speculation about his future in the NBA. Some analysts believe that this change could be a strategy to pressure the Warriors' management to improve the team, according to Marca. Others, however, see this as a sign that Curry, 36, may be considering other options to continue his career. The enigma of Stephen Curry 's future With 2 MVP awards under his arm and an all-time record in three-pointers scored, Curry has revolutionized basketball . His impact on the Golden State franchise is undeniable: he transformed a mediocre team into a dynasty. However, after the departure of Klay Thompson and a below-expectations 2024 season (46-36), the Warriors face an uncertain future. Sporting News suggested that, if Curry feels his legacy could be affected by this situation, he could look for a new team with greater championship aspirations. Rumors of a possible Curry trade to the Lakers are not new. With LeBron James and Anthony Davis on the team, the Lakers could be an attractive destination for the star point guard. However, for now, this remains just speculation. Stephen Curry 's future with the Warriors is uncertain. While some fans cling to the hope that the point guard will stay in Golden State, others are already imagining a future without him. What is certain is that this change in his Instagram bio has generated great expectation and has got everyone talking about the future of one of the best basketball players of all time. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Mexico City - Steph Curry , the reigning gold medalist at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games , has begun to spark rumors and speculation about his departure from the Golden State Warriors in the NBA. Curry was selected in the 2009 Draft by the Warriors, not knowing that in the following years he would be the most important and valuable man in the franchise. Steph Curry leaves the Warriors? The basketball player unleashed a barrage of questions and assumptions through social networks, because he has changed his biography of his official Instagram account , removing the mention he had about the Warriors. The 36-year-old's bio now reads "Believer. Father. Husband. Founder. Philanthropist. And... Olympic Gold Medalist. Philippians 4:13", and "Olympic gold medalist" replacing "Guardian of the @Warriors". This action caused many fans to start linking him to Lebron James' Lakers , assuming they would play together again, after what they achieved in Paris 2024. Although Steph's future is not known for sure, the reality is that the NBA superstar, signed a contract extension with the Warriors in 2021 that ties him to the franchise until 2026. Stephen Curry 's legacy with Golden State The Warriors prior to the 2009 arrival of Chef Curry, were a mid-level team that had gone decades without a championship. With the hiring of this American superstar, Golden State has had five NBA Finals appearances and four championships since 2015 , earning two Most Valuable Player awards along the way. He averaged 26.4 points, 5.1 assists and 4.5 rebounds last NBA season, once again being the team's focal point. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Chicago (USA), Aug 22 (EFE) - NBA Golden State Warriors star Stephen Curry appeared Thursday at the Democratic National Convention in Chicago (USA) to give his support to the party's candidate for the White House, Kamala Harris. "It's been an honor for me to represent our country, it's an honor to support Kamala, let's all do our part," said Curry, with the gold medal won at the Paris Olympics hanging around his neck, in a one-minute video that was projected on the screens of the United Center in Chicago. Curry, a four-time NBA winner, visited the White House last year as an invited guest as the 2022 league champion with the Warriors. "I had the opportunity to visit Kamala with my team last year at the White House. I can say one thing: I knew it then, and I definitely know it now, the Oval Office suits her very well," the basketball player said. "Kamala as president can bring that unity back and continue to move our country forward. This is about preserving hope and faith in our country. Ensuring that families can be taken care of in the most difficult of times," he assured. He further paraphrased former first lady Michelle Obama: "Do something!". "Let's get out to vote in November like never before," she added. Curry's coach in both the Warriors and the Olympic team, Steve Kerr, also participated this past Monday in person at the Democratic convention to give his support to the tandem formed by Harris and Tim Walz.
