fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Nike's Jordan brand said it is taking several of its top sponsored athletes on a tour of China in a push to promote its product lines in the crucial growth market. Dallas Mavericks guard Luka Doncic, New Orleans Pelicans forward Zion Williamson and Jayson Tatum from the Boston Celtics, who all have signature sneakers with the Jordan brand, will take part in the trip set for this autumn, along with the Orlando Magic's Paolo Banchero. The National Basketball Association stars will attend a series of events in Beijing and Shanghai in what the Jordan brand is calling a "significant cultural experience", a representative for the brand said. Management is leaning on Jordan to help revitalise its business in China, where the world's largest sportswear company has stumbled in recent years, hurt by an economic slowdown and rising consumer nationalism that has nudged shoppers toward domestic labels such as Li Ning and Anta Sports Products. Nike and its rivals have a history of bringing their top endorsers to China in order to connect with consumers. Michael Jordan himself visited in 2004 soon after his retirement, making appearances at the Great Wall and a school basketball championship game. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 SAN FRANCISCO, Calif. - Stephen Curry is not going anywhere after renewing his contract with the Golden State Warriors through the 2026-2027 NBA season for a deal worth nearly $63 million. After weeks of speculation about a possible transfer to the Los Angeles Lakers, Curry's agent, Jeff Austin, confirmed that the NBA's all-time three-point leader will remain with the San Francisco team. Rumors about a possible departure of Stephen Curry from the Warriors increased when the 36-year-old point guard removed from his biographies on social networks the reference to his membership in Golden State. View this post on Instagram A post shared by USA Basketball (@usabasketball) He's not going to Lakers Later, Curry was linked to the Lakers, where it was rumored that he could join LeBron James to revive the successful duo that led the U.S. team to the gold medal at the Paris 2024 Olympics. According to U.S. media, Stephen Curry will play his 18th NBA season with the team that drafted him in 2009. LeBron James made basketball history by becoming a cultural phenomenon and sports icon in the United States. Since his arrival in the NBA in 2003, his impressive talent on the court has allowed him to achieve unprecedented accomplishments. Today he shines beyond numbers and trophies, as his influence extends to finances. Indeed, like few stars, he has set a new standard in terms of contracts and business opportunities.

As the NBA evolves, players are tapping into an increasingly lucrative market and LeBron is positioned at the forefront of this trend. Thanks to multiple sponsorships and a formidable media presence. Moreover, his trajectory represents a shift in how athletes can capitalize on their success and fame. In this context, exploring the economic status of the league's superstars provides insight into the success of basketball for the next generations and earnings in the sports industry.

Which players are the highest paid in the NBA 2024/2025?

The Los Angeles Lakers small forward, the oldest player in the league at 40 years old, has once again stood out as one of the highest paid. His net worth is US$128.7 million and US$48.7 million correspond to his performance on the court with the Los Angeles Lakers. Undoubtedly, a legend in terms of trophies and money in the sports industry. However, other figures are also in the prestigious ranking.

  • LeBron James of Los Angeles Lakers: US$128.7 million (US$80 million - US$48.7 million on the court)
  • Stephen Curry of Golden State Warriors: US$105.8 million (US$50 million - US$55.8 million on the court)
  • Kevin Durant of Phoenix Suns: US$99.9 million (US$50 million- US$49.9 million on the court)
  • Giannis Antetokounmpo of Milwaukee Bucks: US$93.8 million (US$45 million - US$48.8 million on the court)
  • Milwaukee Bucks' Damian Lillard: US$64.8 million (US$16 million - US$48.8 million on court)

LeBron James earns more off the court

The four-time NBA champion and Olympic medalist is not the highest paid on the court, as three-pointer Stephen Curry receives a salary of US$55.8 million. Likewise, other players also appear on the list for their talent on the basketball court. Joel Embiid (US$51.4 million), Kevin Durant (US$49.9 million), Bradley Beal (US$50.2 million), Devin Booker (US$49.2 million), Giannis Antetokounmpo (US$48.8 million), Jimmy Butler (US$48.8 million) and Damian Lillard (US$48.8 million) are paid more on the basketball court alone.

However, the veteran Los Angeles Lakers forward adds an estimated $80 million from sponsorships, licensing, memorabilia and other commercial endeavors. The $128.7 million is more than the $124.5 million he earned last season.

LeBron James took a pay cut.

Unbelievable, but real. LeBron James made the decision to reduce his salary for the upcoming season. During the previous year, the renowned basketball player had an income of $49.51 million. This time, he opted to decrease it in order to collaborate with the Lakers on the team's salary cap and make his longing to share the court with his son, Bronny James, come true.

"It was important to LeBron, and yes, he's our highest-paid player, but he's still a bargain to me. It's absolutely unbelievable. Because we're a salary cap league, we can't pay him more than that. He brings recognition to the league, but also internationally. He's a great star and a great leader for this team," commented team owner Jeanie Bus on Petros & Money.

Artificial intelligence has conducted an exhaustive analysis of statistics and achievements in the NBA, revealing that the best player in history is not LeBron James or Stephen Curry , as many might assume. This study, which considers multiple factors, has put a basketball icon who has left an indelible mark on the U.S. league at the center of the discussion.

The debate over who is the best NBA player has been constant over the years, with names like Michael Jordan, Kobe Bryant and other greats of the sport on the list. However, artificial intelligence has brought a new perspective, using data that goes beyond titles and points scored, which has surprised many basketball fans.

Who is the best player in NBA history, according to AI?

The analysis performed by artificial intelligence is based on a combination of advanced statistics, playoff performance and the influence each player has had on his team and the league. This approach allows for a more objective assessment, moving away from the subjective opinions that often dominate basketball discussions. Thus, for the AI, the best basketball player in history is Michael Jordan over Lebron James and Stephen Curry .

Fans' reactions after the AI's revelation

The revelation has generated mixed reactions among fans. Some support the AI's decision, arguing that the numbers don't lie, while others defend their favorite players, citing iconic moments and the emotional connection they have made with them over the years. This debate promises to continue, as the basketball community adapts to the growing influence of technology in sports analytics.

Gemini revealed the name of the ultimate player and forgot about Curry

The popular Gemini platform was not far behind and revealed its answer. "The question of who is the greatest player in NBA history is one of the most debated in the basketball world. There is no definitive and objective answer, as every era and every fan has their own criteria and preferences." The AI tool decided to list and argued the qualities of the figures. In addition, it did not mention the Golden State Warriors three-pointer.

  • Michael Jordan: Known for his incredible offensive talent, competitiveness and ability to lead teams to victory, Jordan is considered by many to be the standard of excellence in basketball .
  • LeBron James: An extremely versatile and athletic player, LeBron has shown longevity and an ability to adapt to the game that puts him in a class of his own. His impact on the game both offensively and defensively is undeniable.
  • Kareem Abdul-Jabbar: The all-time leading scorer in NBA history, Kareem was a dominant player for decades thanks to his famous hook shot and commanding presence in the paint.

New York, Nov 17 (EFE) - James Harden surpassed Ray Allen on Sunday to become the second-leading three-point shooter in NBA history.

The now Los Angeles Clippers player came into the matchup against the Utah Jazz tied with Allen with 2,973 three-pointers made during his career.

He needed just one more three-pointer to reach the second spot in that ranking and Harden made it with 6.10 on the clock in the first quarter.

NBA MVP in 2018, Harden is now only behind the mostly considered the best shooter in basketball history: Stephen Curry (Golden State Warriors), the standout leader on the NBA's all-time list of top three-point shooters with 3,782.

An example of how basketball and the NBA have changed in recent years and the great importance that the three-pointer has acquired can be seen in the number of games it has taken each of these three players to reach their historic records from the perimeter.

Thus, Curry has made 3,782 three-pointers in 965 regular season games, while Harden surpassed 2,973 three-point shots in 1,086 games compared to the 1,300 that Allen needed to reach that figure.

The Golden State Warriors, leaders of the Pacific Division of the Western Conference, are the favorites against the tough Pelicans. The team led by Stephen Curry wants to keep its hopes alive for the NBA Cup 2024. The second edition of the tournament remains in the eyes of all basketball fans in the United States.

Golden and Pelicans live opposite moments, since the San Francisco team is recovering its best version that allowed them to sweep the NBA a few years ago in its golden age. On the other hand, Pelicans are still very irregular on the basketball court. In any case, Steve Kerr's team will have to prove its credentials as champion and favorite in New Orleans.

How do the Golden State Warriors and Pelicans fare?

The Warriors come into Friday's game after yesterday's 120-97 win over the Atlanta Hawks, a favorable result that keeps them in first place in the Pacific Division of the Western Conference with a record of eleven games won and three games lost. In addition, they are on a one-game winning streak this season. The only opponent that challenged them was Dallas Maverick, as they had to come from behind and Curry scored a three-pointer at the buzzer.

For their part, the Pelicans are coming off a 128-100 loss to the Cleveland Cavaliers, a defeat that leaves them in last place in the Southwest division of the Western Conference with a record of four games won and twelve games lost, with a three-game losing streak so far.

Where and at what time to watch Friday's game?

The game between Golden State Warriors and New Orleans Pelicans will be available on TV and streaming. Here is the schedule confirmed by NBA Cup.

  • Stadium: Smoothie King Center, New Orleans
  • Day: Friday, November 22
  • Time: 18:30 CDMX | 19:30 ET USA | 21:30 ARG | 1:30 ESP
  • TV Channel: ESPN (USA, Latam)
  • Streaming: NBA League Pass and Disney + (Latam)

Golden State Warriors and Pelicans lineups by NBA

  • Golden State Warriors: Stephen Curry , Lindy Waters III, Andrew Wiggins, Draymond Green, Trayce Jackson-Davis.
  • New Orleans Pelicans: CJ McCollum, Brandon Boston Jr, Brandon Ingram, Trey Murphy III and Javonte Green.

How many titles do the Golden State Warriors have in the NBA?

The Golden State Warriors have 7 NBA titles. These championships were achieved in the following seasons:

  • 1947 (as Philadelphia Warriors, before moving to California).
  • 1956 (as Philadelphia Warriors).
  • 1975.
  • 2015.
  • 2017.
  • 2018.
  • 2022.

The team has been one of the most successful in recent NBA history, especially in the Stephen Curry and Klay Thompson era, as both were key to the team's renaissance and even took home championships in 2015, 2017, 2018 and 2022 in LeBron James' heyday.

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 The Golden State Warriors recent struggles have left fans wondering: has the team been exposed, or are they simply falling into a bad pattern? After an explosive first half, they ve repeatedly faltered in the third quarter, allowing opponents to mount comebacks. Despite holding an 18-point lead against an under .500 team, the Warriors suffered a loss showing cracks in their game. In this slump, Stephen Curry has laid out three key strategies to help the Warriors turn things around.Three key areas to turn the season aroundHeading into the season, the Warriors didn t seem like title contenders on paper. However, Steve Kerr s new approach, which features a 12-man rotation, has led to improvements in assists, rebounds, and points in the paint. This deep roster, with a potent second unit, can compete with anyone. But this very depth has started to backfire. After dominating early in games, the Warriors have been choking late, with the second unit notably faltering in the third quarter. This recurring issue culminated in their first back-to-back losses.In response, Curry pinpointed three areas the team needs to improve on to stop their slide. Two of them go hand-in-hand:Be More IntentionalUnder Kerr s new system, the Warriors have multiple key players who can step up each night. Curry emphasized the need for focus and intentionality in their offensive game. He said, No matter who s out there on the floor, we re still playing 12 guys, and you have to adjust. Offensively, moving the ball and being involved as a screener, passer, or dribbler we can be more intentional about creating better shots with certain lineups. & 3. Build Communication and TrustWith a new mix of players, the Warriors need to improve their trust and communication, especially on defense. While they rely on basketball IQ and ball movement, Curry believes they must better coordinate as a unit. He pointed to the Monday night game against the Nets, saying, We need to work on our communication and trust in how a five-man defense is supposed to run. Despite improvements in their defensive play, Curry knows it s still not enough to compete at the highest level.These golden rules from Curry could be the key to rescuing the Warriors from their slump and getting them back on track for the rest of the season.Also Read: HYPERLINK https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/nba/top-stories/ja-morant-and-larsa-pippen-back-scotty-pippen-jr-s-message-on-silence-and-success-following-career-best-performance/articleshow/4.cms 00 Ja Morant and Larsa Pippen back Scotty Pippen Jr.'s message on 'silence' and 'success' following career-best performance Stephen Curry vows to strengthen team communication"Where there s a will, there s a way." This old saying may ring true for the Warriors, as two back-to-back losses might have sparked a newfound sense of unity among the team. A single practice session, it seems, was all it took to unlock that potential.Speaking about the importance of communication after the tough losses, Steph Curry emphasized, Any practice window you have, any session, it usually builds the connection you need, especially after a couple of losses because we all need to be in this together. With two practice sessions scheduled this week, the Warriors now have the opportunity to reflect on their mistakes and come back stronger, with purpose and intent. Lonzo Ball, the Chicago Bulls point guard who has returned to play in the NBA after more than 1,000 days out following multiple surgeries on his left knee, has joined the debate over whether or not Kobe Bryant was better than Michael Jordan.

"I love Kobe Bryant, but he was never the best player to me," Lonzo Ball said on 'The WAE Show'.

Does Kobe Bryant really hold the negative record for most missed shots in NBA history?

The Bulls point guard acknowledges that his decision is influenced by "the record for most missed shots (14,481), you know what I mean?" by Kobe Bryant, who had an outstanding 44.7% shooting percentage from the field to score 33,643 points in his career.

What Lonzo Ball forgets about is that LeBron James recently surpassed, with 14,628 missed shots, Kobe Bryant's negative record for most missed shots in NBA history.

In 2017 Lonzo Balla already stated that "I'll take LeBron James over Kobe Bryant".

Lonzo Ball: " Stephen Curry is the best shooter of all time".

Lonzo Ball wanted to highlight the influence of Stephen Curry whom he considers "the best shooter of all time" and who he believes changed the game of basketball : "There is nothing he hasn't done and he also has the NBA championship rings to back it up".

fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Golden State Warriors superstar Stephen Curry has had a long-term contract with Under Armor. However, the NBA star recently signed comedian Desi Banks' Nike Air Force sneakers. This particular incident took place after the Warriors' 114-106 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves.Does this new contract mean that Stephen Curry is going to break ties with Under Armour? Let us have a look.How will Stephen Curry 's signature on Desi Banks' Nike sneaker affect his existing contract with Under Armour?Curry hesitated before signing the sneakers and since he already has a ten-year relationship with Under Armour, he made it obvious that this was a one-time occasion. "Let it be known I am all here for life, this is the only one I am ever going to do, ever!" he said, pointing to the Under Armour logo before signing. @TheWarriorsTalk posted the video of this event on X with the hashtag #BlackAirforceBehavior.The four-time NBA champion has been the face of Under Armour basketball since 2013. As a result, it was unlikely he would sign something made by Nike. But does this shake the foundations of his relationship with the Baltimore-based company?Curry's dedication to Under Armour dates back all the way to an event in 2013. He was signed to Nike at the time, but since his contract was about to expire, he was considering offers from other sneaker companies. Nike reportedly made a number of mistakes during the negotiating process. Mispronouncing his name as "Stephon" in a meeting was one of the most noticeable mistakes. Nike is also accused of reusing a PowerPoint presentation that incorrectly showed Kevin Durant's name rather than Curry's.Curry's choice to sign with Under Armour, which gave him a more lucrative $4 million contract and a signature shoe line, is said to have been influenced by these mistakes. Nike, on the other hand, only offered $2.5 million a year. Curry swiftly established himself as the face of Under Armour's basketball business, demonstrating that this move was a brilliant one.Curry has since been instrumental in establishing the basketball brand for Under Armour. His signature sneakers have always been well-liked, and in August 2024, the "Curry 12" was added to his already renowned sneaker collection. November 2024 saw the release of two new colors, "Wardell Mode" and "Shooting Star," which retailed for $140 on the Under Armour website.Also read: HYPERLINK https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/nba/top-stories/top-30-most-valuable-nba-teams-of-2024/articleshow/5.cms 00 Top 30 most valuable NBA teams of 2024 Curry's dedication to Under Armour is highlighted by his recent encounter with Desi Banks. On the surface, signing a Nike sneaker might not seem like much, but for a star like Curry, it shows how much he values his brand partners. Curry made it plain when he inked Desi Banks' Nike Air Forces: this would be a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 SAN FRANCISCO, Calif. - On Sunday night, the duel between the Dallas Mavericks and the Golden State Warriors not only went down in history for the 143-133 away win, but also for a spectacular record: 48 three-pointers made between the two teams. This mark is just another chapter in the history that Stephen Curry began to write more than a decade ago, when he changed the sport of basketball forever. View this post on Instagram A post shared by NBA (@nba) Curry's impact on the modern NBA Stephen Curry is not only the all-time leader in three-pointers made (3,832), but he is also responsible for the transformation of contemporary basketball . His ability to score from unthinkable distances has popularized a style of play in which the three-point shot increasingly dominates. Teams all over the league have adopted this philosophy, which has even led to consideration of the introduction of a four-point line, given the frequency and effectiveness of shots from behind half-court. You might be interested in: When are the NBA Cup semifinals and where to watch them? A historic night in San Francisco The showdown at the Chase Center was a three-point shooting recital. The Warriors scored 27 threes, setting an all-time record for a team to lose a game, while the Mavericks contributed 21. The 50.5% combined efficiency (48 hits on 95 attempts) highlighted the unprecedented efficiency that characterizes modern basketball . View this post on Instagram A post shared by Golden State Warriors (@warriors) Three-pointers protagonists: Dallas Mavericks (21 three-pointers): Luka Don?i?: 6 of 11 Klay Thompson: 7 of 11 Quentin Grimes: 4 of 8 Others: 4 three-pointers combined Golden State Warriors (27 three-pointers): Stephen Curry : 7 of 13 Andrew Wiggins: 5 of 9 Draymond Green: 5 of 9 Others: 10 three-pointers combined With 45 points, 11 rebounds and 13 assists, Slovenian point guard Luka Don?i? was the driving force for the Mavericks, while teammate Klay Thompson also shined with 28 points and 7-of-11 from long distance. View this post on Instagram A post shared by Dallas Mavericks (@dallasmavs) I felt like no one could miss anything today. They had 18 three-pointers at halftime and we were still ahead. It was a fun game. Luka Don?i? The three-point era and the future of the game Today's style of play has transformed attacks away from the paint to favor outside shooting. The 2024-25 season already shows an average of 37.5 three-point attempts per team, a marked increase over previous campaigns. "A decade ago, if you saw a stat sheet on offense like that for a team, it was winning by 20 or maybe 30 points." Steve Kerr Even figures like Klay Thompson have suggested that the four-point line could be the game's next evolutionary step. View this post on Instagram A post shared by Dallas Mavericks (@dallasmavs). I saw what happened in Chicago against Charlotte, and a lot of fans said that thing about shooting too many threes. It's nice to be able to counter that narrative and show that shooting is at an incredible level in this league. The game will continue to move forward and eventually we'll see, probably, a four-point line." Klay Thompson Stephen Curry : the pioneer who shaped the future Curry, who contributed to the feast with 7 three-pointers and a double-double (26 points, 5 rebounds and 10 assists), remains the benchmark. Thanks to his influence, teams and players have evolved towards a more dynamic and spectacular style of play. Golden State, which has won four rings in the last decade, is the clearest example of how outside shooting can redefine success. Shooting threes and shooting in general is a skill that everyone can work on and improve. We can't all jump three feet up or hit a six-inch jumper. If you can shoot the basket - no matter what level, what league you play in or how tall you are - you can play basketball ." Stephen Curry Meanwhile, the NBA continues to adapt. With nights like Sunday's, it's clear that basketball is constantly evolving, and Stephen Curry is the chief architect of this revolution. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 NBA ratings have been falling this season in the United States and Shaquille O'Neal blames the teams' lack of creativity. According to the former point guard, the game has become less varied, the show is repetitive and he gave as an example the excess of three-pointers. "I have a theory: attendance is down because we're always watching the same thing. Nowadays all the teams shoot triples. Everyone is making the same plays in the damn zone behind the free throw line, with the dribbling and the step back. The ratings are going to keep going down unless we change things like they used to," Shaquille O'Neal said in a podcast. Just last Sunday, in the Golden State-Dallas game, 48 triples were scored by both teams, something never before recorded in the NBA. Only Stephen Curry , the greatest triple-double player in the history of the league, hit 7! Half-seriously, half-jokingly, Shaquille O'Neal considers the Warriors' point guard to be one of those responsible for the ratings situation. "Curry ruined basketball !" League commissioner Adam Silver disagrees. "This year we had to compete in a World Series (of baseball) with two very attractive teams (Dodgers and Yankees). They got great ratings," he explained, also blaming the drop in ratings on other factors, such as the US presidential elections. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Saquille O'Neal, one of the best centers in the history of basketball , winner of four NBA rings with the Los Angeles Lakers and Miami Heat, pointed to Stephen Curry and his great success in outside shooting, beyond the three-point line, as one of the evils of basketball today and especially in the North American league. "I have a theory. The ratings have gone down because we're seeing the same thing all the time. Today every team is shooting three-pointers. They're all running the same plays in the damn zone behind the free throw, with the dribble and the step-back. And the ratings are going to continue to go down unless we change things back to the way they used to be done," he said. The 6-foot-6 New Jersey giant traces the start of this three-point drift to the dynasty that the Golden State Warriors established in 2015, with four titles in seven years. The San Francisco franchise dominated the NBA with an offensive and very showy game that relied on its success from the perimeter, especially from Klay Thompson and especially Stephen Curry . " Stephen Curry and those guys ruined everything. I don't mind Golden State in the past shooting three-pointers, but not every team is a three-point shooter. So why does everybody have the same strategy? It's boring," O'Neal analyzed basketball , so far "a perfect game since Naismith created it." O'Neal adds that "this new era of humans screwed up" and that not "everybody wants to be Steph Curry, but not everybody is Steph Curry." "If the audience goes down so does the money," Shaq warns of an NBA where the dominating center in the area, with the same profile, is no longer in style, now virtually all big players farther away from the hoop and with the ability to shoot from afar. Regarding the drop in viewership, the NBA commissioner, Adam Silver, pointed in another direction by pointing out that this year's poor TV ratings are due to the strong competition of the baseball World Series between the Dodgers and the Yankees, "two very attractive teams". fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Shaquille O'Neal has once again demonstrated that he does not mince words when it comes to giving his opinion. Considered by many as one of the most dominant players in the history of the NBA, he has once again caused a stir in the United States by harshly criticizing the current style of play that characterizes the most important basketball league in the world. For Shaquille O'Neal, the fact that we see more and more shots from the three-point line in every game has resulted in a decline in NBA ratings in recent times. The legendary American center has unleashed his thoughts in a podcast that he hosts weekly with Adam Lefkoe. "The Big Podcast with Shaq" is the title of this fun entertainment program in which the former NBA star and his new partner interview a new guest every week to comment together on some of the most important news in the NBA. His extensive and successful career as a professional basketball player allows Shaq to have hidden in his hat a story or two that no one has told so far. It has been precisely in this podcast where Shaquille O'Neal has decided to expose what is his opinion regarding the fact that the audiences in NBA games are declining more and more as the weeks go by. The historic center of the Lakers has not hesitated to throw a dart at Stephen Curry , who in his opinion represents exactly what he criticizes so much of the game that is currently developing in the American basketball league. The former Lakers star has once again made it clear that, in his opinion, the fact that more and more points are being scored in basketball games does not make them more interesting. For Shaq, the general increase of all teams in trying to score more three-pointers has been one of the main reasons that have caused the considerable drop in audiences that the NBA is currently suffering. This is what he confessed in one of his last interventions in the aforementioned podcast: "The audience is down because we are always seeing the same thing. All the teams in the league are making the same plays," said the former player. "Nowadays all the teams are shooting three-pointers," Shaq immediately protested. For the legendary center, "everyone is running the same plays in the damn zone behind the free throw, dribbling and step-back". A move "made in" Stephen Curry , who is undoubtedly one of the players who best performs this type of deception when preparing to shoot a three-point shot. The Warriors star will go down in NBA history after becoming the best three-point shooter in the competition. His ability to score a three-point shot from long distances set a precedent in the competition, and now every team wants to have a specialist like him in their ranks. Something that doesn't seem to sit well with Shaq: "I don't mind Golden State shooting three-pointers at the time, but now every team has a three-point shooter, so.... Why does everyone have the same strategy?" the former player wondered. Shaquille O'Neal has not hesitated to charge against Stephen Curry , and has stated that in his opinion the solution to regain the expected levels of audience would be to return to resume the style of play that characterized the NBA when the legendary center was still one of the best active basketball players. "And the ratings are going to continue to go down unless we change things back to the way they used to be," Shaq said. The one-time Lakers center also used his explanation to make a half-joking statement about the Warriors star. "He's ruined basketball ," Shaq said, referring to the fact that Stephen Curry 's play has set a precedent in the competition. In the first few weeks of this NBA season, the league's average shooting rate stood at 106 three-pointers made out of 296 attempts. This means that players are barely scoring one three-pointer out of every three attempts they make. They are shooting from further and further away, which has led to a decrease in their effectiveness when it comes to scoring. A fact that could have directly affected audience levels. The numbers don't lie, and if there is something that no one can deny Shaquille O'Neal, it is precisely the fact that fewer and fewer people follow NBA games. This year's opening day had almost half the audience of last season, as reported by ESPN; and . fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Dallas Mavericks guard Luka Doncic is starting his own foundation, saying his primary goal is to ensure that children learning how to play basketball are doing so the right way and within a positive environment. The Luka Doncic Foundation will be formally launched Saturday, which is World Basketball Day. Doncic plans to bring 750 children from the Dallas area to a Mavericks game that night against the Los Angeles Clippers. His foundation has already commissioned a study on youth basketball in the U.S. and the Balkan region of Europe. The study's findings, which will be publicly released Saturday, include that "joy, creativity and exploration are being squeezed out of the game," the foundation said. Doncic's foundation is getting guidance from a Youth Sports Leadership Council, a group that includes his agent Bill Duffy and Golden State Warriors star Stephen Curry . Cavaliers lose Okoro The Cleveland Cavaliers will be without forward Isaac Okoro for several weeks after he suffered a sprained right shoulder. The Cavaliers, who have the NBA's best record at 23-4, said Thursday that Okoro sustained a sprain to his right AC joint during the third quarter of Cleveland's Dec. 16 win at Brooklyn. He will be re-evaluated in two weeks and the team said it will update his status and return "as appropriate." Okoro, the No. 5 pick in the 2020 draft, is one of the team's best defensive players. Heis averaging 6.6 points, 2.3 rebounds and 21 minutes in 23 games. However, fans remain optimistic, given his recent pattern of being upgraded to "available" just before the game goes live. Barring any last-minute setbacks, the 20-time All-Star is expected to be in the starting five tonight against the Golden State Warriors. This matchup not only brings two franchises that are worshipped by basketball fans against each other but also brings together two of the NBA's biggest stars, LeBron James and Stephen Curry , in their fourth Christmas Day faceoff.Despite the injury, LeBron James has been a consistent presence on the court, playing in the Lakers' last four games after a brief two-game absence. In his most recent outing, he delivered a triple-double performance with 28 points, 11 rebounds, and 11 assists in a narrow 117-114 loss to the Detroit Pistons. Any time you get an opportunity to be on the court and compete vs. one of the greatest to ever play this game, you don t take it for granted, LeBron shared after the game against Detroit. Reflecting on his upcoming matchup with Stephen Curry , he added, I don t know how many more opportunities we re going to get to go against each other so it s always fun. The Lakers will look to capitalize on LeBron s presence on the court if he plays, especially considering their current challenges in the Western Conference standings. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Dallas - The Minnesota Timberwolves defeated the Dallas Mavericks 99-105 on Wednesday, who lost Luka Don?i? to injury and nearly came back from 28 points down in the fourth quarter. Don?i? left the court in pain with 2:31 left before halftime with an apparent left calf strain . Dallas (19-11) was already down 38-51 at that point . For the Timberwolves (15-14), Anthony Edwards scored 26 points and grabbed 8 rebounds . Julius Randle , born in Dallas and with his mom in the stands, scored 23 points, collected 10 rebounds and dished out 8 assists . Frenchman Rudy Gobert also finished with a double-double with 14 points and 10 rebounds . The Timberwolves were up +28 (62-90) with 1:23 left in the third quarter, a difference that seemed insurmountable for the Mavericks without Don?i? But led by Kyrie Irving and with a 37-11 run, the Mavericks were only 2 down (99-101) with 1:05 to go. With morale high, Dereck Lively II stole the ball from Edwards on the next play , which ended up in the hands of Irving with the opportunity to tie or take the lead. But the Aussie's three-pointer wouldn't go in and Edwards made a basket to make it 99-103 with 19 seconds left. Klay Thompson also missed the three-pointer on the next possession, forcing Dallas to foul Randle, who made both of his free throws for 99-105. Irving scored 39 points in the loss for Dallas. fLayoutInCell 1 lineColor lineWidth 25400 lineStyle 0 fFlipH 0 fFlipV 0 shapeType 20 Watching LeBron James and Stephen Curry during a face-off is one of the biggest dreams of an NBA fan. This dream came true during one of the much-hyped games on Christmas Day 2024. However, the viewership of the NBA matches was comparatively lower with respect to the NFL s debut on Netflix. After the entire team of the Golden State Warriors was decimated by the Los Angeles Lakers, the NBA and NFL fans clashed on social media after Kevin Durant, LeBron James, and Stephen Curry s ABC poster became viral on the internet. NBA And NFL Fans Clash Over LeBron James, Steph Curry and Kevin Durant s Viral Poster By ABC s Special Feature ABC came up with a special feature based on one of the most prominent NBA stars of the current era. LeBron James, Kevin Durant, and Stephen Curry appeared in the segment named as NBA OG s Convo.Fans took to social media and reacted to the picture shared by the official page of the NBA on X. A netizen mentioned that Basketball is limited to America and it is labeled as a World champion. The tweet read asAn X user even stated- I like both sports but I ain't trying to watch an NBA game where both teams shoot 50 3's each every game...I will gladly watch football over basketball . An NBA even pointed toward the revenue generation that comes from the league. The post mentioned- The NBA generates more money than soccer who cares about Instagram likes. A neutral netizen even mentioned that both the NFL and NBA should be enjoyed, which read as Let people enjoy both men Social Media gives everyone a bad rep. NBA legends LeBron James, Stephen Curry , and Kevin Durant came together to represent the USA during the 2024 Paris Olympics in the summer. The trio even secured gold for the USA. NBA Head Adam Silver and Lakers Star LeBron On One Of The Most Anticipated Games of 2024During one of the biggest nights of the NBA, LeBron James and the reputed league head Adam Silver spoke about the face-off between the Warriors and the Lakers. LeBron James revealed that it was all about the chance to play against one another as he would be entering the 40s club in a few days. HYPERLINK https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/dec/25/nba-christmas-2025-games-preview 00 He said- Any time you get an opportunity to be on the court and compete against one of the greatest to ever play this game, you don t take it for granted. I don t know how many more opportunities we ll get to go against each other. NBA president Adam Silver also spoke about the significance of the game organized on Christmas Day. He said- I wish there were more of our great players who had a chance to play on that stage on Christmas Day. It s been an interesting turnaround from certainly my earlier days in the league when we got a lot of complaints about playing on Christmas and now it comes the other way, which is, Why aren t we playing on Christmas? I wish we could accommodate even more teams. The NBA matches on Christmas have always been one of the most anticipated ones every year. To witness your favorite athletes compete against one another within the warm and cozy confines of our homes with our loved ones simply elevates the Holiday season s spirit.
